*They must demonstrate that their token logic does not convert narrative into value, governance into entitlement, or speculation into ecological performance.*

*Potential access statuses may include:*

| ***Status***                    | ***Meaning***                                                                               |
|---------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Observer status***           | *May learn, participate in non-sensitive forums, or explore compatibility.*                 |
| ***Research / sandbox status*** | *May test interoperability under restricted conditions.*                                    |
| ***Limited participation***     | *May access specific functions under defined boundaries.*                                   |
| ***Governance participation***  | *May participate in governance only within approved scope and legal boundaries.*            |
| ***Rejected / incompatible***   | *Not eligible due to tokenomic, legal, governance, ecological, or constitutional conflict.* |

*Any observation period should be risk-based and governance-defined.*

*A fixed six-month minimum may be used as a policy option, but it should not be presented as a universal constitutional requirement unless formally adopted.*

*Where a token ecosystem has its own governance, treasury, token logic, role structure, and community identity, Prometheus should interact through sovereign alignment and interoperability, not absorption.*

***7.8 Digital Agents, AI Systems, Infrastructure Providers, and Technical Operators***

*Digital agents, AI systems, infrastructure providers, model operators, connector providers, data-service providers, and technical maintainers may participate only through strict role boundaries.*

*Eligibility review may include:*

*• operator identity;  
• technical function;  
• access scope;  
• data permissions;  
• model or system description where relevant;  
• security posture;  
• auditability;  
• logging requirements;  
• incident-response procedure;  
• model-risk review where applicable;  
• compliance with non-bypassability constraints;  
• prohibition on autonomous execution without authorization.*

*No AI system, autonomous agent, dashboard, connector, API, or technical provider may independently authorize:*

*• PRU value;  
• RAP inclusion;  
• investor-facing claims;  
• token issuance;  
• ecological interventions;  
• legal rights;  
• governance decisions;  
• capital deployment.*

*Technical systems may assist.*

*They may observe, route, calculate, compare, flag, recommend, and explain.*

*They may not become constitutional authority.*

*Prometheus uses intelligence.*

*It does not surrender sovereignty to intelligence.*

***7.9 Auditors, Verifiers, Researchers, and Methodology Contributors***

*Auditors, verifiers, researchers, universities, laboratories, and methodology contributors may participate when their role strengthens evidence quality, verification discipline, scientific integrity, or methodology development.*

*Eligibility review may include:*

*• professional identity or institutional affiliation;  
• relevant expertise;  
• conflict-of-interest disclosure;  
• methodology transparency;  
• data-access boundaries;  
• confidentiality obligations;  
• publication or reporting rules;  
• independence requirements where relevant;  
• evidence-handling procedures;  
• agreement not to overstate findings beyond data quality and methodology.*

*Researchers may explore frontier hypotheses.*

*Auditors and verifiers must preserve institutional credibility.*

*A research hypothesis is not PRU value.*

*A laboratory result is not automatically admissible value.*

*A model output is not verification unless methodology and review conditions are satisfied.*

*Scientific curiosity is welcome.*

*Financial admissibility requires evidence discipline.*

***7.10 Religious, Spiritual, Cultural, and Ancestral Communities***

*Prometheus may collaborate with religious, spiritual, cultural, and ancestral communities when such collaboration supports dignity, stewardship, ecological care, education, community resilience, food systems, land restoration, intergenerational continuity, or human coherence.*

*Eligibility review may include:*

*• identification of the community or institution;  
• representative authority where relevant;  
• scope of collaboration;  
• internal governance or leadership structure;  
• safeguarding and consent policies where needed;  
• respect for voluntary participation and freedom of conscience;  
• clear distinction between spiritual meaning and Prometheus evidence;  
• clear distinction between ritual, testimony, observation, and verified indicators;  
• commitment not to use Prometheus participation for coercion, exclusion, manipulation, or financial overclaiming.*

*Prometheus recognizes that communities may speak different languages of meaning.*

*Some may speak of Creation.*

*Some may speak of Nature.*

*Some may speak of Earth.*

*Some may speak of God, Spirit, ancestry, science, ecology, or interdependence.*

*Prometheus does not impose one sacred language.*

*It protects the living foundation behind many languages.*

*A spiritual or cultural community may contribute meaning, care, ritual, memory, stewardship, and human coherence.*

*However, spiritual or cultural legitimacy does not automatically create MRV evidence, PRU value, governance authority, investor rights, or legal entitlement.*

*This boundary protects both the community and Prometheus.*

*Meaning is honored.*

*Evidence is verified.*

***7.11 General Eligibility Outcomes***

*After review, an entity may receive one of several eligibility outcomes.*

| ***Eligibility outcome***      | ***Meaning***                                                                                                   |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Eligible***                 | *Participant may enter within the approved scope.*                                                              |
| ***Eligible with conditions*** | *Participant may enter subject to defined limitations, disclosures, review, or controls.*                       |
| ***Limited / sandbox access*** | *Participant may test compatibility without full participation rights.*                                         |
| ***Observer status***          | *Participant may learn or engage without governance, capital, or operational authority.*                        |
| ***Review required***          | *Additional evidence, legal review, governance review, or documentation is required.*                           |
| ***Restricted***               | *Participant may access only narrow functions due to risk profile.*                                             |
| ***Suspended***                | *Existing participation is paused pending review or remediation.*                                               |
| ***Rejected***                 | *Participant is not eligible under current facts or constitutional constraints.*                                |
| ***Quarantined***              | *Participant or pathway presents serious capture, legal, ecological, financial, tokenomic, or governance risk.* |

*Eligibility is not a permanent badge.*

*It is a governed status.*

*It may evolve as evidence, conduct, legal status, governance maturity, risk profile, Covenant compliance, community-field health, or technical implementation changes.*

***7.12 Eligibility Review Matrix***

*Prometheus may use an eligibility review matrix to make onboarding more transparent.*

| ***Review dimension***             | ***Key question***                                                                                |
|------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Identity / authority***         | *Who is entering, and who has authority to bind the entity?*                                      |
| ***Purpose alignment***            | *Why does the participant want to enter Prometheus?*                                              |
| ***Constitutional compatibility*** | *Does participation align with foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, and the Access Covenant?* |
| ***Role clarity***                 | *What function will the participant perform?*                                                     |
| ***Evidence responsibility***      | *Will the participant generate, handle, verify, or rely on evidence?*                             |
| ***Governance scope***             | *What decisions, if any, can the participant influence?*                                          |
| ***Financial boundary***           | *Does participation create or imply financial rights?*                                            |
| ***Token boundary***               | *Is any token involved, and what does it represent or not represent?*                             |
| ***Legal boundary***               | *Does the relationship require legal documentation or jurisdictional review?*                     |
| ***Risk classification***          | *What risks does this participant introduce or reduce?*                                           |
| ***Review cycle***                 | *When should eligibility be reviewed again?*                                                      |

*This matrix helps Prometheus remain open without becoming vague.*

*It gives the onboarding process a spine.*

***7.13 Final Eligibility Principle***

*Eligibility exists to protect openness from capture.*

*Prometheus should be open to persons, communities, institutions, capital, technology, research, governance innovation, cultural wisdom, spiritual traditions, and future human coordination forms.*

*But openness without constitutional discipline becomes vulnerability.*

*The eligibility layer therefore asks one question before all others:*

*can this participant enter without weakening the living foundation, evidence discipline, governance integrity, legal clarity, human dignity, or regenerative fidelity of Prometheus?*

*If yes, access may be granted within the correct scope.*

*If not, access must be conditioned, limited, deferred, or refused.*

*Participation is welcome.*

*Contribution is welcome.*

*Difference is welcome.*

*Plurality is welcome.*

*Capture is not.*

***8. Procedures for Gaining Access***

*Access to Prometheus is not granted through assumption, proximity, enthusiasm, capital, technical capacity, community status, token exposure, or institutional prestige.*

*Access is granted through a reviewable procedure.*

*The purpose of the access procedure is to translate the Access Covenant and the eligibility criteria into a clear operational pathway.*

*The procedure must remain:*

*• proportional;  
• documented;  
• reviewable;  
• role-specific;  
• evidence-aware;  
• legally appropriate;  
• technically grounded where implemented;  
• respectful of human dignity;  
• aligned with the constitutional source layer.*

*The access procedure is not designed to create unnecessary bureaucracy.*

*It is designed to prevent ambiguity.*

*A participant should know:*

*• what they are applying for;  
• which role or access scope is being requested;  
• which documents or declarations are required;  
• which governance body or process reviews the request;  
• which risks are being evaluated;  
• which rights are granted;  
• which rights are not granted;  
• which renewal or review duties apply;  
• which consequences may follow from breach, misrepresentation, or drift.*

*Prometheus must remain open enough to welcome real contribution and disciplined enough to protect the living field from capture.*

*The access procedure is the bridge between openness and institutional integrity.*

***8.1 Application***

*An application for access should identify the participant, the requested access scope, the intended role, and the relevant eligibility pathway.*

*Depending on the participant type, the application may be submitted through a Prometheus hApp interface, governance portal, legal intake process, institutional onboarding workflow, written application, signed agreement, or other approved pathway.*

*Prometheus should not assume that all access procedures are already fully automated.*

*Where digital infrastructure is implemented, the application may generate a signed digital record, source-chain entry, hash reference, or access-status record.*

*Where digital infrastructure is not yet implemented, the application may be processed through documented governance, legal, operational, or institutional procedures.*

*The application may include:*

*• participant type;  
• basic identifying information;  
• requested access scope;  
• intended role or function;  
• contact or representative details;  
• authority of representative, where applicable;  
• Access Covenant acknowledgment;  
• eligibility documentation required under Section 7;  
• legal documentation, where relevant;  
• governance disclosures, where relevant;  
• token, treasury, or financial-rights disclosures, where relevant;  
• data-access or technical-access requests, where relevant;  
• conflict-of-interest disclosures;  
• risk disclosures;  
• any other information reasonably required by the reviewing body.*

*The application must distinguish between:*

*• ordinary participation;  
• community participation;  
• governance participation;  
• operational participation;  
• technical participation;  
• MRV participation;  
• capital-facing participation;  
• institutional participation;  
• legal or financial participation.*

*This distinction is essential.*

*A participant may be eligible for one form of access and not another.*

*A community may be eligible for educational participation but not PRU-related claims.*

*A technical provider may be eligible for infrastructure support but not governance authority.*

*A capital allocator may be eligible for exploratory dialogue but not investor-facing participation.*

*A token community may be eligible for sandbox interoperability but not Prometheus governance rights.*

*Access must be scoped before it is granted.*

***8.2 Intake and Completeness Review***

*Before substantive review, the application should undergo an intake and completeness check.*

*The purpose of this step is to determine whether the application contains enough information to be evaluated.*

*The intake process may verify:*

*• identity or entity information;  
• representative authority;  
• requested access scope;  
• required documentation;  
• Access Covenant acknowledgment;  
• eligibility pathway;  
• obvious conflicts;  
• missing information;  
• need for legal review;  
• need for technical review;  
• need for MRV review;  
• need for community consultation;  
• need for enhanced risk classification.*

*If the application is incomplete, the reviewing body should request additional information.*

*An incomplete application should not be treated as denial unless the participant refuses to provide required information, provides materially false information, or presents a clear constitutional, legal, ecological, financial, technical, or governance risk.*

*The correct outcome for an incomplete but potentially valid application is:*

*review pending.*

*This preserves openness without weakening diligence.*

***8.3 Substantive Review***

*After intake, the application enters substantive review.*

*The reviewing body may be a Stewardship Circle, onboarding circle, governance circle, protocol-stewardship body, institutional review body, legal reviewer, technical reviewer, MRV reviewer, or other approved process depending on the access type.*

*The review should evaluate:*

*• constitutional compatibility;  
• Access Covenant alignment;  
• eligibility under Section 7;  
• role clarity;  
• risk profile;  
• legal requirements;  
• governance implications;  
• technical implications;  
• MRV implications;  
• financial or tokenomic implications;  
• community-field implications;  
• conflict-of-interest issues;  
• public-communication risks;  
• potential for capture, drift, overclaiming, or category collapse.*

*The review should not rely on one signal alone.*

*A strong application may require convergence across documents, declarations, governance records, public information, operational evidence, legal instruments, technical review, community consultation, or institutional due diligence.*

*Where appropriate, the reviewing body may classify the application according to risk:*

| ***Risk class***    | ***Meaning***                                                                                                                                       | ***Review intensity***                                           |
|---------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Low risk***      | *Ordinary participation with limited access and no sensitive claims.*                                                                               | *Standard review.*                                               |
| ***Moderate risk*** | *Participation affects community, governance, operations, data, or public representation.*                                                          | *Enhanced review.*                                               |
| ***High risk***     | *Participation affects capital, MRV, PRU, RAP, tokenization, institutional claims, legal rights, or sensitive infrastructure.*                      | *Formal review with legal, governance, technical, or MRV input.* |
| ***Critical risk*** | *Participation may affect constitutional integrity, investor-facing claims, protected infrastructure, major public commitments, or systemic trust.* | *Constitutional, legal, and multi-layer review required.*        |

*Risk classification does not imply hostility.*

*It determines the level of care required.*

*Prometheus should be friendly at the door and forensic at the boundary.*

***8.4 Community and Affected-Party Consultation***

*Where an application affects an existing community, territory, bioregion, stakeholder group, Tribe, DAO, token community, land base, data community, or institutional partner, consultation may be required.*

*Consultation may include:*

*• affected community representatives;  
• local stewards;  
• relevant governance circles;  
• technical maintainers;  
• MRV validators;  
• legal counterparties;  
• institutional partners;  
• community-interface roles;  
• conflict or integrity roles;  
• bioregional representatives.*

*Consultation is not automatic veto.*

*It is field intelligence.*

*The purpose is to identify context, risk, trust conditions, historical tensions, operational constraints, local knowledge, and possible hidden impacts.*

*Prometheus should distinguish:*

*• consultation;  
• consent;  
• advice;  
• approval;  
• legal authorization;  
• governance decision;  
• constitutional review.*

*These are not the same thing.*

*A community may provide context without holding formal decision authority.*

*A legal entity may authorize an agreement without creating community legitimacy.*

*A governance circle may approve a scope while still requiring MRV or legal review.*

*The procedure must keep these layers legible.*

***8.5 Decision Outcomes***

*After review, the reviewing body may issue one of several outcomes.*

| ***Decision outcome***                   | ***Meaning***                                                                                                                        |
|------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Approved***                           | *Access is granted within the defined scope.*                                                                                        |
| ***Approved with conditions***           | *Access is granted subject to limitations, disclosures, review duties, remediation, time limits, or monitoring.*                     |
| ***Limited / sandbox access***           | *Access is granted only for testing, observation, research, learning, or restricted interoperability.*                               |
| ***Observer status***                    | *Participant may learn or engage without operational, governance, capital, or technical authority.*                                  |
| ***Request for additional information*** | *Application is paused until missing information is provided.*                                                                       |
| ***Manual review required***             | *Application requires higher-level governance, legal, technical, MRV, or constitutional review.*                                     |
| ***Deferred***                           | *Application may be reconsidered later after specific conditions change.*                                                            |
| ***Restricted***                         | *Access is narrower than requested due to risk profile.*                                                                             |
| ***Rejected***                           | *Access is denied under current facts or constitutional constraints.*                                                                |
| ***Quarantined***                        | *The pathway presents serious capture, legal, ecological, financial, tokenomic, technical, or governance risk and must be isolated.* |

*Every material decision should be documented.*

*Documentation should include:*

*• participant identity or entity reference;  
• requested scope;  
• review pathway;  
• decision outcome;  
• granted scope;  
• limitations or conditions;  
• review date;  
• responsible reviewing body;  
• reasoned explanation where appropriate;  
• legal or technical references where relevant;  
• appeal or reconsideration pathway, where applicable.*

*The purpose of documentation is not bureaucracy.*

*It is institutional memory.*

*A system without memory becomes capturable by charisma, urgency, capital, or confusion.*

***8.6 Access Grant and Scope Definition***

*When access is approved, the scope of access must be defined.*

*Access may include one or more of the following:*

*• learning access;  
• community access;  
• governance access;  
• operational access;  
• technical access;  
• MRV access;  
• data-room access;  
• research access;  
• service-provider access;  
• capital-facing access;  
• institutional access;  
• sandbox access;  
• pilot-specific access.*

*Each access grant should specify:*

*• what the participant may do;  
• what the participant may not do;  
• which systems or spaces they may access;  
• which data they may access;  
• which decisions they may influence;  
• which claims they may make publicly;  
• whether legal documentation is required;  
• whether renewal is required;  
• whether monitoring or reporting applies;  
• whether access is time-bounded;  
• whether access depends on continued Covenant compliance.*

*No participant should receive ambiguous access.*

*Ambiguous access becomes future conflict.*

*Prometheus should therefore make the access grant precise enough that the participant, the community, governance reviewers, auditors, and future institutional counterparties can understand what was actually authorized.*

***8.7 Record Architecture***

*Approved access should generate an appropriate record.*

*The record may be digital, legal, governance-based, operational, or combined depending on implementation stage and access type.*

*A mature record may include:*

*• participant identity or entity reference;  
• Access Covenant version;  
• eligibility category;  
• requested access scope;  
• approved access scope;  
• conditions or restrictions;  
• timestamp;  
• reviewer or reviewing body;  
• legal agreement reference, where applicable;  
• hash reference, where applicable;  
• source-chain entry, where implemented;  
• governance decision reference;  
• renewal date;  
• suspension or revocation status, if any.*

*Where Prometheus uses Holochain-compatible infrastructure, access records may be represented through signed entries, source-chain records, DHT-published references, or validation-controlled access states.*

*However, the document should preserve implementation discipline.*

*A record may support traceability.*

*It does not by itself create financial rights, PRU value, land rights, investor rights, token entitlement, or governance authority beyond the scope explicitly granted.*

*The access record is evidence of status.*

*It is not a blank cheque from the gods of distributed systems.*

***8.8 Renewal and Reaffirmation***

*Access should be periodically reviewed.*

*The review frequency should depend on role, risk, access scope, legal context, and implementation maturity.*

*Possible renewal triggers include:*

*• annual reaffirmation;  
• expiration of access term;  
• change of participant role;  
• change of entity structure;  
• change of control;  
• change of tokenomics;  
• change of investment strategy;  
• change of legal status;  
• change of MRV role;  
• change of technical permissions;  
• breach or suspected breach;  
• material governance concern;  
• material ecological, financial, technical, or reputational risk.*

*Reaffirmation may include:*

*• renewed Access Covenant acknowledgment;  
• confirmation of current role;  
• confirmation of current representative authority;  
• updated documentation;  
• conflict-of-interest update;  
• confirmation of continued eligibility;  
• review of conditions or restrictions;  
• update of access scope.*

*Failure to renew should be handled proportionately.*

*Possible responses include:*

*• reminder;  
• grace period;  
• review-required status;  
• temporary limitation of sensitive access;  
• suspension of governance or technical functions;  
• suspension of capital-facing privileges;  
• termination of access where appropriate.*

*Prometheus should avoid automatic punitive escalation unless formally defined, technically implemented, legally appropriate, and governance-approved.*

*The purpose of renewal is continuity of coherence, not bureaucratic ambush.*

***8.9 Material Changes***

*A participant must disclose material changes that may affect eligibility, risk, access scope, or Covenant alignment. Material changes may include:*

*• change of legal identity;  
• change of ownership or control;  
• change of representative authority;  
• change of governance structure;  
• change of tokenomics;  
• change of treasury policy;  
• change of investment strategy;  
• change of regulatory status;  
• change of data-processing practices;  
• change of technical infrastructure;  
• change of MRV methodology or role;  
• change of public claims;  
• change of business model;  
• change of land-access arrangement;  
• change of community governance;  
• emergence of conflict of interest;  
• litigation, enforcement, insolvency, or serious reputational event.*

*Material changes do not automatically terminate access.*

*They trigger review. The review may result in:*

*• no change;  
• updated access scope;  
• additional documentation;  
• enhanced monitoring;  
• temporary restriction;  
• reclassification of risk;  
• legal review;  
• governance review;  
• suspension;  
• termination.*

*The principle is adaptive governance. Prometheus should respond to reality as it changes.*

***8.10 Ongoing Compliance and Monitoring***

*Participation in Prometheus is not a one-time event.*

*It is a continuing relationship.*

*Ongoing compliance may include:*

*• Covenant compliance;  
• role compliance;  
• reporting obligations;  
• technical-access discipline;  
• MRV integrity;  
• governance participation rules;  
• legal agreement compliance;  
• public-communication discipline;  
• data-protection compliance;  
• conflict-of-interest updates;  
• periodic review.*

*Monitoring may be human, procedural, legal, technical, governance-based, or automated where implementation allows.*

*Prometheus must not overclaim automatic enforcement.*

*Where automated alerts, validation rules, warrant logic, reputation signals, anomaly detection, or technical controls are implemented, they should support review.*

*They should not replace due process where human, legal, ecological, financial, or constitutional interpretation is required.*

*The system may flag.*

*The system may restrict.*

*The system may pause.*

*The system may escalate.*

*But high-impact consequences should remain reviewable according to the applicable governance and legal pathway.*

*This is essential for institutional credibility.*

*Automatic punishment is not governance.*

*It is machinery wearing a judge’s wig.*

*Prometheus must be better than that.*

***8.11 Appeal, Reconsideration, and Review***

*Where access is denied, restricted, suspended, or materially conditioned, the participant may be entitled to appeal or reconsideration depending on the nature of the decision, access scope, legal context, and governance rules.*

*An appeal pathway may include:*

*• reasoned request for reconsideration;  
• submission of additional evidence;  
• review by a higher or separate circle;  
• legal review, where relevant;  
• technical review, where relevant;  
• MRV review, where relevant;  
• integrity review, where relevant;  
• constitutional review for source-layer questions.*

*Appeals should not become a mechanism to pressure the system into lowering constitutional standards.*

*The reviewing body should distinguish between:*

*• factual error;  
• missing information;  
• misunderstanding of access scope;  
• changed circumstances;  
• governance disagreement;  
• legal dispute;  
• constitutional incompatibility.*

*A participant may correct facts.*

*A participant may provide missing evidence.*

*A participant may narrow the requested scope.*

*A participant may enter through sandbox access.*

*But no appeal may override the foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, evidence-before-value discipline, PRU/MRV boundaries, or source-layer non-bypassability.*

*The highest appeal is not power.*

*The highest appeal is fidelity.*

***8.12 Emergency Limitation of Access***

*In urgent situations, Prometheus may temporarily limit access before full review is complete.*

*Emergency limitation may be appropriate where there is credible risk of:*

*• ecological harm;  
• data manipulation;  
• MRV falsification;  
• investor-facing misrepresentation;  
• tokenomic confusion;  
• technical compromise;  
• security breach;  
• governance capture;  
• coercion or exploitation;  
• serious Covenant violation;  
• legal or regulatory exposure;  
• reputational harm to the ecosystem;  
• constitutional bypass.*

*Emergency limitation should be:*

*• temporary;  
• documented;  
• proportionate;  
• reviewable;  
• limited to the affected function where possible;  
• escalated to the proper reviewing body.*

*Emergency action should not become a shortcut for ordinary governance.*

*It is an immune response, not a permanent constitution.*

*After emergency limitation, the system should proceed to review, remediation, suspension, restoration, restriction, or termination according to the applicable process.*

***8.13 Final Access Procedure Principle***

*The access procedure exists to ensure that Prometheus remains both open and coherent.*

*It should welcome contribution. It should respect pluralism.*

*It should make space for communities, capital, technology, science, culture, spirituality, governance innovation, and future forms of human coordination.*

*But it should never confuse welcome with waiver.*

*Access does not waive the Covenant.*

*Participation does not waive evidence.*

*Community status does not waive dignity.*

*Capital does not waive law.*

*Technology does not waive review.*

*AI does not waive authorization.*

*Tokens do not waive legal structure.*

*Governance does not waive the source layer.*

*The final principle is therefore:*

*access is not possession;  
participation is not entitlement;  
approval is not immunity;  
status is not sovereignty;  
contribution is not capture;  
and openness is protected by disciplined boundaries.*

*Prometheus becomes trustworthy because it does not close the door to the world.*

*It builds a threshold worthy of what may enter.*

***9. Covenant Violation, Remediation, and Consequence Architecture***

*The Access Covenant must be enforceable enough to protect Prometheus and proportionate enough to preserve justice.*

*A Covenant violation is not merely a procedural error.*

*It is a disturbance in the relationship between participant, community, evidence, governance, and the constitutional source layer.*

*For this reason, Prometheus treats violations through a graduated architecture of review, remediation, limitation, suspension, exclusion, and external escalation where necessary.*

*The purpose is not punishment for its own sake. The purpose is to protect the living field, preserve evidence integrity, maintain trust, prevent capture, and restore coherence wherever restoration remains possible. Prometheus therefore adopts a consequence architecture based on:*

*• evidence;  
• proportionality;  
• due process;  
• right to respond where appropriate;  
• remediation before exclusion where possible;  
• protection of vulnerable participants;  
• protection of ecological and investor-facing integrity;  
• protection of the constitutional source layer;  
• legal and governance review for high-impact cases;  
• technical enforcement only where implemented, tested, and governed.*

*The system must be firm without becoming arbitrary.*

*It must be protective without becoming punitive by default.*

*It must be capable of exclusion without turning exclusion into its first language.*

*A regenerative constitution must know how to say no. But it must also know how to repair.*

***9.1 Definition of Covenant Violation***

*A Covenant violation occurs when a participant, entity, role-holder, community, technical operator, capital allocator, digital agent, or institutional actor acts in a manner materially inconsistent with the Access Covenant, foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, source-layer constraints, evidence-before-value discipline, or approved participation scope. A violation may occur through:*

*• action;  
• omission;  
• misrepresentation;  
• concealment;  
• negligence;  
• repeated non-compliance;  
• attempted bypass;  
• coercion;  
• data manipulation;  
• governance manipulation;  
• tokenomic confusion;  
• investor-facing overclaiming;  
• ecological harm;  
• misuse of community status;  
• misuse of capital influence;  
• misuse of technical access;  
• misuse of AI or automated systems;  
• public communication that materially misrepresents Prometheus status, rights, evidence, PRU, RAP, MRV, TRBK, tokenization, financial entitlement, or legal authority.*

*Not every error is a violation.*

*Not every disagreement is a violation.*

*Not every failed proposal is a violation.*

*Not every weak signal is misconduct.*

*Prometheus must distinguish between:*

*• mistake;  
• misunderstanding;  
• immaturity;  
• negligence;  
• breach;  
• manipulation;  
• capture attempt;  
• constitutional violation.*

*This distinction protects fairness. It also protects the system from both naivety and overreaction.*

***9.2 Violation Review Pathway***

*A potential violation should pass through a review pathway before significant consequences are imposed, except where emergency limitation is required to prevent serious harm.*

*The review pathway may include:*

1.  ***Signal or report**  
    A concern, anomaly, complaint, evidence gap, technical flag, governance concern, community concern, audit finding, or legal notice is identified.*

2.  ***Initial classification**  
    The concern is classified by severity, domain, urgency, affected parties, and possible constitutional relevance.*

3.  ***Evidence collection**  
    Relevant documents, records, logs, communications, MRV evidence, technical events, governance records, legal instruments, or witness statements are gathered.*

4.  ***Participant response**  
    Where appropriate, the participant is given an opportunity to respond, clarify, correct, or provide missing information.*

5.  ***Review body assessment**  
    The appropriate governance, legal, MRV, technical, integrity, or constitutional review body assesses the matter.*

6.  ***Consequence determination**  
    A proportionate response is selected based on severity, intent, repetition, harm, evidence, legal context, and willingness to remediate.*

7.  ***Documentation**  
    The decision and reasoning are recorded.*

8.  ***Remediation or enforcement**  
    Corrective, restrictive, restorative, suspension, exclusion, or escalation measures are applied.*

9.  ***Appeal or reconsideration**  
    Where applicable, the participant may seek review through the defined pathway.*

10. ***Closure or monitoring**  
    The case is closed, monitored, or escalated depending on residual risk.*

*This pathway ensures that Prometheus does not confuse automated detection with judgment.*

*A signal may trigger review. It does not automatically become guilt.*

***9.3 Severity Classification***

*Prometheus may classify Covenant violations by severity.*

*Severity should be determined by considering:*

*• intent;  
• harm;  
• repetition;  
• role of the participant;  
• access scope;  
• affected parties;  
• ecological impact;  
• evidence impact;  
• governance impact;  
• legal or regulatory exposure;  
• investor-facing consequences;  
• risk to community trust;  
• risk to constitutional integrity;  
• willingness to disclose, repair, and cooperate.*

*A severity framework may be expressed as follows:*

| ***Severity*** | ***Examples***                                                                                                                                                                                                | ***Default response orientation***                                                                                   |
|----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Minor***    | *Inadvertent reporting error, incomplete documentation, procedural delay, minor misunderstanding of scope.*                                                                                                   | *Clarification, education, correction, warning, or remediation.*                                                     |
| ***Moderate*** | *Repeated negligence, failure to correct after notice, role overreach, weak documentation affecting trust, minor misleading claim.*                                                                           | *Formal remediation plan, restricted access, role review, monitoring, or temporary limitation.*                      |
| ***Major***    | *Deliberate MRV distortion, serious public misrepresentation, governance manipulation, significant breach of access scope, exploitative conduct, material data integrity issue.*                              | *Suspension, quarantine, non-admissible status, governance review, legal review, public correction where required.*  |
| ***Critical*** | *Intentional falsification of PRU-relevant evidence, serious ecological harm, coercion, investor-facing fraud, constitutional bypass attempt, capture attempt, serious technical compromise, systemic abuse.* | *Emergency limitation, constitutional review, exclusion, external legal or regulatory escalation where appropriate.* |

*Severity classification is not mechanical.*

*It must be interpreted through evidence and context.*

*A minor error repeated after warnings may become moderate.*

*A moderate issue affecting investor-facing claims may become major.*

*A single critical action may justify immediate protective limitation before full review.*

*Prometheus must be able to protect itself without abandoning due process.*

***9.4 Consequence Types***

*Prometheus may apply different consequence types depending on severity and context.*

| ***Consequence type***        | ***Function***                                                                                                                                |
|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Clarification***           | *Corrects misunderstanding or ambiguous scope.*                                                                                               |
| ***Education / remediation*** | *Helps participant realign with the Covenant.*                                                                                                |
| ***Formal warning***          | *Records a breach and requires correction.*                                                                                                   |
| ***Correction notice***       | *Requires correction of inaccurate claim, record, report, or communication.*                                                                  |
| ***Remediation plan***        | *Defines actions, deadlines, responsibilities, and review points.*                                                                            |
| ***Role review***             | *Reviews whether the participant should continue holding a role.*                                                                             |
| ***Restricted status***       | *Limits specific rights, accesses, functions, or claims.*                                                                                     |
| ***Suspended status***        | *Temporarily pauses sensitive access pending review or remediation.*                                                                          |
| ***Non-admissible status***   | *Prevents a claim, record, output, or pathway from entering PRU, RAP, governance, investor-facing, token, MRV, or public-reporting pathways.* |
| ***Quarantined status***      | *Isolates a pathway, dataset, participant, project, or claim due to serious risk.*                                                            |
| ***Corrective disclosure***   | *Requires public or private correction where prior communication created confusion or risk.*                                                  |
| ***Exclusion***               | *Removes participant from defined forms of participation according to governance and legal procedure.*                                        |
| ***External escalation***     | *Refers serious misconduct to competent legal, regulatory, fiduciary, enforcement, or institutional authorities.*                             |

*Consequence does not always mean exclusion. In many cases, the correct response is correction, education, limitation, or remediation.*

*Where harm is serious, repeated, intentional, or constitutional, stronger measures become necessary.*

*Prometheus protects participation by protecting trust.*

***9.5 Remediation First, Where Possible***

*Prometheus should prefer remediation where the violation is minor, moderate, unintentional, correctable, and not structurally harmful.*

*A remediation plan may include:*

*• corrected documentation;  
• corrected public communication;  
• renewed Access Covenant affirmation;  
• additional training;  
• role clarification;  
• role limitation;  
• supervision;  
• technical access review;  
• data correction;  
• MRV resubmission;  
• community repair process;  
• conflict mediation;  
• legal documentation update;  
• reporting correction;  
• review date.*

*Remediation is appropriate when the participant demonstrates:*

*• good faith;  
• willingness to correct;  
• transparency;  
• cooperation with review;  
• respect for the Covenant;  
• no serious intent to manipulate, extract, or bypass.*

*Remediation is not appropriate where it becomes a shield for repeated harm.*

*A regenerative system forgives error.*

*It does not normalize abuse.*

***9.6 Non-Admissibility of Claims and Outputs***

*One of the most important consequences in Prometheus is non-admissibility.*

*Non-admissibility means that a claim, output, record, dataset, communication, action, or pathway may not enter protected Prometheus pathways.*

*These may include:*

*• MRV validation;  
• PRU assessment;  
• PRU maturity classification;  
• RAP inclusion;  
• investor reporting;  
• public impact reporting;  
• token-mediated representation;  
• governance decision-making;  
• capital-facing communication;  
• institutional data rooms;  
• legal or financial structuring.*

*Non-admissibility protects the system without necessarily excluding the participant entirely.*

*For example:*

*• a community narrative may remain meaningful but not MRV-admissible;  
• a dataset may remain archived but not PRU-admissible;  
• a pilot claim may remain exploratory but not investor-facing;  
• a token claim may remain internal to another ecosystem but not Prometheus-valid;  
• an ecological observation may remain useful but not financially admissible.*

*This distinction is essential.*

*Prometheus does not need to punish every ambiguity.*

*It needs to prevent ambiguity from becoming value.*

***9.7 Technical Enforcement Boundary***

*Technical systems may support Covenant enforcement.*

*They may:*

*• detect anomalies;  
• flag invalid entries;  
• restrict permissions;  
• pause sensitive functions;  
• log suspicious behavior;  
• require manual review;  
• quarantine records;  
• revoke credentials;  
• enforce validation rules where implemented;  
• prevent unauthorized operations.*

*However, Prometheus must not overclaim automatic enforcement.*

*Technical enforcement is valid only where the relevant mechanism is:*

*• implemented;  
• tested;  
• audited where appropriate;  
• governed;  
• documented;  
• legally and procedurally compatible with the consequence imposed.*

*Holochain-compatible validation rules, warrants, source-chain records, DHT references, access-control logic, or policy engines may support integrity.*

*They do not replace governance judgment in high-impact matters.*

*Software may reject an invalid entry.*

*Software may flag a breach.*

*Software may restrict a permission.*

*Software may preserve evidence.*

*But software should not independently impose high-impact legal, financial, reputational, governance, or human consequences without the applicable review pathway.*

*Prometheus uses technical enforcement as immune support.*

*It does not outsource justice to code.*

***9.8 Token-Related Consequences***

*Token-related consequences require special care.*

*No token-related penalty, restriction, reduction, lock, forfeiture, burn, slash, suspension, or loss of entitlement should be described as automatic unless it is legally structured, technically implemented, contractually disclosed, governance-approved, and jurisdictionally reviewed.*

*Where token-mediated participation exists, possible responses may include:*

*• temporary suspension of token-mediated governance participation;  
• restriction of access functions;  
• loss of eligibility for specific token-gated privileges;  
• removal from a whitelisted pathway;  
• review-required status;  
• limitation of voting scope;  
• correction of misleading token-related claims;  
• legal review before any economic consequence.*

*Prometheus should avoid casual use of terms such as slashing, forfeiture, or permanent token loss unless those mechanisms are formally part of a compliant legal and technical instrument.*

*The correct principle is:*

*token-related consequences must follow the rights actually attached to the token.*

*A token cannot lose rights it never legally had.*

*A participant cannot forfeit value through a mechanism that was never properly disclosed, agreed, and structured.*

*This preserves both constitutional integrity and legal credibility.*

***9.9 Public Communication and Corrective Disclosure***

*Some violations create confusion outside the system.*

*This may occur through public claims about:*

*• PRU value;  
• RAP inclusion;  
• MRV verification;  
• ecological performance;  
• investor rights;  
• token rights;  
• land rights;  
• governance authority;  
• partnership status;  
• institutional approval;  
• health claims;  
• financial returns;  
• legal status.*

*Where public confusion occurs, Prometheus may require corrective disclosure.*

*Corrective disclosure may include:*

*• private correction to affected parties;  
• public clarification;  
• amended report;  
• revised dashboard language;  
• investor notice;  
• removal of misleading material;  
• corrected token or community communication;  
• legal disclaimer;  
• governance statement.*

*Corrective disclosure should be proportionate.*

*It should correct confusion without unnecessary humiliation.*

*The purpose is not reputational theatre.*

*The purpose is semantic hygiene.*

*Prometheus cannot allow words to become unverified value.*

***9.10 Ecological and MRV Violations***

*Ecological and MRV violations are especially serious because Prometheus depends on evidence integrity.*

*Examples may include:*

*• falsified soil data;  
• manipulated sensor readings;  
• altered laboratory reports;  
• fabricated field observations;  
• misleading satellite or drone interpretation;  
• hidden adverse events;  
• inflated biodiversity claims;  
• unreported ecological damage;  
• double counting;  
• unsupported PRU claims;  
• failure to disclose methodology limitations;  
• intentional misclassification of evidence tier;  
• conversion of exploratory hypotheses into financial claims.*

*Responses may include:*

*• MRV review;  
• data quarantine;  
• non-admissible status;  
• resampling;  
• third-party audit;  
• methodology review;  
• PRU recalculation;  
• PRU maturity classification downgrade where applicable;  
• RAP exclusion where applicable;  
• investor reporting correction;  
• suspension of MRV privileges;  
• legal or regulatory escalation where warranted.*

*The principle is clear:*

*weak evidence may guide learning;*

*verified evidence may support reporting;*

*admissible evidence may support value;*

*falsified evidence threatens the system.*

***9.11 Governance Violations***

*Governance violations occur when decision-making processes are manipulated, bypassed, captured, misrepresented, or used to weaken constitutional integrity.*

*Examples may include:*

*• role overreach;  
• hidden conflicts of interest;  
• coercive voting pressure;  
• token-weight manipulation;  
• community intimidation;  
• suppression of dissent;  
• abuse of facilitation role;  
• unauthorized representation;  
• unrecorded material decisions;  
• false claim of approval;  
• bypass of Access Covenant conditions;  
• misuse of emergency procedure;  
• attempt to alter protected constitutional principles through ordinary governance.*

*Responses may include:*

*• governance review;  
• role suspension;  
• role replacement;  
• conflict-resolution process;  
• decision invalidation;  
• re-vote or consent process;  
• integrity circle review;  
• constitutional review;  
• restriction of governance participation;  
• external legal review where relevant.*

*Governance is not valid merely because a meeting occurred.*

*Governance is valid when authority, process, record, consent, and constitutional boundaries are intact.*

***9.12 Constitutional Violations***

*A constitutional violation is a serious breach involving foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, source-layer constraints, Access Covenant substance, non-bypassability rules, human dignity, ecological integrity, or evidence-before-value discipline.*

*Examples may include:*

*• attempt to amend or weaken Eternity Clauses;  
• attempt to bypass the Access Covenant;  
• attempt to authorize extraction under regenerative language;  
• attempt to convert unverified claims into PRU value;  
• attempt to use AI output as constitutional authority;  
• attempt to use token governance to override living-system reality constraints;  
• attempt to use capital pressure to weaken source-layer protections;  
• attempt to redefine human dignity, ecological integrity, MRV, PRU, RAP, or governance boundaries outside the constitutional pathway.*

*Constitutional violations may trigger:*

*• immediate review;  
• emergency limitation;  
• non-admissibility;  
• quarantine;  
• suspension;  
• constitutional interpretation;  
• exclusion;  
• public correction;  
• external escalation where appropriate.*

*The standard is strict.*

*No ordinary governance process can legitimate a constitutional violation.*

*No token vote can legitimate it.*

*No investor demand can legitimate it.*

*No model output can legitimate it.*

*No emergency can legitimate it.*

*A constitutional violation is not merely unauthorized.*

*It is outside the valid Prometheus pathway.*

***9.13 Appeal and Reconsideration***

*Participants affected by material consequences may request appeal or reconsideration where appropriate.*

*The appeal pathway may include:*

*• correction of factual error;  
• submission of missing evidence;  
• clarification of scope;  
• proof of remediation;  
• review by another circle;  
• legal review;  
• technical review;  
• MRV review;  
• constitutional review.*

*Appeal is not a mechanism to relitigate the Covenant itself.*

*It is a mechanism to ensure that facts, evidence, proportionality, and process were handled correctly.*

*An appeal may result in:*

*• confirmation of decision;  
• modification of consequence;  
• restoration of access;  
• revised remediation plan;  
• narrower restriction;  
• stronger restriction;  
• escalation;  
• closure.*

*Appeal preserves justice.*

*It does not suspend the constitutional source layer.*

***9.14 Restoration of Status***

*Where a participant has corrected a violation, completed remediation, or reduced risk, status may be restored.*

*Restoration may require:*

*• renewed Covenant affirmation;  
• completion of remediation plan;  
• corrected documentation;  
• corrected public communication;  
• governance review;  
• technical access review;  
• legal clearance;  
• MRV resubmission;  
• community repair;  
• monitoring period;  
• updated risk classification.*

*Restoration should be possible where trust can be rebuilt.*

*Prometheus is a regenerative system.*

*It should allow responsible return when repair is real.*

*However, restoration is not automatic.*

*Some breaches may permanently affect eligibility for specific roles, claims, or access scopes.*

*A participant may be restored for ordinary participation but remain restricted from MRV validation, governance authority, investor-facing communication, technical administration, or capital-facing access.*

*Restoration should be precise.*

*Trust is rebuilt by clarity.*

***9.15 External Legal, Regulatory, or Institutional Escalation***

*Certain conduct may require escalation beyond Prometheus governance.*

*External escalation may be appropriate where there is credible evidence of:*

*• fraud;  
• investor deception;  
• securities violation;  
• data breach;  
• ecological damage requiring legal response;  
• misuse of funds;  
• coercion or exploitation;  
• fiduciary breach;  
• regulatory non-compliance;  
• public-sector misconduct;  
• criminal conduct;  
• serious harm to vulnerable persons.*

*External escalation may involve:*

*• legal counsel;  
• regulators;  
• auditors;  
• fiduciaries;  
• public authorities;  
• law enforcement where legally required;  
• institutional partners;  
• insurers or guarantors where relevant.*

*Prometheus governance does not replace law.*

*It complements law within the ecosystem.*

*Where legal duties exist, they must be respected.*

***9.16 Final Consequence Principle***

*The final principle is:*

*protect life;  
protect dignity;  
protect evidence;  
protect trust;  
protect the source layer;  
repair where possible;  
restrict where necessary;  
exclude where unavoidable;  
escalate where legally required.*

*Prometheus does not treat consequence as vengeance.*

*It treats consequence as immune intelligence.*

*A living system protects itself by recognizing disturbance, isolating harm, repairing tissue, learning from injury, and restoring circulation.*

*Prometheus must do the same.*

*A Covenant violation is therefore not only a breach of rule.*

*It is a signal about system coherence.*

*The correct response is not automatic punishment.*

*The correct response is proportionate restoration of integrity.*

*Where repair is possible, repair.*

*Where limitation is necessary, limit.*

*Where exclusion is required, exclude.*

*Where law must act, escalate.*

*Where the constitution is threatened, protect the source.*

*The Access Covenant opens the door.*

*The consequence architecture keeps the house from burning down after the party starts.*

***10. Constitutional Interpretation and Source-Layer Review***

*Prometheus requires a constitutional interpretation process capable of resolving ambiguity without centralizing sovereignty.*

*A living constitutional system must be able to interpret its own principles, apply them to new cases, respond to conflict, and clarify boundaries over time.*

*However, interpretation must not become domination.*

*No circle, founder, token majority, investor, AI model, protocol steward, technical operator, oracle, or institutional actor may become the final sovereign over the Prometheus constitution.*

*The constitution remains the authority.*

*Evidence remains the discipline.*

*Governance remains the pathway.*

*Living-system reality remains the ground.*

*Constitutional interpretation therefore exists to clarify meaning, not to create arbitrary power.*

*It exists to preserve fidelity, not to manufacture exceptions.*

*It exists to protect the source layer, not to convert governance into supremacy.*

***10.1 Purpose of Constitutional Interpretation***

*Constitutional interpretation is required when a proposal, action, claim, governance process, technical implementation, financial structure, token mechanism, AI output, MRV pathway, community process, or institutional relationship raises a question about constitutional meaning.*

*Such questions may include:*

*• whether an action is compatible with the foundational articles;  
• whether an action violates the Eternity Clauses;  
• whether a proposal belongs to ordinary governance or constitutional amendment;  
• whether a claim is evidence-backed or merely narrative;  
• whether a token mechanism creates category confusion;  
• whether a PRU, RAP, MRV, HSI, TRBK, or governance term is being used consistently with canonical definitions;  
• whether a technical system bypasses source-layer constraints;  
• whether an AI-generated recommendation exceeds its valid authority;  
• whether a community collaboration remains within sovereign alignment;  
• whether a financial structure creates extraction under regenerative language;  
• whether emergency action is justified and proportionate;  
• whether a participant’s conduct requires consequence, remediation, or constitutional review.*

*Interpretation should therefore be understood as a review function.*

*It does not replace ordinary governance.*

*It does not replace legal review.*

*It does not replace MRV.*

*It does not replace scientific evidence.*

*It does not replace community consent where consent is required.*

*It determines whether a pathway remains constitutionally admissible.*

***10.2 Interpretive Authority as Distributed Review***

*Prometheus should not vest ultimate interpretive authority in a single circle.*

*A constitutional question may require input from different bodies depending on the matter.*

*Relevant review bodies may include:*

*• Stewardship Circles;  
• protocol stewards;  
• constitutional review circles;  
• MRV reviewers;  
• legal reviewers;  
• technical reviewers;  
• governance facilitators;  
• community-interface representatives;  
• affected-party reviewers;  
• scientific or ecological advisors;  
• capital-market or compliance reviewers where relevant.*

*This is distributed review, not fragmented authority.*

*Each body contributes from its domain of competence.*

*The role of the constitutional review pathway is to integrate these perspectives under the source-layer hierarchy.*

*No single reviewer owns the constitution. No single circle becomes the constitution.*

*No technical system becomes the constitution.*

*No founder intent becomes the constitution.*

*The constitution is interpreted through documented, reviewable, multi-domain reasoning constrained by the foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, evidence-before-value discipline, and living-system reality.*

***10.3 Source-Layer Hierarchy***

*When constitutional interpretation is required, the hierarchy of authority is:*

| ***Layer***                                     | ***Interpretive function***                                                                                    |
|-------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Living-system reality***                     | *The ecological, biological, relational, and physical substrate that governance must respect.*                 |
| ***Eternity Clauses***                          | *Unamendable constraints protecting life, dignity, evidence, ecological integrity, and regenerative fidelity.* |
| ***Foundational articles***                     | *Constitutional structure defining Prometheus identity and governance boundaries.*                             |
| ***Access Covenant***                           | *Entry and participation condition for all Prometheus-facing actors.*                                          |
| ***Canonical definitions***                     | *Meaning of PRU, RAP, MRV, HSI, TRBK, OHE, tokenization, governance, and related terms.*                       |
| ***Approved methodologies***                    | *Versioned technical, MRV, financial, governance, and operational methods.*                                    |
| ***Ordinary governance decisions***             | *Valid only within the above boundaries.*                                                                      |
| ***Technical implementation***                  | *Valid only when aligned with constitutional meaning and approved methodology.*                                |
| ***AI outputs***                                | *Assistive only; never independently authoritative.*                                                           |
| ***Market, investor, or operational pressure*** | *Relevant context, never constitutional authority.*                                                            |

*This hierarchy prevents category inversion.*

*A token vote cannot override the Access Covenant.*

*An investor term sheet cannot override MRV.*

*A model output cannot override governance.*

*A dashboard cannot override methodology.*

*A methodology cannot override the Eternity Clauses.*

*A founder preference cannot override living-system reality.*

*Prometheus remains coherent only while this hierarchy remains intact.*

***10.4 Interpretive Principles***

*Constitutional interpretation should follow defined principles.*

***Life-preserving interpretation***

*Where multiple interpretations are possible, the interpretation that best preserves life, dignity, ecological integrity, and regenerative capacity should be preferred.*

***Evidence-before-value interpretation***

*No interpretation may allow unverified narrative, projected impact, cultural meaning, spiritual testimony, AI inference, or financial expectation to become PRU value, RAP inclusion, investor-facing claim, or ecological performance without evidence and admissibility.*

***Function-separation interpretation***

*PRU, RAP, MRV, TRBK, HSI, HoloFuel, stablecoin rails, governance rights, land rights, cash-flow rights, investor exposure, and future tokenized representations must remain functionally distinct unless a formal legal and governance framework explicitly connects them.*

***Least-capture interpretation***

*Where a decision could create capture by founders, capital, tokens, technical operators, AI systems, institutions, or community elites, the interpretation that preserves distributed accountability and reviewability should be preferred.*

***Pilot-first interpretation***

*Where evidence is immature, the system should prefer bounded pilots, sandbox status, manual review, disclosure, and staged progression rather than immediate institutional claims.*

***Local-context interpretation***

*Regenerative systems are contextual. Interpretation should respect place, community, ecology, culture, and operational reality while preserving constitutional boundaries.*

***Future-generation interpretation***

*Where long-term consequences are material, future generations should be treated as present stakeholders in the interpretive process.*

***No-bypass interpretation***

*No interpretation may authorize a pathway that makes constitutional bypass easier, hidden, irreversible, or non-reviewable.*

***10.5 Founder Intent and Founding Memory***

*Founding intent may help illuminate the origin of Prometheus.*

*It may provide context.*

*It may preserve memory.*

*It may explain why certain principles were articulated.*

*It may help future builders understand the danger that the architecture was designed to prevent.*

*However, founding intent is not supreme authority.*

*Founders do not own the constitution.*

*Founding communities do not own the constitution.*

*Historical memory does not create veto power.*

*Original vision does not justify constitutional bypass.*

*The proper role of founding memory is custodial.*

*It may inform interpretation where the text is ambiguous.*

*It may help preserve fidelity.*

*It may warn against semantic drift.*

*It may support education and continuity.*

*But where founding memory conflicts with the foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, evidence-before-value discipline, or living-system reality, the constitutional source layer prevails.*

*Prometheus honors its origin by refusing to become captive to origin.*

***10.6 Ecological and Scientific Advice***

*Scientific, ecological, and bioregional advice may be essential in constitutional interpretation.*

*Questions involving soil, water, biodiversity, planetary boundaries, human health, MRV, ecological risk, land stewardship, or regenerative claims should involve appropriate expertise.*

*This may include:*

*• soil scientists;  
• ecologists;  
• hydrologists;  
• agroecologists;  
• biodiversity specialists;  
• MRV experts;  
• public-health researchers;  
• local stewards;  
• indigenous or traditional ecological knowledge holders where appropriate;  
• bioregional observers;  
• auditors and verifiers;  
• institutional reviewers.*

*However, ecological advice is not oracle supremacy.*

*No advisor becomes an oracle.*

*No dataset becomes absolute authority.*

*No model becomes final truth.*

*No scientific committee can authorize violation of the Eternity Clauses.*

*Ecological and scientific advice informs interpretation.*

*It does not replace the constitution.*

*The correct principle is:*

*science informs the pathway;*

*evidence constrains the claim;*

*the constitution governs admissibility;*

*living-system reality remains the ground.*

***10.7 Precedent and Learning***

*Past decisions may guide future interpretation.*

*Prometheus should maintain a constitutional interpretation record where material decisions are documented.*

*This record may include:*

*• the question presented;  
• affected provisions;  
• evidence considered;  
• governance bodies involved;  
• ecological or scientific advice received;  
• legal or technical review;  
• reasoning;  
• decision;  
• limitations;  
• dissenting or minority reasoning where useful;  
• review date;  
• later correction or reversal if required.*

*Precedent supports consistency.*

*However, precedent is not a cage.*

*A past decision may be refined, distinguished, corrected, or overruled when later evidence, legal analysis, governance maturity, ecological reality, or constitutional reasoning shows that correction is necessary.*

*The common-law method is useful only when it remains accountable to life.*

*Prometheus should therefore treat precedent as learning memory, not fossilized authority.*

***10.8 Interpretive Procedure***

*A constitutional interpretation process may follow this sequence:*

1.  ***Trigger**  
    A question, conflict, proposal, risk, ambiguity, violation, or novel case is identified.*

2.  ***Issue framing**  
    The interpretive question is stated clearly.*

3.  ***Source-layer mapping**  
    Relevant foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant provisions, canonical definitions, methodologies, and governance rules are identified.*

4.  ***Domain review**  
    Appropriate ecological, MRV, legal, technical, governance, community, financial, or scientific reviewers are consulted.*

5.  ***Risk classification**  
    The risk of capture, bypass, overclaiming, legal exposure, ecological harm, financial misrepresentation, technical fragility, or governance drift is classified.*

6.  ***Reasoned interpretation**  
    A written interpretation is produced, explaining the reasoning and boundaries.*

7.  ***Decision status**  
    The decision is classified as advisory, binding within a defined scope, temporary, pilot-specific, methodology-specific, constitutional, or requiring amendment.*

8.  ***Publication or restricted record**  
    The decision is recorded publicly or internally depending on confidentiality, legal, privacy, and governance constraints.*

9.  ***Review pathway**  
    The decision includes a review point, appeal pathway, or correction mechanism where appropriate.*

10. ***Implementation and auditability**  
    Any operational implementation is linked to records, execution gates, controls, and audit trails.*

*This process prevents interpretive power from becoming informal.*

*If the constitution must be interpreted, the interpretation must itself be reviewable.*

***10.9 Interpretation Outcomes***

*A constitutional interpretation may produce several outcomes.*

| ***Outcome***                           | ***Meaning***                                                                                          |
|-----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Admissible***                        | *The pathway is compatible with constitutional constraints.*                                           |
| ***Admissible with conditions***        | *The pathway may proceed only under defined limitations, disclosures, controls, or review.*            |
| ***Pilot / sandbox only***              | *The pathway may be explored but not represented as mature, verified, or institutionally admissible.*  |
| ***Non-admissible***                    | *The claim, output, method, participant, or pathway may not enter protected Prometheus pathways.*      |
| ***Requires ordinary governance***      | *The issue belongs to operational governance rather than constitutional interpretation.*               |
| ***Requires constitutional amendment*** | *The issue materially affects constitutional architecture and must follow the amendment pathway.*      |
| ***Barred by Eternity Clauses***        | *The pathway is outside Prometheus and cannot be authorized.*                                          |
| ***Requires legal review***             | *The pathway may not proceed until jurisdictional or contractual analysis is complete.*                |
| ***Requires MRV review***               | *Evidence or methodology is insufficient for the claim being made.*                                    |
| ***Requires technical review***         | *Implementation may affect security, source-chain integrity, permissions, or execution gates.*         |
| ***Requires public correction***        | *Communication created semantic, financial, ecological, or governance confusion requiring correction.* |

*The outcome must define scope.*

*An action may be admissible for research and non-admissible for investment.*

*A claim may be admissible as narrative and non-admissible as MRV evidence.*

*A token may be admissible for community access and non-admissible as PRU exposure.*

*A dataset may be admissible for learning and non-admissible for valuation.*

*This precision is essential.*

*Prometheus does not need fewer categories.*

*It needs cleaner categories.*

***10.10 AI and Constitutional Interpretation***

*AI may assist constitutional interpretation.*

*It may:*

*• summarize relevant provisions;  
• identify conflicts;  
• compare precedents;  
• map risks;  
• draft interpretive options;  
• classify issues;  
• flag category collapse;  
• support legal or MRV review;  
• generate decision memos for human review.*

*AI may not become constitutional authority.*

*AI may not issue final binding interpretations.*

*AI may not override the Access Covenant.*

*AI may not authorize PRU value.*

*AI may not transform narrative into evidence.*

*AI may not waive legal review.*

*AI may not bypass human dignity, ecological integrity, governance process, or source-layer constraints.*

*AI-assisted interpretation must remain:*

*• evidence-linked;  
• versioned;  
• reviewable;  
• auditable where appropriate;  
• bounded by role;  
• subject to human or governance authorization.*

*The rule is simple:*

*AI may help the constitution speak more clearly.*

*AI may not speak above the constitution.*

***10.11 Emergency Constitutional Interpretation***

*In urgent situations, a temporary constitutional interpretation may be necessary before full review is complete.*

*This may occur where delay could create:*

*• ecological harm;  
• investor-facing misrepresentation;  
• data manipulation;  
• technical compromise;  
• governance capture;  
• tokenomic confusion;  
• legal exposure;  
• public communication risk;  
• source-layer bypass.*

*Emergency interpretation should be:*

*• temporary;  
• narrow;  
• documented;  
• proportionate;  
• reviewable;  
• escalated to full review as soon as feasible.*

*Emergency interpretation may pause or restrict action.*

*It may not weaken Eternity Clauses.*

*It may not create permanent constitutional change.*

*It may not authorize what the source layer prohibits.*

*Emergency interpretation exists to protect the constitution, not to suspend it.*

***10.12 Final Interpretive Rule***

*The final interpretive rule is this:*

*interpretation clarifies;*

*it does not dominate.*

*review integrates;*

*it does not centralize sovereignty.*

*founding memory guides;*

*it does not rule.*

*science informs;*

*it does not replace constitutional admissibility.*

*AI assists;*

*it does not govern.*

*capital participates;*

*it does not command.*

*community matters;*

*it does not bypass dignity.*

*governance evolves;*

*it does not override life.*

*Prometheus remains valid only while constitutional interpretation preserves the relationship between life, dignity, evidence, governance, and regenerative fidelity.*

*Where ambiguity exists, interpret toward life.*

*Where power concentrates, interpret toward accountability.*

*Where claims inflate, interpret toward evidence.*

*Where speed accelerates, interpret toward reviewability.*

*Where systems diverge, interpret toward clear boundaries.*

*Where the future is affected, interpret as if future generations were already in the room.*

*The constitution is not a weapon held by one circle.*

*It is the root system of the whole architecture.*

*The interpreter is not sovereign.*

*The source is sovereign.*

***11. The Unamendable Declaration***

*Let this be clearly understood by all who seek to access, participate in, govern, finance, extend, automate, represent, or benefit from the h•eart•h Prometheus ecosystem.*

*The Eternity Clauses are not ordinary governance rules.*

*They are not policy preferences.*

*They are not strategic guidelines.*

*They are not symbolic statements.*

*They are not temporary commitments.*

*They are the constitutional recognition of living-system realities that precede and exceed any human institution.*

*They recognize that life, dignity, soil, water, biodiversity, ecological integrity, evidence, voluntary participation, regenerative responsibility, and the continuity of future generations are not created by governance.*

*They are the ground upon which governance becomes meaningful.*

*Prometheus does not make these principles true.*

*Prometheus chooses to become accountable to them.*

*This is the unamendable declaration.*

*The foundational articles may be clarified.*

*Governance procedures may evolve.*

*Implementation layers may mature.*

*Legal instruments may adapt to jurisdiction.*

*Technical systems may be upgraded.*

*Interfaces may improve.*

*Methodologies may be refined.*

*Translations may become clearer.*

*Operational procedures may change.*

*But the source-layer commitments protected by the Eternity Clauses may not be inverted, diluted, bypassed, repealed, suspended, tokenized away, voted away, automated away, financed away, or reinterpreted into their opposite.*

*No ordinary governance process may override them.*

*No constitutional amendment may weaken them.*

*No token vote may bypass them.*

*No investor requirement may subordinate them.*

*No legal wrapper may neutralize them.*

*No AI model may reinterpret them beyond the constitutional source layer.*

*No market opportunity may justify their erosion.*

*No emergency may suspend them.*

*No founder memory may own them.*

*No community identity may stand above them.*

*No institution may convert them into optional language.*

*The reason is simple.*

*The Eternity Clauses do not impose an ideology upon life.*

*They recognize the conditions through which life remains possible.*

*Soil does not become optional because a vote prefers liquidity.*

*Water does not become secondary because a model optimizes throughput.*

*Biodiversity does not become decorative because a financial structure simplifies risk.*

*Human dignity does not become negotiable because a community claims urgency.*

*Evidence does not become unnecessary because a narrative is beautiful.*

*Regeneration does not become valid because a token represents it.*

*A living system is not required to obey institutional convenience.*

*Institutions must learn to obey living-system reality.*
