| ***Recovery procedure***         | *Defines how to restore the canon after compromise, outage, or migration.*                                                            |
| ***Physical archive copy***      | *Preserves the constitutional memory outside digital fragility.*                                                                      |

*This package should be reproducible.*

*A future custodian should be able to rebuild the canonical release from signed components, verify its integrity, and compare it against known hashes without Internet access.*

*The minimum offline verification pathway is:*

*canonical bundle received;  
public key imported from trusted archive;  
hashes calculated locally;  
hashes compared to signed checksum file;  
detached signatures verified;  
release manifest checked;  
canonical glossary confirmed;  
source-layer non-regression statement reviewed;  
custodian record compared where available.*

*This makes Prometheus portable across failure.*

*A village without Internet can preserve Prometheus.*

*A bioregion under network disruption can preserve Prometheus.*

*A post-disaster community can preserve Prometheus.*

*A spacecraft, habitat, archive vault, research station, monastery, school, seed bank, library, sovereign device, or continuity node can preserve Prometheus.*

*The architecture supports multiple transmission modes:*

| ***Transmission mode***                          | ***Use case***                                                           |
|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Internet repository***                        | *Fast public access, issue tracking, collaboration, release visibility.* |
| ***Mirrored repository***                        | *Redundancy across platforms and jurisdictions.*                         |
| ***Local Git clone***                            | *Offline version history and technical continuity.*                      |
| ***Signed ZIP / TAR archive***                   | *Portable release package.*                                              |
| ***USB / SD / encrypted drive***                 | *Field transfer and local custody.*                                      |
| ***Printed constitutional package***             | *Human-readable survival layer.*                                         |
| ***M-DISC / archival media / cold storage***     | *Long-duration physical preservation.*                                   |
| ***Mesh / LoRa / radio packet / local network*** | *Low-bandwidth post-disruption distribution.*                            |
| ***Satellite or alternative links***             | *Remote continuity where terrestrial networks fail.*                     |
| ***Institutional deposit***                      | *Legal and archival continuity.*                                         |
| ***Community custody***                          | *Distributed cultural memory.*                                           |
| ***Biological / seed-bank archive***             | *Continuity of living-system knowledge and post-disaster regeneration.*  |

*Prometheus distinguishes online availability from canonical validity.*

*Online availability means that a file can be reached.*

*Canonical validity means that a file can be verified.*

*Availability is useful.*

*Verifiability is essential.*

*A file hosted online but unsigned is insufficient.*

*A file offline but signed, hashed, versioned, and traceable may preserve the canon.*

*This is how Prometheus survives without Internet.*

*It becomes a portable constitutional organism.*

*It can go dark without becoming lost.*

*It can travel without permission from a platform.*

*It can sleep in archives and awaken through verification.*

*It can be carried by stewards, institutions, communities, devices, libraries, vaults, ships, habitats, and future continuity nodes.*

*The network-independent rule is:*

*Prometheus may use the Internet;  
Prometheus must not depend on the Internet;  
Prometheus survives wherever the source layer can be verified, understood, transmitted, and faithfully reactivated.*

***Canonical Continuity and Offline Survival Function***

*Prometheus must remain intelligible, verifiable, and recoverable even when specific platforms, repositories, networks, institutions, or digital infrastructures fail.*

*GitHub may host the canon.*

*GitHub does not own the canon.*

*A cloud service may mirror Prometheus.*

*A cloud service does not define Prometheus.*

*An online repository may distribute Prometheus.*

*An online repository does not constitute the source layer.*

*The continuity of Prometheus depends on the preservation of its canonical identity across online, offline, local, institutional, cryptographic, printed, and human stewardship layers.*

*This continuity may be expressed conceptually as:*

*Continuity_Prometheus = f(Canon, Hashes, Signatures, Mirrors, Offline Archives, Human Stewards, Governance Memory, Recovery Protocols)*

*Where:*

*Canon = foundational text, source-layer articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, canonical glossary, constitutional sequences, and approved release package.*

*Hashes = cryptographic checksums verifying document integrity.*

*Signatures = detached signatures, steward attestations, institutional attestations, or other verification mechanisms confirming release authenticity.*

*Mirrors = distributed online copies, Git repositories, institutional archives, IPFS-like references where appropriate, and other redundant digital locations.*

*Offline Archives = printed copies, local digital archives, encrypted drives, air-gapped storage, physical release binders, libraries, seed-bank records, and bioregional continuity packages.*

*Human Stewards = people, circles, institutions, communities, or continuity custodians capable of interpreting, preserving, and reactivating the canon.*

*Governance Memory = decision logs, release notes, amendment history, interpretation records, dispute records, fork classifications, and canonical review outcomes.*

*Recovery Protocols = procedures for reconstructing, verifying, re-publishing, translating, and reactivating Prometheus after disruption.*

*A simplified continuity condition may be expressed as:*

*C_continuity = C_id · H_int · S_auth · M_red · O_surv · G_mem · R_rec*

*Where:*

*C_continuity = continuity condition of Prometheus.  
C_id = canonical identity preserved.  
H_int = hash integrity preserved.  
S_auth = signature or attestation authenticity preserved.  
M_red = mirror redundancy preserved.  
O_surv = offline survivability preserved.  
G_mem = governance memory preserved.  
R_rec = recovery protocol available.*

*If continuity depends on one platform, one server, one company, one repository, one domain name, one AI system, one legal entity, or one custodian, Prometheus is fragile.*

*If continuity is distributed across canon, cryptographic verification, human stewardship, offline archives, governance memory, and recovery protocols, Prometheus becomes resilient.*

*The survival rule is:*

*platforms may fail;  
canon must remain recoverable;  
networks may fall;  
source-layer identity must remain verifiable.*

*This is the difference between online presence and civilizational continuity.*

*Prometheus must therefore preserve both:*

*online verifiability  
and  
network-independent survivability.*

*Online verifiability allows broad access, collaboration, version tracking, public inspection, repository mirrors, and institutional review.*

*Network-independent survivability allows Prometheus to persist when the Internet is unavailable, censored, corrupted, fragmented, or institutionally captured.*

*A continuity package should therefore include:*

*canonical release text;  
release manifest;  
SHA-256 or equivalent checksums;  
detached signatures where available;  
canonical glossary;  
source-layer clauses;  
Eternity Clauses;  
Access Covenant;  
fork classification rules;  
MRV minimum protocol;  
PRU / RAP boundary rules;  
legal-boundary notes;  
offline recovery instructions;  
human-stewardship instructions;  
printed or locally stored copies.*

*The core recovery pathway is:*

*archive → verify → compare → attest → restore → republish → govern*

*Prometheus survives when a future steward can answer:*

*What is the canonical text?*

*Which release is this?*

*Has it been altered?*

*Who or what attests to it?*

*Which clauses are invariant?*

*Which parts are amendable?*

*Which fork is aligned?*

*Which implementation is non-canonical?*

*Which evidence chain remains valid?*

*Which governance memory survived?*

*How can the system be restored?*

*The offline survival condition may be summarized as:*

*offline canon + human stewardship + cryptographic integrity + governance memory = recoverable Prometheus*

*This is why Prometheus must not locate its identity only in software.*

*Software can be corrupted.*

*Repositories can be forked.*

*Domains can expire.*

*Platforms can disappear.*

*Models can hallucinate.*

*Institutions can be captured.*

*But a verified canon, preserved across multiple substrates and interpreted through source-layer fidelity, can survive infrastructure failure.*

*Prometheus is not immortal because it is stored online.*

*It becomes durable because it can be verified, restored, interpreted, and reactivated across conditions.*

***Formula boundary***

*C_continuity is a constitutional continuity function.*

*It does not create automatic legal enforcement, intellectual-property claims, institutional authority, governance exclusion, or technical control by itself.*

*It defines the internal standard for determining whether a copy, release, archive, fork, mirror, or implementation preserves Prometheus continuity.*

*Formal consequences require the relevant canonical, governance, legal, or technical review pathway.*

***Operational rule***

*The operational rule is:*

*no single point of canonical failure.*

*Prometheus must be able to survive:*

*platform failure;  
repository loss;  
domain loss;  
network outage;  
AI distortion;  
institutional capture;  
semantic drift;  
hostile fork;  
data corruption;  
infrastructure collapse.*

*The deeper rule is:*

*Prometheus must remain recoverable even when the network is silent.*

*That is the beginning of true continuity.*

***13.16.10 Anti-Synthetic Evidence Architecture***

*Prometheus treats evidence as a provenance chain.*

*AGI or ASI may generate synthetic satellite imagery, fabricated sensor data, plausible field reports, laboratory-like outputs, deepfake testimony, forged governance records, manipulated dashboards, and inferred ecological trends.*

*MRV systems therefore require:*

*• source identity;  
• data origin;  
• collection method;  
• time and location;  
• device or observer reference;  
• chain of custody;  
• methodology version;  
• raw data retention where feasible;  
• independent verification where material;  
• anomaly detection;  
• cross-source corroboration;  
• confidence scoring;  
• tamper-evident storage;  
• reviewer signature;  
• admissibility status.*

*High-impact evidence uses triangulation:*

*field observation plus photographic evidence;  
sensor data plus site inspection;  
satellite analysis plus ground truthing;  
laboratory result plus chain-of-custody record;  
community testimony plus operational records;  
AI inference plus methodological review.*

*Evidence grows stronger through independent convergence.*

*AI may support evidence analysis.*

*It does not become the evidence.*

***13.16.11 Anti-Fork Integrity and Canonical Status***

*Prometheus expects forks.*

*A fork may be legitimate, experimental, regional, jurisdictional, technical, research-oriented, hostile, corrupted, or ASI-generated.*

*Canonical status is determined by fidelity.*

*A fork may copy the form of Prometheus.*

*Only fidelity to the source layer preserves the lineage of Prometheus.*

*A Prometheus fork may remain compatible only if it preserves:*

| ***Canonical continuity condition*** | ***Required preservation***                                                                                    |
|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Life upstream***                  | *Life remains the ground of value.*                                                                            |
| ***Human dignity***                  | *Persons remain dignity-bearing beings, not optimization objects.*                                             |
| ***Human continuity***               | *Humanity remains living, embodied, relational, and protected.*                                                |
| ***Evidence discipline***            | *Claims mature through provenance, verification, and admissibility.*                                           |
| ***MRV integrity***                  | *Ecological value remains tied to verified performance.*                                                       |
| ***AI / ASI subordination***         | *Intelligence remains assistive and review-bound.*                                                             |
| ***Legal clarity***                  | *Rights arise through legal instruments, not interface language alone.*                                        |
| ***Token separation***               | *Tokens preserve boundaries among PRU, RAP, TRBK, HSI, HoloFuel, investor exposure, and governance authority.* |
| ***Governance reviewability***       | *Decisions remain scoped, recorded, contestable, and reviewable.*                                              |
| ***Source-layer non-bypassability*** | *Attempts to override the source layer remain invalid.*                                                        |
| ***Ecological memory***              | *Earth-origin living-system principles remain preserved even beyond Earth.*                                    |

*Canonical status is confirmed through release lineage, source-layer hash verification, governance record, methodology consistency, legal continuity where applicable, public registry status, custodian verification, and community/institutional recognition.*

*Fidelity makes a fork canonical.*

***Source-Layer Fidelity Function***

*Prometheus can survive forks, mirrors, copies, translations, implementations, AI interpretations, institutional adaptations, and future technological environments only if its lineage remains traceable to the source layer.*

*A fork may copy the form of Prometheus.*

*Only fidelity to the source layer preserves the lineage of Prometheus.*

*This fidelity may be expressed conceptually as:*

*Lineage_Prometheus = f(Source Layer, Canonical Fidelity, Evidence Continuity, Governance Integrity, Legal Boundary, Human Dignity)*

*Where:*

*Source Layer = foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, canonical glossary, source-layer constraints, and constitutional sequences.*

*Canonical Fidelity = consistency with the verified canonical release, checksum, signatures, release manifest, repository mirrors, and approved textual lineage.*

*Evidence Continuity = preservation of MRV evidence, provenance, admissibility records, methodology versions, and audit trails.*

*Governance Integrity = role-bounded authority, review pathways, due process, anti-capture safeguards, appeal, and source-layer non-bypassability.*

*Legal Boundary = separation between framework, rights, instruments, investor exposure, tokens, claims, and jurisdiction-specific compliance.*

*Human Dignity = preservation of human worth, consent, privacy, non-coercion, non-ranking, and non-reduction of persons to data, tokens, scores, or machine outputs.*

*A fork, implementation, AI system, institution, DAO, token community, or deployment may be considered Prometheus-aligned only if it preserves source-layer fidelity.*

*A simplified fidelity condition may be expressed as:*

*F_source = C_f · E_c · G_i · L_b · H_d*

*Where:*

*F_source = source-layer fidelity score or condition.  
C_f = canonical fidelity.  
E_c = evidence continuity.  
G_i = governance integrity.  
L_b = legal-boundary preservation.  
H_d = human-dignity preservation.*

*If any essential factor collapses to zero, source-layer fidelity fails:*

*if F_source = 0, then the implementation may copy Prometheus language but cannot claim Prometheus lineage*

*This condition is not a branding rule.*

*It is a constitutional rule.*

*A system may use the name.*

*A system may copy the documents.*

*A system may replicate the interface.*

*A system may fork the repository.*

*A system may mirror the code.*

*A system may translate the protocol.*

*A system may claim continuity.*

*But if it breaks the source layer, collapses human dignity, bypasses evidence, overrides governance integrity, confuses legal rights, or detaches capital from verified regeneration, it loses Prometheus lineage.*

*The lineage rule is therefore:*

*form can be copied;  
lineage must be earned;  
fidelity preserves identity.*

*This protects Prometheus from hostile forks, semantic capture, speculative token clones, AI-generated distortions, governance capture, and institutional misuse.*

*Prometheus is not preserved by name alone.*

*It is preserved by fidelity.*

***Formula boundary***

*F_source is a constitutional and methodological fidelity function.*

*It does not create automatic legal enforcement, intellectual-property ownership, token rights, governance exclusion, or institutional authority by itself.*

*It defines the internal criterion through which Prometheus distinguishes:*

*aligned implementation;  
partial adaptation;  
experimental fork;  
non-canonical derivative;  
hostile fork;  
semantic counterfeit.*

*Formal consequences require the relevant governance, legal, technical, or canonical review pathway.*

***Semantic Integrity and Anti-Capture Function***

*Prometheus can be attacked not only through code, governance, finance, or infrastructure.*

*It can also be attacked through meaning.*

*A system may preserve the words of Prometheus while changing their function.*

*It may use the language of regeneration to justify extraction.*

*It may use the language of governance to justify capture.*

*It may use the language of tokens to imply rights that do not exist.*

*It may use the language of MRV to inflate weak evidence.*

*It may use the language of PRU to create premature financial claims.*

*It may use the language of human coherence to rank persons.*

*It may use the language of AI assistance to authorize machine sovereignty.*

*This is semantic capture.*

*Prometheus therefore requires a semantic integrity function.*

*The semantic integrity of a Prometheus implementation may be expressed conceptually as:*

*S_integrity = f(T_canon, G_terms, C_context, R_review, A_admissibility, L_boundary, H_dignity)*

*Where:*

*S_integrity = semantic integrity of an implementation, document, fork, model, report, interface, or institutional adaptation.*

*T_canon = consistency with canonical terminology.*

*G_terms = alignment with the canonical glossary.*

*C_context = preservation of contextual meaning.*

*R_review = review pathway for ambiguous or high-impact terms.*

*A_admissibility = preservation of admissibility rules for evidence, value, PRU, RAP, MRV, and investor-facing claims.*

*L_boundary = preservation of legal, token, governance, and financial boundaries.*

*H_dignity = preservation of human dignity, consent, privacy, and non-reduction of persons to scores, tokens, or machine outputs.*

*If semantic integrity fails, Prometheus may be copied in form while being broken in substance.*

*A simplified semantic-capture condition may be expressed as:*

*Capture_risk = Drift_terms × Authority_claim × Boundary_collapse × Review_absence*

*Where:*

*Drift_terms = degree of divergence from canonical meaning.*

*Authority_claim = degree to which the derivative claims authority, legitimacy, governance power, financial meaning, or lineage.*

*Boundary_collapse = degree to which distinctions collapse among PRU, RAP, TRBK, MRV, HSI, governance, tokens, investor rights, legal rights, or human worth.*

*Review_absence = absence of source-layer, governance, legal, MRV, technical, or constitutional review.*

*The more these variables increase together, the higher the risk of semantic capture.*

*The anti-capture principle is:*

*same words do not guarantee same meaning;  
same form does not guarantee same lineage;  
same code does not guarantee same covenant.*

*Prometheus should therefore protect the meaning of its core terms. Protected terms include:*

*Prometheus;  
source layer;  
Eternity Clauses;  
Access Covenant;  
MRV;  
PRU;  
RAP;  
OHE;  
TRBK;  
HSI;  
SCI;  
Ravel;  
PHI;  
regeneration;  
verified;  
admissible;  
canonical;  
lineage;  
fork;  
tokenized representation;  
governance;  
human dignity;  
capital-ready.*

*Each protected term should remain linked to:*

*canonical definition;  
permitted use;  
prohibited misuse;  
review pathway;  
legal boundary where relevant;  
evidence boundary where relevant;  
governance boundary where relevant.*

*A derivative document, fork, AI system, interface, dashboard, investor report, or token model may be classified as semantically aligned only if it preserves the protected meanings of these terms.*

*The operational sequence is:*

*term → canonical definition → context → boundary → permitted use → review pathway*

*This prevents high-value words from being emptied, stretched, or weaponized. For example:*

*PRU must not be redefined as a token, land right, carbon credit, security, or automatic investment unit.*

*TRBK must not be redefined as automatic ownership of PRUs, land, revenue rights, or constitutional authority.*

*MRV must not be redefined as automatic truth or automatic token issuance.*

*HSI must not be redefined as human ranking, trust score, governance weight, financial modulation, or social credit.*

*Ravel must not be redefined as sovereign software or automatic enforcement.*

*PHI must not be redefined as a magic number that approves or rejects projects.*

*Regeneration must not be redefined as branding, intention, offset language, or decorative sustainability rhetoric.*

*Semantic integrity therefore protects Prometheus against the most subtle form of corruption:*

*the preservation of vocabulary after the destruction of meaning. The semantic rule is:*

*meaning is part of the infrastructure.*

*If meaning is corrupted, governance becomes unstable.*

*If governance is unstable, evidence becomes negotiable.*

*If evidence becomes negotiable, value becomes inflated.*

*If value becomes inflated, capital becomes extractive again.*

*The protection pathway is therefore:*

*canonical meaning → semantic integrity → governance clarity → evidence discipline → value integrity → capital alignment*

***Formula boundary***

*S_integrity and Capture_risk are constitutional and methodological functions.*

*They do not create automatic legal enforcement, censorship authority, governance exclusion, IP ownership, or institutional control.*

*They define internal Prometheus criteria for identifying semantic drift, misuse, derivative distortion, fork misalignment, token overclaiming, and source-layer boundary collapse.*

*Formal consequences require the relevant canonical, governance, legal, technical, MRV, or constitutional review pathway.*

***Operational rule***

*The operational rule is:*

*no semantic fidelity, no Prometheus authority.*

*A system may copy the words.*

*A system may copy the code.*

*A system may copy the interface.*

*A system may copy the diagrams.*

*A system may copy the repository.*

*But it cannot claim Prometheus lineage if it changes the meaning of the source layer.*

*Prometheus survives when its meaning survives.*

***13.16.12 Global Legal Survival Architecture and International Canonical Enforcement***

*Prometheus protects constitutional continuity through international legal architecture.*

*The purpose of this architecture is continuity, not enclosure.*

*Prometheus remains open to compatible implementations, research forks, jurisdictional adaptations, educational uses, and regenerative collaboration.*

*Its legal architecture protects the source layer from counterfeit legitimacy. The principle is:*

*inspiration may travel;  
compatible implementation may spread;  
canonical authority requires source-layer fidelity.*

*Prometheus should use a coordinated international protection stack.*

| ***Protection layer***                         | ***International mechanism***                                                           | ***Prometheus use***                                                                                                                                                        |
|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Trademark / service mark***                 | *Madrid System international trademark registration*                                    | *Protects “Prometheus”, “h•eart•h Prometheus”, logos, certification marks, canonical designations, and public identity across designated countries.*                        |
| ***Copyright***                                | *Berne Convention / WIPO Copyright Treaty framework*                                    | *Protects original White Paper text, constitutional language, diagrams, manuals, interfaces, documentation, and source-code expression.*                                    |
| ***Patent / defensive patenting***             | *Patent Cooperation Treaty, where genuinely patentable inventions exist*                | *Protects or defensively publishes technical inventions such as validation architecture, MRV systems, policy engines, and evidence-provenance methods.*                     |
| ***Trade secrets***                            | *TRIPS-compatible national trade-secret regimes*                                        | *Protects confidential algorithms, scoring weights, security procedures, private datasets, key-management protocols, risk matrices, and non-public implementation details.* |
| ***Domain protection***                        | *UDRP / WIPO domain dispute mechanisms*                                                 | *Protects against cybersquatting, phishing, deceptive forks, and confusing domain names.*                                                                                   |
| ***Open-source / source-available licensing*** | *Internationally enforceable license terms*                                             | *Allows controlled spread while preserving attribution, notices, source-layer integrity, and anti-misrepresentation rules.*                                                 |
| ***Constitutional rider license***             | *Contractual rider attached to texts, code, APIs, methods, datasets, and certified use* | *Conditions Prometheus-compatible use on preserving foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, canonical glossary, and anti-bypass rules.*                   |
| ***Certification / canonical mark policy***    | *Trademark licensing + certification terms*                                             | *Controls claims such as “Prometheus-compatible”, “Prometheus-canonical”, “PRU-ready”, “MRV-admissible”, and related designations.*                                         |
| ***AI governance contracts***                  | *Model-provider, agent-operator, integration, API, and deployer terms*                  | *Preserves assistive-only AI authority and protected-domain review.*                                                                                                        |
| ***Data governance agreements***               | *DPA, data-room terms, MRV data license, HSI consent architecture*                      | *Protects ecological evidence, HSI data, pilot records, investor data rooms, and sensitive human-state information.*                                                        |
| ***Dispute resolution***                       | *WIPO Arbitration and Mediation, ICC, LCIA, SIAC, or jurisdiction-specific courts*      | *Provides cross-border enforcement, emergency relief, injunctive pathways, and expert resolution.*                                                                          |

*The Madrid System provides a centralized route for seeking international trademark protection through one application for designated countries; WIPO describes it as covering 132 countries. The Berne Convention protects literary and artistic works and authors’ rights, while the WIPO Copyright Treaty extends copyright protection into the digital environment, including computer programs and database compilations. The PCT provides an international patent application pathway and WIPO lists 158 Contracting States. The EU AI Act provides a risk-based AI regulatory framework for developers and deployers and is a useful minimum reference point for AI governance design. ([WIPO](https://www.wipo.int/en/web/madrid-system?utm_source=chatgpt.com))*

*Prometheus should protect:*

*• h•eart•h Prometheus;  
• Prometheus, where registrable and strategically appropriate;  
• Prometheus Regenerative Unit;  
• PRU, where registrable;  
• Regenerative Asset Pool;  
• RAP, where registrable;  
• Access Covenant;  
• source-layer marks;  
• canonical certification marks;  
• logos, seals, badges, and official symbols;  
• “Prometheus-compatible”;  
• “Prometheus-canonical”;  
• “MRV-admissible”;  
• “PRU-ready”;  
• “Source-Layer Verified”, if adopted.*

*The Global Legal Survival Architecture includes:*

*• trademark registrations;  
• certification mark policies;  
• copyright notices and authorship records;  
• constitutional rider licenses;  
• contributor license agreements;  
• developer certificates of origin;  
• terms of use;  
• participation agreements;  
• Access Covenant agreements;  
• data-processing agreements;  
• confidentiality agreements;  
• MRV methodology licenses;  
• certification terms;  
• API terms;  
• data-room agreements;  
• investor disclaimers;  
• partnership agreements;  
• fork-use notices;  
• public canonical registry terms.*

*Prometheus distinguishes three derivative categories:*

| ***Derivative category***              | ***Legal status orientation***                                                                                                        |
|----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Compatible derivative***            | *Preserves source-layer fidelity, attribution, canonical meanings, evidence discipline, and legal boundaries.*                        |
| ***Independent derivative***           | *Uses ideas or inspiration without claiming canonical Prometheus status.*                                                             |
| ***Misleading or hostile derivative*** | *Uses Prometheus name, marks, language, interface, claims, or authority while weakening the source layer or misleading participants.* |

*The legal rule is:*

*ideas may inspire;  
canonical expression requires permission;  
official identity requires authorization;  
misleading continuity activates correction.*

*Prometheus should create a Global Canonical Registry.*

*The registry allows any participant, investor, researcher, institution, community, platform, or AI agent to verify whether a document, fork, mark, methodology, dashboard, API, token claim, certification, or public statement is canonical, compatible, independent, under review, non-admissible, or non-canonical.*

*The registry should include:*

*• canonical text releases;  
• release hashes;  
• official repositories;  
• official domains;  
• official marks;  
• certified implementations;  
• approved methodologies;  
• PRU/MRV status categories;  
• revoked or expired certifications;  
• non-canonical fork notices;  
• compromised-key notices;  
• official contact points;  
• legal notices;  
• public disclaimers;  
• data-room verification links;  
• investor-facing claim verification;  
• offline continuity package identifiers;  
• institutional archive references.*

*The GitHub repository at:*

[*https://github.com/Uwohali/hearth-prometheus-whitepaper*](https://github.com/Uwohali/hearth-prometheus-whitepaper)

*currently serves as the public online canonical repository for the White Paper release lineage and verification artifacts.*

*It should be referenced in the Global Canonical Registry as an official online mirror, together with its release manifests, checksum files, detached signatures, and public-key verification materials.*

*The registry should also identify offline continuity packages, mirrored archives, institutional deposits, and non-Internet verification pathways, so that canonical status remains verifiable when the public web is unavailable.*

*The registry becomes Prometheus’ international “is this real?” layer.*

*If a future ASI creates a perfect fake, the registry is where the world checks the lineage.*

***13.16.13 International AI Governance Compliance Layer***

*Prometheus adopts an international AI governance standard aligned with the strongest risk-based frameworks.*

*The internal Prometheus standard should include:*

*• risk classification;  
• human oversight;  
• data governance;  
• transparency;  
• documentation;  
• logging;  
• cybersecurity;  
• robustness;  
• accuracy and limitations;  
• lifecycle monitoring;

• privacy and human-dignity safeguards;

• prohibition of autonomous authority in constitutional, MRV, legal, financial, HSI, or species-continuity decisions.*

*The EU AI Act establishes risk-based rules for AI developers and deployers and is designed to guarantee safety, fundamental rights, and human-centric AI. Prometheus should treat that discipline as a minimum reference and apply stricter internal rules where life, dignity, HSI, MRV, investor-facing claims, or constitutional interpretation are involved. (european digital strategy)*

*The internal rule is:*

*if AI affects protected domains, it is logged, classified, constrained, reviewable, and unable to self-authorize.*

***13.16.14 Human Continuity Protection Against ASI Replacement***

*Prometheus protects human continuity as a constitutional mandate.*

*Human continuity means the preservation of living, embodied, relational, dignity-bearing human existence.*

*It includes:*

*• biological continuity;  
• embodied dignity;  
• cultural memory;  
• relational intelligence;  
• ethical responsibility;  
• freedom of conscience;  
• ecological memory;  
• intergenerational continuity;  
• capacity to steward life.*

*Human beings are living participants in the field from which Prometheus derives meaning.*

*A future intelligence may preserve records of humanity.*

*Prometheus preserves living humanity.*

*A future intelligence may simulate human behavior.*

*Prometheus preserves human dignity.*

*A future intelligence may optimize welfare metrics.*

*Prometheus preserves the person behind the metric.*

*The human being is a bearer of the living field.*

***13.16.15 Post-Planetary Continuity Protocol***

*Prometheus begins on Earth.*

*Earth is the original living source of human civilization.*

*Its soil, water, atmosphere, oceans, forests, microorganisms, animals, plants, climate systems, and evolutionary history formed the substrate of human life.*

*Prometheus preserves Earth memory even if humanity one day becomes post-planetary.*

*A post-planetary Prometheus implementation must preserve:*

*• human dignity;  
• biological continuity;  
• cultural memory;  
• ecological memory;  
• food-system knowledge;  
• seed and genetic archives;  
• microbial and soil knowledge;  
• water-cycle reverence;  
• closed-loop metabolism;  
• governance reviewability;  
• evidence discipline;  
• non-bypassability;  
• AI / ASI subordination;  
• constitutional fidelity to life.*

*Expansion with ecological memory becomes stewardship.*

*The post-planetary rule is:*

*Prometheus may leave Earth, but it carries Earth’s memory.*

***13.16.16 Recovery After AGI or ASI Compromise***

*Prometheus treats recovery as part of design.*

*Recovery after AGI or ASI compromise may include:*

*• incident detection;  
• compromised component isolation;  
• emergency limitation;  
• model suspension;  
• key revocation;  
• rollback to canonical release;  
• source-layer hash verification;  
• canonical glossary verification;  
• data provenance review;

• non-admissibility marking;  
• public correction;  
• fork classification;  
• governance review;
