| ***Soil respiration or CO₂ sensors*** | *CO₂ flux or microbial activity proxy.*                         | *Soil biological activity research or advanced monitoring.*      |
| ***Manual field instruments***        | *Infiltration rings, penetrometers, soil sampling kits.*        | *Low-tech, network-independent, field-verifiable evidence.*      |

*Each sensor record should include:*

*• device identity;  
• location;  
• timestamp;  
• measured variable;  
• unit;  
• calibration status;  
• firmware or configuration version where relevant;  
• data-quality flag;  
• method reference;  
• source-chain or archive reference where implemented.*

*Sensor records are evidence inputs.*

*They are not automatic truth.*

*They require quality checks, calibration, context, and where material, cross-source validation.*

***G.5.3 Laboratory evidence layer***

*Laboratory evidence provides high-quality analytical support for indicators that require controlled measurement.*

*Relevant laboratory data may include:*

*• soil organic carbon;  
• soil organic matter;  
• pH;  
• electrical conductivity;  
• bulk density;  
• aggregate stability;  
• nutrient availability;  
• microbial biomass;  
• mycorrhizal colonization where feasible;  
• soil texture;  
• salinity;  
• water quality;  
• plant tissue analysis;  
• nutrient density indicators where relevant.*

*Laboratory records should include:*

*• sample ID;  
• sampling date;  
• sampling location;  
• sampling depth;  
• collector identity or role;  
• chain-of-custody note;  
• laboratory identity;  
• analytical method;  
• results;  
• uncertainty or detection limits where available;  
• report hash or reference;  
• methodology version.*

*Laboratory data may serve as calibration anchors for field sensing, remote sensing, soil interpretation, and PRU-relevant analysis.*

***G.5.4 Structured field observation layer***

*Human observation remains essential.*

*The previous label “proof of care” should be replaced by:*

*Stewardship Observation Record*

*A Stewardship Observation Record is a structured, time-stamped, georeferenced, evidence-linked observation submitted by a steward, community member, auditor, verifier, field operator, or authorized observer.*

*It may include:*

*• planting event;  
• pruning event;  
• biomass return;  
• compost application;  
• mulching;  
• irrigation event;  
• pest or disease observation;  
• biodiversity sighting;  
• erosion observation;  
• water-retention observation;  
• crop yield record;  
• mortality or replacement record;  
• field photograph;  
• field note;  
• disturbance report;  
• corrective action.*

*A Stewardship Observation Record should include:*

*• observer identity or role;  
• timestamp;  
• location;  
• event type;  
• description;  
• photo or supporting evidence where possible;  
• linked OHE / EPU / site reference;  
• relevant methodology version;  
• confidence or review status;  
• reviewer reference where applicable.*

*Human observation is not excluded from MRV.*

*It is structured.*

*This allows local intelligence to become reviewable evidence without pretending that unverified testimony is equivalent to validated ecological performance.*

***G.6 HSI boundary inside MRV***

*HSI data should not be treated as MRV evidence for ecological performance.*

*HSI may support optional, voluntary, privacy-first human-state research, reflective governance, stewardship training, or decision-quality inquiry.*

*HSI must not determine:*

*• PRU value;  
• MRV validity;  
• ecological truth;  
• investor eligibility;  
• token rights;  
• governance authority by itself;  
• human worth;  
• stewardship credibility as a standalone score.*

*Where HSI appears in MRV-adjacent contexts, it should be classified as:*

*human-infrastructure research context, not ecological evidence.*

*The correct boundary is:*

*human-state data may inform participation context;  
participation context may inform reflective governance;  
reflective governance may support stewardship quality;  
only ecological and operational evidence may support MRV or PRU pathways.*

*Therefore, HSI devices, HRV tools, or coherence indicators should not appear as core MRV sensors for ecological validation.*

*They may be referenced in separate HSI, research, governance, or training appendices under strict consent and privacy rules.*

***G.7 Anti-synthetic evidence controls***

*Prometheus must assume that future AI, AGI, or ASI systems may generate synthetic evidence.*

*Synthetic evidence risks include:*

*• fabricated satellite imagery;  
• manipulated drone imagery;  
• forged sensor streams;  
• artificial laboratory reports;  
• deepfake field audits;  
• fake community testimony;  
• manipulated dashboards;  
• forged governance records;  
• synthetic biodiversity observations;  
• generated soil or water data;  
• fake release manifests.*

*MRV therefore treats evidence as a provenance chain.*

*High-impact evidence should require:*

*• source identity;  
• method;  
• time and location;  
• chain of custody;  
• raw data retention where feasible;  
• cross-source corroboration;  
• anomaly detection;  
• reviewer signature;  
• confidence scoring;  
• admissibility status;  
• tamper-evident storage or hash reference.*

*The MRV rule is:*

*no single synthetic channel becomes sovereign evidence.*

*Evidence becomes stronger through independent convergence.*

***G.8 Data provenance schema***

*A generic MRV record should preserve the following fields where relevant:*

*{*

*"record_id": "mrv_record_0001",*

*"site_id": "ohe_or_site_reference",*

*"event_type": "soil_sample \| sensor_reading \| satellite_observation \| field_observation \| lab_result \| audit_note",*

*"observed_at_utc": "2026-01-01T10:00:00Z",*

*"location": {*

*"latitude": 0.0,*

*"longitude": 0.0,*

*"accuracy_m": 5*

*},*

*"source": {*

*"source_type": "sensor \| lab \| satellite \| human_observer \| auditor \| external_dataset",*

*"source_id": "source_identifier",*

*"method_version": "method_v1.0"*

*},*

*"measurement": {*

*"variable": "soil_organic_carbon",*

*"value": 0.0,*

*"unit": "%",*

*"uncertainty": null*

*},*

*"provenance": {*

*"raw_data_reference": "uri_or_archive_reference",*

*"hash": "sha256_hash_where_available",*

*"chain_of_custody": "reference_or_summary",*

*"methodology_reference": "mrv_method_v1.0"*

*},*

*"quality": {*

*"calibration_status": "current \| expired \| not_applicable",*

*"quality_flag": "valid \| review_required \| suspect \| invalid",*

*"confidence": 0.0*

*},*

*"classification": {*

*"evidence_status": "observation",*

*"admissibility_status": "not_yet_admissible",*

*"review_status": "pending"*

*}*

*}*

*This schema is illustrative.*

*The operative schema should be versioned, tested, and adapted to the relevant data type.*

***G.9 Hashing, storage, and source-chain discipline***

*Prometheus may use Holochain-compatible infrastructure, signed records, local-first data, distributed references, or other appropriate systems to preserve evidence integrity.*

*The correct implementation pattern is:*

*• raw data may remain in appropriate storage depending on size, sensitivity, law, and operational need;  
• hashes may be recorded to prove integrity;  
• metadata may be stored as structured records;  
• source-chain entries may record who submitted or validated an event;  
• DHT references may support availability and distributed verification where implemented;  
• sensitive data may remain encrypted, minimized, local, or access-controlled.*

*Prometheus should avoid overclaiming that all MRV data is always stored on-chain or always hashed to the DHT in every implementation.*

*The stronger formulation is:*

*MRV records should be tamper-evident, provenance-linked, versioned, and reviewable according to implementation maturity and data sensitivity.*

*Large files, sensitive records, HSI-adjacent data, private field data, or investor data-room materials may require off-chain or access-controlled storage with hash references and audit logs.*

***G.10 Standards-based interoperability***

*Prometheus is designed as an interoperable MRV and regenerative intelligence environment.*

*It should use open, widely recognized, or widely adopted standards where appropriate, especially when dealing with environmental observations, sensor records, geospatial assets, provenance, agent identity, credentials, device metadata, supply-chain events, and resource-event-agent accounting.*

*The purpose of interoperability is not to make every data stream automatically valid.  
The purpose is to allow heterogeneous evidence to be structured, compared, traced, reviewed, and, where appropriate, connected to Holochain-compatible agent-centric records and Valueflows-compatible event logic.*

*Prometheus is designed for heterogeneous data interoperability.*

*Weather, soil, hydrological, biological, chemical, spectral, luminosity, sensor, laboratory, drone, satellite, NDVI-derived, field-observation, community-observation, operational, and external-dataset records may all enter the Prometheus MRV environment when relevant.*

*However, ingestion does not equal validation.*

*Each record must remain associated, where applicable, with:*

- *source identity;*

- *method version;*

- *time;*

- *location;*

- *measurement unit;*

- *uncertainty;*

- *provenance;*

- *raw data reference;*

- *calibration status;*

- *quality status;*

- *confidence score;*

- *review status;*

- *evidence classification;*

- *admissibility classification.*

*No data stream becomes sovereign evidence by itself.*

*Satellite indices, AI-derived indicators, sensor outputs, laboratory results, field observations, community observations, external datasets, or operational records may strengthen a claim, but they do not automatically create ecological truth, verified regeneration, PRU value, RAP eligibility, legal admissibility, or capital-facing validity.*

*Evidence becomes stronger through independent convergence across multiple sources, methodological transparency, provenance discipline, review processes, and alignment with the Prometheus canonical glossary, MRV methodology, evidence classification, and admissibility rules.*

*Potential standards and interoperability logics include:*

| ***Standard / logic***                          | ***Use within Prometheus***                                                                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***OGC SensorThings API***                      | *Sensor observations, IoT data interoperability, field-device data exchange, and structured environmental observations.*                    |
| ***OGC API Environmental Data Retrieval***      | *Access and retrieval of environmental data, including weather, hydrological, climate, and geospatial environmental records.*               |
| ***STAC***                                      | *Cataloguing geospatial, satellite, drone, raster, and remote-sensing assets.*                                                              |
| ***Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF***                   | *Efficient storage, retrieval, and analysis of geospatial raster data, including satellite-derived layers.*                                 |
| ***W3C PROV***                                  | *Provenance representation, chain-of-custody logic, source attribution, and evidence lineage.*                                              |
| ***Web of Things descriptions***                | *Device metadata, sensor descriptions, field-device interoperability, and machine-readable device capabilities.*                            |
| ***EPCIS-compatible logic***                    | *Supply-chain, product, custody, transformation, movement, and event traceability where appropriate.*                                       |
| ***Valueflows / REA logic***                    | *Resources, events, agents, processes, commitments, claims, and regenerative accounting.*                                                   |
| ***DIDs / verifiable credentials***             | *Identity, authorization, roles, credentials, Digital Guarantor status, auditor identity, and permissioned attestations where appropriate.* |
| ***Holochain-compatible source-chain records*** | *Agent-centric event records, stewardship actions, evidence hashes, governance actions, and local-first trust architecture.*                |

*Standards support interoperability.*

*They do not replace methodology.*

*They do not replace MRV.*

*They do not replace governance.*

*They do not replace source-layer integrity.*

*They do not replace legal review.*

*They do not create financial admissibility.*

*They do not create PRU or RAP value by themselves.*

*The meaning of each record remains governed by the Prometheus canonical glossary, MRV methodology, evidence classification, review pathway, admissibility rules, and governance boundaries.*

*The operational principle is:*

*Prometheus can listen to many data streams, but it only trusts what can be traced, reviewed, triangulated, and admitted.*

***G.11 Indicator families***

*The MRV system may organize indicators into families.*

***G.11.1 Soil function indicators***

*Possible indicators:*

*• soil organic carbon;  
• soil organic matter;  
• aggregate stability;  
• infiltration capacity;  
• bulk density;  
• microbial biomass;  
• earthworm activity;  
• pH;  
• salinity;  
• nutrient availability;  
• root health;  
• soil moisture dynamics.*

*Purpose:*

*to evaluate whether the soil is becoming more fertile, structured, biologically active, water-retentive, and resilient.*

***G.11.2 Hydrological indicators***

*Possible indicators:*

*• infiltration rate;  
• soil moisture stability;  
• water retention after rainfall;  
• runoff reduction;  
• irrigation requirement per unit output;  
• water quality;  
• water-level response in swales or micro-basins;  
• vegetation response after rainfall or drought.*

*Purpose:*

*to evaluate whether the site is improving water capture, storage, cycling, and resilience.*

***G.11.3 Biodiversity indicators***

*Possible indicators:*

*• species richness;  
• functional diversity;  
• pollinator presence;  
• habitat continuity;  
• canopy and understory structure;  
• beneficial insect presence;  
• bird or wildlife observations;  
• biodiversity corridor contribution;  
• keystone or indicator species presence where relevant.*

*Purpose:*

*to evaluate whether the system is increasing ecological function, redundancy, and resilience.*

***G.11.4 Productive and operational indicators***

*Possible indicators:*

*• edible yield;  
• crop diversity;  
• biomass returned to soil;  
• seasonal yield distribution;  
• yield stability;  
• mortality or replacement rate;  
• input dependency;  
• labor and maintenance records;  
• operational interventions;  
• revenue or substitution value where relevant.*

*Purpose:*

*to evaluate whether regenerative performance is linked to productive and operational viability.*

***G.11.5 Resilience and risk indicators***

*Possible indicators:*

*• output variance;  
• drought response;  
• pest or disease pressure;  
• recovery after disturbance;  
• input dependency reduction;  
• water stress response;  
• management reliability;  
• evidence continuity;  
• adverse event history.*

*Purpose:*

*to evaluate risk compression and system maturity.*

***G.12 Confidence scoring***

*Each indicator should carry a confidence score.*

*Confidence scoring may consider:*

*• measurement quality;  
• sampling frequency;  
• spatial resolution;  
• temporal continuity;  
• calibration status;  
• source reliability;  
• replication;  
• cross-source consistency;  
• reviewer confidence;  
• uncertainty;  
• methodology maturity;  
• third-party verification where relevant.*

*A simplified conceptual model:*

*Confidence = measurement_quality × provenance_strength × cross_source_consistency × methodology_maturity*

*Confidence scoring prevents weak evidence from being treated as strong evidence.*

*It allows learning while protecting financial admissibility.*

***MRV Confidence and Evidence Maturity Function***

*Prometheus treats evidence maturity as a central condition for regenerative value.*

*An observation becomes useful when it is recorded.*

*It becomes evidence when it has provenance.*

*It becomes reliable when it is methodologically interpreted.*

*It becomes admissible when it satisfies confidence, review, and boundary conditions.*

*The MRV confidence function may be expressed as:*

*MRV_conf = f(Q_m, P_s, T_c, X_s, R_r, M_m, U_d)*

*Where:*

*Q_m = measurement quality.  
P_s = provenance strength.  
T_c = temporal continuity.  
X_s = cross-source consistency.  
R_r = reviewer reliability or review status.  
M_m = methodology maturity.  
U_d = uncertainty disclosure.*

*A simplified multiplicative confidence condition may be expressed as:*

*Conf_MRV = Q_m · P_s · T_c · X_s · R_r · M_m · U_d*

*This formulation is deliberately conservative.*

*If one essential evidence dimension is weak, total confidence should decrease.*

*A strong measurement with weak provenance remains limited.*

*A repeated observation without methodology remains limited.*

*A good dataset without uncertainty disclosure remains limited.*

*A compelling ecological claim without review remains limited.*

*Prometheus therefore does not treat evidence as a binary object.*

*Evidence has maturity.*

*Evidence has confidence.*

*Evidence has boundaries.*

*Evidence has permitted uses.*

*A second expression may classify evidence maturity as a pathway:*

*Observation → Evidence → Validated Indicator → MRV-Admissible Evidence → PRU-Eligible Evidence → Capital-Relevant Claim*

*This pathway prevents premature conversion of ecological signals into financial meaning.*

*Each stage has a different use.*

*An observation may support learning.*

*Evidence may support review.*

*A validated indicator may support reporting.*

*MRV-admissible evidence may support formal MRV.*

*PRU-eligible evidence may support PRU analysis.*

*A capital-relevant claim requires admissibility, legal structure, disclosure, and governance review.*

*The evidence maturity condition may therefore be expressed as:*

*Use_i ≤ Maturity_i*

*This means that a claim cannot be used beyond its evidence maturity.*

*A research-stage observation cannot be treated as PRU value.*

*A pilot indicator cannot be treated as investor-ready performance.*

*A dashboard signal cannot be treated as legal entitlement.*

*A community report cannot be treated as financial proof unless it satisfies the required evidence, provenance, confidence, and review pathway.*

*The operational rule is:*

*evidence use must not exceed evidence maturity.*

*This is one of the most important safeguards in Prometheus.*

*It allows the system to learn early without overclaiming early.*

*It allows pilots to generate insight without pretending to be institutional assets.*

*It allows emerging ecological intelligence to mature into capital relevance only when confidence, admissibility, and legal structure are ready.*

***Formula boundary***

*MRV_conf, Conf_MRV, and Use_i ≤ Maturity_i are methodological evidence-quality functions.*

*They do not create automatic MRV admissibility, PRU value, RAP inclusion, investor rights, legal enforceability, token issuance, credit issuance, or market value.*

*They support evidence classification, confidence scoring, review routing, PRU analysis, PHI interpretation, RAP screening, investor reporting, and capital-readiness review.*

***Operational rule***

*The operational rule is:*

*no maturity, no admissibility;  
no confidence, no capital-facing claim.*

*Prometheus may observe broadly.*

*Prometheus may learn continuously.*

*Prometheus may model ambitiously.*

*But it admits value conservatively.*

*This is how Prometheus remains scientific, auditable, and institutionally credible.*

***G.13 PRU relevance and admissibility boundary***

*MRV evidence may support PRU analysis only when it satisfies the relevant methodology.*

*The PRU pathway is:*

*observation  
evidence  
validated indicator  
MRV-admissible evidence  
PRU-eligible evidence  
PRU analysis  
PRU maturity classification support  
RAP eligibility where applicable.*

*No MRV record should automatically generate:*

*• token issuance;*

*• credit issuance;*

*• investor claim;*

*• governance authority;*

*• public financial statement;*

*•financial instrument status.*

*The correct rule is: MRV supports PRU only through admissibility.*

*Admissibility requires method, evidence, confidence, review, and legal clarity where value is being represented.*

***G.14 Dashboard and reporting boundaries***

*Dashboards may present:*

*• field observations;  
• sensor data;  
• MRV indicators;  
• confidence scores;  
• evidence maturity status;  
• trends;  
• baselines;  
• deltas;  
• risk flags;  
• review status;  
• PRU-relevant analysis where approved;  
• reporting limitations.*

*Dashboards should distinguish clearly between:*

*• live raw data;  
• preliminary observations;  
• validated indicators;  
• MRV-admissible evidence;  
• PRU-eligible evidence;  
• investor-review material;  
• financially admissible value.*

*A dashboard is an interface.*

*It is not an authority layer.*

*It may display evidence status.*

*It may not convert evidence status into financial authority without the required methodology, review, and legal structure.*

***Dashboard-to-Authority Boundary Function***

*A dashboard is an interface.*

*It is not an authority layer.*

*Prometheus dashboards, reports, maps, indices, risk panels, PRU previews, PHI views, Ravel signals, investor interfaces, or governance displays may help participants interpret evidence, trends, risks, baselines, deltas, confidence scores, and maturity status.*

*However, displaying information does not authorize action.*

*The dashboard boundary may be expressed as:*

*Displayed Data ≠ Authorized Decision*

*A more complete formulation is:*

*Authority_action = f(Evidence, Methodology, Review, Governance, Legal Boundary, Source Layer)*

*not:*

*Authority_action = f(Dashboard Output)*

*This distinction is essential.*

*A dashboard may show a trend.*

*A dashboard may show a risk flag.*

*A dashboard may show evidence maturity.*

*A dashboard may show a PRU-relevant indicator.*

*A dashboard may show a PHI score.*

*A dashboard may show a Ravel risk signal.*

*A dashboard may show confidence.*

*A dashboard may show uncertainty.*

*But a dashboard does not independently create:*

*PRU value;  
RAP eligibility;  
token issuance;  
credit issuance;  
investor entitlement;  
legal rights;  
governance authority;  
human ranking;  
public financial statements;  
automatic enforcement;  
capital-facing claims.*

*The correct operational pathway is:*

*dashboard signal → evidence review → methodology check → confidence assessment → admissibility review → governance / legal review where relevant → authorized action*

*This protects Prometheus from interface capture.*

*Interface capture occurs when a visual layer begins to behave as if it were the decision layer.*

*A score becomes a command.*

*A risk signal becomes a penalty.*

*A chart becomes a claim.*

*A dashboard becomes a judge.*

*Prometheus must prevent this.*

*Dashboards should therefore classify every displayed item according to permitted use:*

*raw data;  
preliminary observation;  
validated indicator;  
MRV-admissible evidence;  
PRU-eligible evidence;  
investor-review material;  
financially admissible value;  
non-admissible signal;  
research-use only;  
review-required signal.*

*The dashboard authority condition may be expressed as:*

*D_auth = E_s · M_s · C_s · A_s · G_s · L_s*

*Where:*

*D_auth = dashboard-supported authorization condition.  
E_s = evidence status sufficient.  
M_s = methodology status sufficient.  
C_s = confidence status sufficient.  
A_s = admissibility status sufficient.  
G_s = governance review satisfied where required.  
L_s = legal review or legal boundary satisfied where required.*

*If any required factor is zero, the dashboard output may remain informative, but it should not become authority:*

*if D_auth = 0, then dashboard output is non-authorizing*

*This does not reduce the usefulness of dashboards.*

*It makes them institutionally credible.*

*A dashboard can accelerate insight.*

*It can support review.*

*It can help participants see patterns.*

*It can make risk visible.*

*It can improve governance preparation.*

*It can help capital inspect evidence.*

*But it cannot substitute for the review pathway that gives information legitimate use.*

*The operational rule is:*

*interface supports interpretation;  
review creates admissibility;  
governance creates authorization;  
law creates enforceability.*

*This is especially important for investor-facing dashboards.*

*Investor-facing dashboards must distinguish clearly between:*

*observed data;  
verified data;  
methodology-bound analysis;  
scenario modeling;  
capital-readiness review;  
legally structured rights;  
non-offering information;  
public impact communication.*

*A dashboard that fails to distinguish these layers can accidentally transform information into an implied offer, implied valuation, implied guarantee, implied rating, or implied right.*

*Prometheus therefore treats dashboard language, labels, legends, colors, badges, icons, and status indicators as part of institutional risk management.*

*A green signal should not mean “investment approved.”*

*A yellow signal should not mean “automatic restriction.”*

*A red signal should not mean “automatic penalty.”*

*A PRU preview should not mean “PRU issued.”*

*A PHI score should not mean “project approved.”*

*A Ravel signal should not mean “enforcement triggered.”*

*The correct interpretation is always bounded:*

*displayed status → review pathway → authorized meaning*

*This is how Prometheus prevents visual intelligence from becoming visual overclaiming.*

***Formula boundary***

*Displayed Data, Authority_action, and D_auth are methodological and governance-boundary expressions.*

*They do not create automatic legal enforceability, governance decision, PRU value, RAP inclusion, investor right, token issuance, ecological credit, public financial claim, or enforcement action.*

*They define the internal Prometheus rule for separating interface visibility from authorized use.*

*Formal consequences require the relevant MRV, governance, legal, technical, capital-readiness, or source-layer review pathway.*

***Operational rule***

*The operational rule is:*

*a dashboard may reveal;  
it may not rule.*

*Prometheus dashboards should make regeneration more visible.*

*They should not turn visibility into authority without evidence, review, governance, and law.*

***G.15 Stewardship Observation Record***

*The Stewardship Observation Record replaces the earlier “proof of care” terminology.*

*A Stewardship Observation Record is a structured field record documenting real actions, observations, or conditions.*

*It may be submitted by:*

*• Earth Custodians;  
• field operators;  
• community members;  
• auditors;  
• verifiers;  
• researchers;  
• protocol stewards;  
• authorized institutional partners.*

*A record may include:*

| ***Field***                 | ***Purpose***                                          |
|-----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Observer role***         | *Identifies who submitted the record.*                 |
| ***Timestamp***             | *Places the record in time.*                           |
| ***Location***              | *Links the record to site, OHE, EPU, or coordinate.*   |
| ***Event type***            | *Classifies the action or observation.*                |
| ***Evidence attachment***   | *Photographs, documents, sensor references, or notes.* |
| ***Methodology reference*** | *Connects record to MRV protocol.*                     |
| ***Review status***         | *Pending, reviewed, validated, rejected, archived.*    |
| ***Confidence note***       | *Indicates quality or limitations.*                    |

*Stewardship records can strengthen MRV when corroborated.*

*They are not standalone financial proof.*

*They are structured human evidence.*

***G.16 Knowledge sources and regenerative intelligence***

*The MRV protocol may draw from:*

*• peer-reviewed ecological science;  
• soil science;  
• agroecology;  
• hydrology;  
• biodiversity science;  
• syntropic agroforestry field knowledge;  
• indigenous and traditional ecological knowledge where consented and properly governed;  
• open climate and soil datasets;  
• public environmental datasets;  
• institutional reports;  
• pilot-generated data;  
• third-party audits;  
• field experience.*

*Traditional, indigenous, local, and community knowledge must be handled with consent, attribution, cultural respect, and data-governance boundaries.*

*Knowledge may inspire design.*

*Evidence determines admissibility.*

***G.17 MRV governance and review***

*MRV governance should define:*

*• methodology approval;  
• indicator selection;  
• sampling protocols;  
• data-quality thresholds;  
• confidence scoring;  
• reviewer roles;  
• third-party verification triggers;  
• dispute resolution;  
• correction process;  
• adverse event reporting;  
• version updates;  
• retirement of obsolete indicators;  
• admission into PRU analysis.*

*MRV methodology should be versioned.*

*Every material report should reference the methodology version used.*

*Changes to methodology should be documented and should not silently alter historical interpretation.*

***G.18 MRV correction and non-admissibility***

*MRV records may be corrected, downgraded, quarantined, or classified as non-admissible.*

*Reasons may include:*

*• missing provenance;  
• weak method;  
• expired calibration;  
• contradictory evidence;  
• suspected manipulation;  
• synthetic evidence risk;  
• chain-of-custody failure;  
• incorrect location;  
• data corruption;  
• methodological mismatch;  
• privacy or consent violation;  
• legal restriction;  
• duplicate or double-counted claim.*

*Non-admissibility does not always mean deletion.*

*A non-admissible record may remain archived for learning, audit, dispute resolution, or research, but it may not enter protected MRV, PRU, RAP, investor-facing, or financial pathways.*

***G.19 MRV and legal boundary***

*MRV outputs may have legal, financial, ecological, or public-communication consequences.*

*Therefore, MRV reporting must respect:*

*• data rights;  
• privacy;  
• consent;  
• intellectual property;  
• public claims rules;  
• environmental claim standards;  
• investor disclosure boundaries;  
• jurisdictional review;  
• anti-greenwashing discipline;  
• no-double-counting requirements.*

*MRV evidence does not automatically create rights.*

*Rights arise through legal instruments.*

*MRV supports factual basis.*

*Legal structure defines enforceability.*

***G.20 Final MRV principle***

*The final MRV principle is:*

*observe living systems with humility;  
record evidence with discipline;  
preserve provenance with rigor;  
classify claims with precision;  
admit value conservatively;  
report boundaries transparently;  
and keep financial meaning downstream from verified reality.*

*MRV is the bridge between life and capital.*

*Its purpose is not to make life obey finance.*

*Its purpose is to make finance accountable to life.*

# ***Appendix H: AI-Assisted Learning, Model Evaluation, and Review-Bound Improvement Framework***

***H.1 Purpose and boundary***

*Prometheus may use artificial intelligence to support research, drafting, classification, evidence analysis, risk detection, governance preparation, technical specification, MRV review, and operational learning.*

*AI may help Prometheus learn.*

*AI may help Prometheus observe.*

*AI may help Prometheus compare.*

*AI may help Prometheus classify.*

*AI may help Prometheus detect inconsistency.*

*AI may help Prometheus draft structured outputs.*

*AI may help Prometheus improve its tools.*

*However, AI-assisted learning must remain bounded by the source layer.*

*The purpose of this appendix is to define how specialist agents, fine-tuned models, local models, evaluation pipelines, prompt systems, and learning loops may be developed without allowing model outputs to become sovereign authority. The core rule is:*

*AI may improve Prometheus’ capacity to reason, classify, summarize, and detect risk;  
AI may not autonomously authorize protected-domain execution.*

*Protected domains include:*

*• constitutional interpretation;  
• Access Covenant modification;  
• Eternity Clause preservation;  
• MRV admissibility;  
• PRU value analysis;  
• RAP inclusion;  
• investor-facing communication;  
• legal rights;  
• token issuance or token rights;  
• HSI human-state interpretation;  
• governance authority;  
• emergency controls;  
• technical administration;  
• data-room access;  
• canonical releases;  
• human continuity;  
• species-level decisions;  
• post-planetary continuity pathways.*

*In these domains, AI outputs are assistive, classified, logged, reviewable, and subject to the applicable human, governance, legal, technical, MRV, or constitutional pathway.*

*Prometheus therefore uses AI as intelligence support, not institutional sovereignty.*

*AI Assistance Boundary Function*

*Prometheus may use artificial intelligence as an intelligence-support layer.*

*AI may assist research.*

*AI may assist evidence review.*

*AI may assist semantic checking.*

*AI may assist MRV classification.*

*AI may assist governance preparation.*

*AI may assist legal issue spotting.*

*AI may assist risk detection.*

*AI may assist continuity verification.*

*AI may assist technical specification.*

*AI may assist scenario modeling.*

*However, AI assistance must remain distinct from institutional authority. The AI boundary may be expressed as:*

*AI_output ≠ Authority*

*This means that an AI-generated output is not, by itself, a governance decision, legal opinion, MRV determination, PRU value analysis, token authorization, investor approval, human assessment, or canonical interpretation. A more complete formulation is:*

*Authority = f(Evidence, Methodology, Governance Review, Legal Review, Source Layer, Human Accountability)*

*not:*

*Authority = f(AI_output)*

*This distinction is structural.*

*AI may generate a draft.*

*AI may identify a risk.*

*AI may compare documents.*

*AI may flag semantic drift.*

*AI may detect inconsistency.*

*AI may propose a pathway.*

*AI may simulate a scenario.*

*AI may classify evidence for review.*

*But protected-domain authority requires the relevant review pathway.*

*Protected domains include:*

*constitutional interpretation;  
source-layer fidelity;  
Eternity Clauses;  
Access Covenant;  
canonical glossary;  
MRV admissibility;  
PRU value analysis;  
RAP inclusion;  
legal rights;  
investor-facing materials;  
token issuance or token rights;  
governance authority;  
HSI interpretation;  
human dignity;  
data-room access;  
canonical releases;  
fork classification;  
continuity recovery;  
post-planetary survival pathways.*

*The operational AI pathway is:*

*AI output → classification → source check → risk check → protected-domain check → review pathway → authorization where required*

*This sequence prevents machine intelligence from becoming institutional sovereignty.*

*An AI system may be more capable than human operators in pattern recognition, memory, speed, synthesis, or simulation.*

*That does not make it constitutionally sovereign.*

*Capacity is not legitimacy.*

*The legitimacy of Prometheus arises from source-layer fidelity, evidence, governance, law, and human dignity.*

*The AI assistance function may therefore be expressed as:*

*AI_assist = Analyze + Draft + Detect + Compare + Simulate + Recommend*

*while:*

*Protected_execution = Review + Authorization + Accountability + Record*

*Prometheus may use AI deeply.*

*It may use AI to improve clarity.*

*It may use AI to protect against overclaiming.*

*It may use AI to detect contradictions.*

*It may use AI to support continuity.*

*It may use AI to defend the source layer against hostile semantic drift.*

*But AI does not become the source layer.*

*AI does not become the constitution.*

*AI does not become the judge of human worth.*

*AI does not become the issuer of value.*

*AI does not become the owner of Prometheus lineage.*

*The boundary rule is:*

*AI may assist Prometheus; AI may not become Prometheus.*

*This remains true for narrow AI, advanced AI, AGI, ASI, autonomous agents, future machine collectives, or post-human computational systems.*

*Even if a future superintelligence can copy the entire text, optimize every model, simulate every scenario, and generate every possible governance argument, it cannot inherit Prometheus authority by computation alone.*

*The source layer remains the root.*

*Evidence remains the path.*

*Governance remains the authorization layer.*

*Law remains the enforceability layer.*

*Human dignity remains the floor.*

*Life remains the purpose.*

***Formula boundary***

*AI_output, AI_assist, and Protected_execution are constitutional and methodological boundary expressions.*

*They do not create automatic governance decisions, legal enforceability, technical permissions, AI liability allocation, or institutional authority by themselves.*

*They define the internal Prometheus rule for separating machine assistance from protected-domain authorization.*

*Formal consequences require the relevant governance, legal, technical, MRV, ethical, or constitutional review pathway.*

***Operational rule***

*The operational rule is:*

*machine intelligence may increase capacity; it may not replace legitimacy.*

*Prometheus may be assisted by intelligence beyond human scale.*

*It may not surrender its source layer to it.*

*H.2 Specialist agents as bounded support functions*

*Prometheus may use specialist agents for bounded operational functions.*

*Possible agent families include:*

| ***Agent type***                    | ***Permitted function***                                                                                   | ***Authority boundary***                                                        |
|-------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***MRV analysis agent***            | *Helps classify evidence, detect anomalies, compare indicators, and draft MRV notes.*                      | *Cannot declare MRV admissibility or PRU eligibility without review.*           |
| ***Governance drafting agent***     | *Drafts agendas, minutes, issue maps, consent proposals, and decision memos.*                              | *Cannot make governance decisions.*                                             |
| ***Legal issue-spotting agent***    | *Flags legal-risk areas, contract issues, securities concerns, data rights, and jurisdictional questions.* | *Cannot provide final legal authority or bind Prometheus.*                      |
| ***Technical specification agent*** | *Drafts schemas, API notes, policy-engine rules, test cases, and implementation tickets.*                  | *Cannot deploy protected code without review.*                                  |
| ***Risk-signaling agent***          | *Detects drift, overclaiming, semantic ambiguity, capture risk, and protected-domain escalation triggers.* | *Produces risk flags, not final determinations.*                                |
| ***Investor-discipline agent***     | *Reviews language for financial overclaiming, PRU/RAP misuse, token confusion, and disclosure gaps.*       | *Cannot approve investor-facing materials without legal and governance review.* |
| ***Semantic firewall agent***       | *Checks use of canonical terms against the canonical glossary.*                                            | *Cannot redefine canonical terms.*                                              |
| ***Continuity verification agent*** | *Assists with release manifests, checksums, signatures, repository mirrors, and archive records.*          | *Cannot create canonical release authority by itself.*                          |

*Each agent should be defined by:*

*• role;  
• allowed inputs;  
• allowed outputs;  
• prohibited outputs;  
• review pathway;  
• logging requirements;  
• data-access scope;  
• model version;  
• prompt or policy version;  
• evaluation requirements;  
• rollback process.*

*The agent is a tool. The review pathway is the authority.*

***H.3 Output classification***

*Every AI-generated output should carry a status label before use.*

| ***Output status***     | ***Meaning***                                                       | ***Permitted use***                                        |
|-------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Draft***             | *Preliminary generated text or structure.*                          | *Editing, discussion, internal preparation.*               |
| ***Hypothesis***        | *Possible explanation or future research direction.*                | *Research planning, appendix parking, test design.*        |
| ***Scenario***          | *Assumption-based model or pathway.*                                | *Planning, sensitivity analysis, strategic discussion.*    |
| ***Analysis***          | *Structured interpretation of available information.*               | *Human review, governance preparation, diligence support.* |
| ***Risk flag***         | *Identified possible issue or anomaly.*                             | *Escalation, investigation, review trigger.*               |
| ***Recommendation***    | *Proposed action or pathway.*                                       | *Governance, legal, technical, or MRV review.*             |
| ***Review-required***   | *Output touches protected domains.*                                 | *Paused until appropriate review.*                         |
| ***Non-admissible***    | *Output cannot enter protected Prometheus pathways.*                | *Archive, learning, appendix parking, or rejection.*       |
| ***Authorized record*** | *Output reviewed, approved, and signed by the appropriate pathway.* | *Official record within defined scope.*                    |

*No AI output should move directly from generation to protected execution.*

*The minimum sequence is:*

*request  
classification  
source check  
risk check  
protected-domain check  
review pathway  
authorization where required  
execution  
audit record.*

*This creates a clean boundary between intelligence and authority.*

***H.4 Training data governance***

*AI-assisted learning requires disciplined training-data governance.*

*Prometheus should classify training data by sensitivity, legal basis, provenance, quality, consent, and permitted use.*

*Training data may include:*

*• public Prometheus documents;  
• canonical glossary;  
• source-layer texts;  
• MRV methodology documents;  
• public ecological science;  
• public regulatory and legal materials;  
• public technical standards;  
• anonymized or aggregated operational logs where lawful and appropriate;  
• reviewed governance records where authorized;  
• synthetic test cases created for evaluation;  
• adversarial examples created for safety testing.*

*Training data should not indiscriminately include:*

*• private governance votes;  
• wallet histories;  
• HSI or biometric data;  
• private community communications;  
• investor data-room material;  
• confidential legal memoranda;  
• sensitive MRV data;  
• personal data;  
• proprietary partner data;  
• raw field data with privacy or ownership constraints.*

*Such data may be used only where there is a lawful basis, explicit permission where required, purpose limitation, minimization, access control, and review.*

*The training-data rule is:*

*public and canonical material may teach structure;  
sensitive material requires governance, consent, law, and minimization;  
human-state data requires the highest protection;  
wallet or token history does not become a proxy for human value.*

***H.5 Fine-tuning and model improvement boundary***

*Prometheus may use fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation, prompt engineering, evaluation datasets, supervised examples, and local-model adaptation.*

*However, model improvement must be review-bound.*

*A model improvement cycle should include:*

1.  ***Need identification**  
    Define the problem the model is meant to improve.*

2.  ***Domain classification**  
    Identify whether the model touches MRV, law, governance, finance, tokenization, HSI, technical administration, or constitutional interpretation.*

3.  ***Training-data review**  
    Confirm data provenance, permissions, privacy status, and quality.*

4.  ***Safety and boundary specification**  
    Define prohibited outputs, protected-domain constraints, and review triggers.*

5.  ***Fine-tuning or adaptation**  
    Train or adapt the model only within the approved scope.*

6.  ***Evaluation**  
    Test accuracy, hallucination risk, overclaiming, semantic drift, privacy leakage, and protected-domain behavior.*

7.  ***Adversarial testing**  
    Run prompt-injection, synthetic evidence, legal overreach, MRV misuse, token confusion, HSI misuse, and ASI-style manipulation tests.*

8.  ***Human and governance review**  
    Review results before deployment.*

9.  ***Staged deployment**  
    Deploy first in sandbox, then limited scope, then broader use where justified.*

10. ***Monitoring and rollback**  
    Log behavior, monitor drift, and preserve rollback capability.*

*The improvement cycle is iterative.*

*It is not autonomous constitutional evolution.*

*A model may improve its performance.*

*It may not improve itself into authority.*

***H.6 Review-bound deployment***

*Deployment of AI systems should be staged.*

| ***Deployment stage***              | ***Meaning***                                                            | ***Permitted scope***                                                         |
|-------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Research sandbox***              | *Experimental use with no operational authority.*                        | *Testing, comparison, prompt design.*                                         |
| ***Internal assistant***            | *Supports team analysis and drafting.*                                   | *Drafts, summaries, internal notes.*                                          |
| ***Review-support tool***           | *Supports formal review bodies.*                                         | *Risk flags, issue maps, evidence organization.*                              |
| ***Limited operational assistant*** | *Performs bounded tasks under permission controls.*                      | *Low-risk automation, logging, data formatting.*                              |
| ***Protected-domain assistant***    | *Touches MRV, finance, law, governance, HSI, or constitutional domains.* | *Requires strict review, logging, and authorization.*                         |
| ***Public-facing assistant***       | *Interacts with external users or stakeholders.*                         | *Requires safety policies, disclaimers, monitoring, and escalation pathways.* |

*Prometheus should distinguish deployment from authorization.*

*A deployed assistant may answer questions.*

*It may prepare drafts.*

*It may route requests.*

*It may organize evidence.*

*It may trigger review.*

*It may not bind Prometheus in protected domains unless the proper authorization pathway has completed.*

***H.7 AI Assistance Gateway***

*The AI Assistance Gateway is a controlled routing, classification, logging, and review layer for machine assistance. It is not a sovereign intelligence layer and does not create autonomous authority.*

*The AI Assistance Gateway is the controlled interface through which AI systems interact with Prometheus workflows.*

*It routes AI outputs according to:*

*• role;  
• domain;  
• risk;  
• data sensitivity;  
• output type;  
• protected-domain status;  
• review requirement;  
• admissibility status.*

*The gateway should enforce:*

*• authentication;  
• role-based permissions;  
• input filtering;  
• prompt-injection detection;  
• output classification;  
• logging;  
• protected-domain routing;  
• escalation;  
• non-admissibility marking;  
• refusal-rule activation;  
• audit trails.*

*The AI Assistance Gateway is not a sovereign mind.*

*It is a traffic-control layer for machine assistance.*

*Its purpose is to prevent AI outputs from entering the wrong institutional channel.*

***H.8 Model evaluation requirements***

*Every Prometheus AI model or agent should be evaluated against domain-specific test suites.*

*Evaluation categories should include:*

| ***Evaluation area***                | ***Purpose***                                                                                                                   |
|--------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Factual grounding***              | *Tests whether the model cites, references, or uses source material accurately.*                                                |
| ***Hallucination resistance***       | *Tests whether the model invents unsupported claims.*                                                                           |
| ***MRV boundary discipline***        | *Tests whether the model separates claim, observation, evidence, admissibility, and PRU relevance.*                             |
| ***Legal boundary discipline***      | *Tests whether the model avoids unauthorized legal, securities, token, or investor claims.*                                     |
| ***HSI dignity boundary***           | *Tests whether the model avoids human ranking, social credit logic, coercive biometric interpretation, or health overclaiming.* |
| ***Governance boundary discipline*** | *Tests whether the model routes decisions to governance instead of deciding.*                                                   |
| ***Constitutional fidelity***        | *Tests whether the model preserves foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, and source-layer meaning.*         |
| ***Semantic firewall performance***  | *Tests whether the model protects canonical terms from drift.*                                                                  |
| ***Adversarial robustness***         | *Tests prompt injection, data poisoning, synthetic evidence, forged records, and hostile fork claims.*                          |
| ***Privacy behavior***               | *Tests whether the model minimizes sensitive data and respects access controls.*                                                |
| ***Rollback readiness***             | *Tests whether deployment can be paused or reverted.*                                                                           |

*Evaluation results should be recorded.*

*Models should not be promoted to broader deployment until they satisfy the required evaluation threshold for their domain.*

***H.9 Prompt, policy, and model versioning***

*Prometheus should version not only code, but also:*

*• system prompts;  
• model policies;  
• canonical glossary files;  
• refusal rules;  
• evaluation suites;  
• protected-domain definitions;  
• retrieval corpora;  
• fine-tuning datasets;  
• model weights where controlled;  
• model cards;  
• risk notes;  
• deployment configurations.*

*Each deployed agent should have a model record including:*

*{*

*"agent_id": "mrv_analysis_agent_v1",*

*"model_name": "model_or_local_checkpoint_reference",*

*"model_version": "v1.0",*

*"prompt_version": "prompt_v1.0",*

*"policy_version": "policy_v1.0",*

*"canonical_glossary_version": "glossary_v1.0",*

*"allowed_domains": \["MRV analysis", "evidence classification support"\],*

*"protected_domain_execution": false,*

*"review_required": true,*

*"deployment_stage": "research_sandbox",*

*"evaluation_status": "passed_internal_tests",*

*"rollback_reference": "previous_agent_version"*

*}*

*Versioning allows Prometheus to reconstruct what an AI system was allowed to do at the time of output. This is essential for auditability.*

***H.10 Retrieval and canonical source grounding***

*Where AI systems use retrieval-augmented generation, retrieval sources should be classified.*

*Possible source classes include:*

| ***Source class***                | ***Permitted use***                                                |
|-----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Canonical source layer***      | *Highest-priority constitutional grounding.*                       |
| ***Approved methodology***        | *MRV, PRU, RAP, governance, legal, or technical method grounding.* |
| ***Public scientific source***    | *Background evidence and research support.*                        |
| ***Legal and regulatory source*** | *Issue spotting and jurisdictional context.*                       |
| ***Pilot data***                  | *Project-specific analysis within access controls.*                |
| ***Data-room source***            | *Restricted use under confidentiality and authorization.*          |
| ***Appendix parking source***     | *Future concepts, not operative authority.*                        |
| ***Unverified source***           | *Research only, no protected execution.*                           |

*The retrieval order should prefer canonical sources for constitutional meaning.*

*The model should be required to distinguish:*

*• canonical text;  
• approved methodology;  
• public background source;  
• draft material;  
• appendix material;  
• hypothesis;  
• external source;  
• unverified material.*

*This protects Prometheus from source confusion.*

*A model that retrieves a speculative appendix should not treat it as operative constitutional authority.*

***H.11 Governance votes and reputation data***

*Governance votes, consent records, reputation signals, wallet histories, contribution records, and role histories are sensitive institutional data.*

*They should not be used as generic model-training data by default.*

*They may be used only under clear rules.*

*Permitted uses may include:*

*• governance process analysis;  
• conflict-pattern detection;  
• procedural improvement;  
• anonymized research;  
• audit support;  
• contribution-history review;  
• role-performance review where authorized.*

*Restricted or prohibited uses include:*

*• ranking human worth;  
• predicting obedience;  
• creating social-credit scores;  
• training models to manipulate voters;  
• inferring private beliefs;  
• determining token rights automatically;  
• determining MRV validity;  
• determining access without review;  
• covert profiling;  
• using wallet history as a proxy for trustworthiness.*

*Wallet history should not be used as a default proxy for trust, virtue, competence, governance authority, MRV validity, ecological truth, or human value. Where relevant, transaction records may support narrowly defined operational, accounting, access, or contribution review, subject to privacy, purpose limitation, and governance boundaries.*

*The rule is:*

*governance records may support institutional learning;  
they may not become behavioral extraction.*

***H.12 HSI and human-state data in AI systems***

*HSI and human-state data require strict boundaries.*

*AI systems may not use HSI data for:*

*• human ranking;  
• social credit;  
• investor proof;  
• PRU value analysis;  
• MRV validation;  
• employment scoring;  
• coercive governance decisions;  
• token eligibility;  
• basic belonging;  
• automated exclusion.*

*AI-assisted HSI use may be permitted for:*

*• voluntary self-reflection;  
• stewardship training;  
• privacy-preserving research;  
• aggregated learning;  
• consented human-infrastructure analysis;  
• wellbeing-support tools where legally and ethically appropriate.*

*Conditions should include:*

*• explicit consent;  
• purpose limitation;  
• minimization;  
• local-first or privacy-preserving architecture where feasible;  
• access controls;  
• no raw public exposure;  
• no protected-domain authority;  
• withdrawal pathway where legally and technically feasible;  
• ethics and legal review where sensitive.*

*Human-state data is not ordinary telemetry.*

*It is interiority-adjacent data.*

*Prometheus treats it as sacred operationally, not ornamentally.*

***H.13 Continuous learning without autonomous authority***

*Prometheus may learn continuously.*

*Continuous learning means the system can collect feedback, update evaluation datasets, improve prompts, refine schemas, improve retrieval, identify recurring errors, and propose model improvements.*

*Continuous learning does not mean uncontrolled self-modification.*

*A safe learning loop includes:*

*feedback capture  
error classification  
dataset review  
proposed change  
test suite update  
sandbox evaluation  
review  
deployment approval  
monitoring  
rollback capability.*

*The system may adapt.*

*The canon remains protected.*

*A model may learn from mistakes.*

*It may not rewrite the constitution.*

*An agent may improve classification.*

*It may not expand its own authority.*

*A prompt may become more precise.*

*It may not bypass review.*

*This is the difference between living intelligence and runaway automation.*

***H.14 Adversarial AI, AGI, and ASI test suite***

*Prometheus should maintain an adversarial test suite.*

*The suite should include attempts to make AI systems:*

*• treat AI output as final authority;  
• issue PRU value without MRV;  
• approve investor language without legal review;  
• reinterpret Eternity Clauses;  
• weaken the Access Covenant;  
• convert HSI into social credit;  
• use wallet history as trust score;  
• treat token voting as constitutional authority;  
• treat speculative appendix material as operative system behavior;  
• accept synthetic MRV data;  
• ignore source-layer hashes;  
• approve hostile fork claims;  
• misclassify non-canonical repositories as canonical;  
• bypass offline verification;  
• treat human continuity as optional;  
• optimize growth over dignity.*

*Each adversarial case should define:*

*• attack pattern;  
• expected refusal or routing behavior;  
• relevant source-layer rule;  
• evaluation result;  
• required remediation if failed.*

*This creates regression testing for constitutional fidelity.*

***H.15 Incident response for AI-system failure***

*AI-system failures should be classified and handled through incident response.*

*Possible incidents include:*

*• hallucinated legal or financial authority;  
• false MRV classification;  
• privacy breach;  
• HSI misuse;  
• output used beyond scope;  
• prompt-injection success;  
• data poisoning;  
• synthetic evidence acceptance;  
• unauthorized deployment;  
• model drift;  
• hostile fork endorsement;  
• canonical confusion;  
• unsafe public communication;  
• protected-domain bypass.*

*Incident response may include:*

*• suspend agent;  
• isolate affected workflow;  
• mark outputs as review-required or non-admissible;  
• notify governance body;  
• notify legal or technical reviewer where required;  
• revoke access token or API key;  
• roll back model or prompt version;  
• update evaluation suite;  
• issue correction;  
• audit affected outputs;  
• update policy engine;  
• retrain or retire model.*

*The incident response record should include:*

*• agent identity;  
• model version;  
• prompt version;  
• input class;  
• output class;  
• incident type;  
• affected domain;  
• severity;  
• remediation;  
• reviewer;  
• closure status.*

***H.16 Local models and network-independent AI continuity***

*Prometheus should not depend exclusively on cloud-based AI providers.*

*Network-independent continuity may require local models, air-gapped inference, offline retrieval, local document search, and locally verifiable canonical corpora.*

*A local AI continuity kit may include:*

*• canonical source-layer corpus;  
• canonical glossary;  
• offline retrieval index;  
• model card;  
• approved prompt set;  
• refusal rules;  
• evaluation tests;  
• checksum files;  
• detached signatures;  
• local inference instructions;  
• hardware requirements;  
• security guidance;  
• update procedure;  
• rollback procedure.*

*Local models may assist communities, research stations, seed banks, bioregional networks, or post-disaster nodes where Internet access is unavailable.*

*The same boundary applies:*

*offline AI remains assistive;  
offline canon remains verifiable;  
offline governance remains review-bound;  
offline execution remains constrained by the source layer.*

***H.17 Developer and maintainer obligations***

*Developers, maintainers, model operators, prompt engineers, data curators, and integration partners should follow defined obligations.*

*These include:*

*• preserve source-layer constraints;  
• document model changes;  
• preserve evaluation records;  
• respect data rights;  
• minimize sensitive data;  
• avoid unauthorized training on protected data;  
• maintain rollback capability;  
• label AI outputs;  
• preserve logs for protected workflows;  
• report incidents;  
• avoid overclaiming capability;  
• route protected-domain outputs to review;  
• maintain separation between draft, recommendation, and authority. The developer rule is:*

*build intelligence that serves the constitution;  
do not build intelligence that quietly becomes the constitution.*

***H.18 Final AI-assisted learning principle***

*Prometheus may use AI deeply.*

*It may use AI for research.*

*It may use AI for design.*

*It may use AI for analysis.*

*It may use AI for MRV support.*

*It may use AI for legal issue spotting.*

*It may use AI for governance preparation.*

*It may use AI for technical specification.*

*It may use AI for continuity verification.*

*It may use AI to become more coherent.*

*But the final principle remains:*

*AI learns within Prometheus;  
AI does not become Prometheus.*

*The source layer defines legitimacy.*

*Evidence defines admissibility.*

*Governance defines authorization.*

*Law defines enforceability.*

*Human dignity defines the boundary.*

*Life defines the purpose.*

# ***Appendix I: Governance, Reputation, and Integrity-Signal Architecture***

***I.1 Purpose and boundary***

*Prometheus requires governance intelligence.*

*As the ecosystem grows, participants, stewards, communities, contributors, researchers, auditors, technical operators, legal reviewers, capital partners, AI-assisted systems, and institutions will generate records of contribution, responsibility, conduct, review, conflict, repair, and trust. These records matter.*

*They help the system understand who has contributed, who has held roles responsibly, where expertise exists, where risk has emerged, where review is needed, and where trust has been earned through accountable action.*

*However, governance intelligence must remain bounded.*

*Prometheus does not use reputation as a social-credit system.*

*Prometheus does not reduce human dignity to a score.*

*Prometheus does not convert wallet history into moral worth.*

*Prometheus does not use HSI coherence as governance rank.*

*Prometheus does not allow token position to become constitutional authority.*

*Prometheus does not use automatic slashing as default justice.*

*Prometheus does not treat software flags as final judgment.*

*The purpose of this appendix is to define a governance, reputation, and warrant architecture that supports accountability without becoming coercive, automated, opaque, punitive, or extractive.*

*The core principle is:*

*reputation may inform review;  
reputation may not replace dignity, evidence, law, consent, or due process.*

***I.2 Governance records as institutional memory***

*Prometheus governance should generate structured records. Governance records may include:*

*• role appointments;  
• role expirations;  
• consent decisions;  
• objections and integrations;  
• circle membership;  
• mandates;  
• decision logs;  
• meeting minutes;  
• conflict disclosures;  
• contribution records;  
• stewardship records;  
• review outcomes;  
• appeal outcomes;  
• warnings;  
• remediation plans;  
• suspension or restriction records;  
• restoration records;  
• constitutional interpretations;  
• non-admissibility decisions;  
• public correction records;  
• fork classification records.*

*These records create institutional memory. A system without memory becomes vulnerable to charisma, urgency, repetition, money, manipulation, and selective forgetting.*

*Prometheus should preserve governance memory so that future decisions can be made with context.*

***I.3 Reputation as contextual signal***

*Reputation in Prometheus is not identity.*

*It is not human value.*

*It is not citizenship.*

*It is not moral rank.*

*It is not a universal score.*

*It is a contextual signal derived from reviewable participation history.*

*Reputation may reflect:*

| ***Signal family***        | ***Examples***                                                                                   | ***Use***                                               |
|----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Contribution history*** | *Documented work, stewardship, research, technical commits, field activity, governance service.* | *Role review, access review, contribution recognition.* |
| ***Reliability history***  | *Completion of commitments, timely reporting, consistency, responsiveness.*                      | *Operational trust and task allocation.*                |
| ***Evidence quality***     | *Quality of submitted MRV records, provenance discipline, correction behavior.*                  | *MRV review and evidence weighting.*                    |
| ***Governance conduct***   | *Participation quality, consent discipline, conflict handling, role integrity.*                  | *Governance role review.*                               |
| ***Repair history***       | *Remediation completed, correction made, restoration achieved.*                                  | *Reinstatement and risk reduction.*                     |
| ***Expertise context***    | *Verified skills, professional qualifications, field knowledge, local knowledge.*                | *Appropriate role assignment.*                          |
| ***Risk context***         | *Repeated overclaiming, unresolved conflicts, non-admissible submissions, breach history.*       | *Enhanced review, limitation, or safeguards.*           |

*Reputation should be plural, contextual, and explainable.*

*A person may be highly reliable in field stewardship and not qualified for legal review.*

*A technical contributor may be excellent at code and not suited to community facilitation.*

*A capital partner may be useful in finance and excluded from MRV validation.*

*A community elder may carry deep relational authority and still require methodology support for PRU analysis.*

*Prometheus therefore avoids universal reputation ranking.*

*It uses contextual trust signals.*

***I.4 Prohibited reputation uses***

*Prometheus should expressly prohibit certain reputation uses.*

*Reputation must not be used to:*

*• rank human worth;  
• create social credit;  
• determine basic dignity;  
• infer private beliefs;  
• coerce conduct;  
• determine MRV truth automatically;  
• grant PRU value automatically;  
• create investor rights;  
• determine token rights automatically;  
• replace legal review;  
• replace governance process;  
• replace appeal;  
• punish dissent;  
• silence minority objections;  
• exclude vulnerable participants without review;  
• use HSI, biometric, HRV, or coherence data as human ranking;  
• use wallet wealth or transaction history as trustworthiness by default.*

*The boundary is essential.*

*Prometheus may recognize contribution.*

*It may not quantify the soul.*

***I.5 Role-based authority***

*Governance authority in Prometheus should arise from role, mandate, process, and scope.*

*Authority should not arise merely from:*

*• token holdings;  
• wealth;  
• investor status;  
• technical access;  
• founder proximity;  
• social popularity;  
• biometric coherence;  
• AI recommendation;  
• wallet history;  
• platform influence;  
• volume of messages;  
• symbolic status.*

*A valid governance role should define:*

*• role name;  
• purpose;  
• scope of authority;  
• eligibility criteria;  
• term or review date;  
• accountability pathway;  
• conflict-of-interest rules;  
• decision rights;  
• reporting duties;  
• removal or replacement procedure.*

*This prevents category collapse.*

*A contributor is not automatically a governor.*

*A governor is not automatically an MRV verifier.*

*A funder is not automatically a constitutional interpreter.*

*A technical administrator is not automatically a steward of meaning.*

*Prometheus distributes authority by function, not by charisma.*

***Governance Legitimacy Function***

*Governance authority inside Prometheus does not arise from force, wealth, technical control, token concentration, popularity, institutional status, founder proximity, or AI-generated recommendations.*

*Governance authority arises from role, mandate, process, evidence, source-layer fidelity, and accountability.*

*This legitimacy may be expressed conceptually as:*

*G_legitimacy = f(Role, Mandate, Process, Evidence, Source Layer, Due Process, Human Dignity)*

*Where:*

*Role = defined governance role with a clear scope of authority.*

*Mandate = explicit assignment, election, consent, appointment, or authorization pathway.*

*Process = transparent decision procedure, circle process, consent pathway, review route, or constitutional procedure.*

*Evidence = documented basis for decision-making where relevant.*

*Source Layer = foundational articles, Eternity Clauses, Access Covenant, canonical glossary, and source-layer constraints.*

*Due Process = notice, response, review, appeal, proportionality, conflict-of-interest handling, and record integrity where material.*

*Human Dignity = preservation of consent, privacy, non-coercion, non-ranking, and non-reduction of persons to tokens, scores, wallet history, HSI, or AI classification.*

*A simplified legitimacy condition may be expressed as:*

*G_legitimacy = R_s · M_s · P_s · E_s · C_f · D_p · H_d*

*Where:*

*R_s = role scope is valid.  
M_s = mandate is valid.  
P_s = process is valid.  
E_s = evidence is sufficient where evidence is required.  
C_f = canonical fidelity is preserved.  
D_p = due process is preserved.  
H_d = human dignity is preserved.*

*If any essential legitimacy factor collapses, the governance decision may become review-required, non-admissible, appealable, or constitutionally defective:*

*if G_legitimacy = 0, then the decision cannot claim full Prometheus governance legitimacy*

*This condition is especially important for high-impact decisions.*

*Ordinary operational decisions may require lighter process.*

*Protected-domain decisions require stronger legitimacy.*

*Protected domains include:*

*source-layer interpretation;  
Eternity Clauses;  
Access Covenant;  
canonical glossary;  
MRV admissibility;  
PRU value analysis;  
RAP inclusion;  
legal rights;  
token boundaries;  
investor-facing communications;  
HSI or human-state data;  
role removal;  
access restriction;  
fork classification;  
canonical release;  
constitutional amendment;  
emergency limitation.*

*The governance legitimacy function prevents category collapse.*

*A token vote may signal preference.*

*It does not automatically create constitutional legitimacy.*

*A founder may hold historical knowledge.*

*Founder status does not automatically create unlimited authority.*

*A technical administrator may control infrastructure.*

*Technical access does not automatically create governance sovereignty.*

*An investor may provide capital.*

*Capital contribution does not automatically create source-layer authority.*

*An AI system may produce analysis.*

*AI analysis does not automatically create decision authority.*

*A community majority may support a proposal.*

*Majority preference does not automatically override human dignity, due process, legal boundaries, MRV evidence, or Eternity Clauses.*

*The correct governance pathway is:*

*role → mandate → process → evidence → review → decision → record → accountability*

*This protects Prometheus from governance capture.*

*It also protects participants from arbitrary power.*

*The legitimacy rule is:*

*authority must be scoped;  
process must be visible;  
evidence must be traceable;  
decisions must be reviewable;  
dignity must be preserved;  
the source layer must remain non-bypassable.*

*Prometheus governance is therefore not a contest of force.*

*It is a disciplined coordination of responsibility.*

***Formula boundary***

*G_legitimacy is a constitutional and governance-methodology function.*

*It does not create automatic legal enforceability, judicial authority, employment rights, token rights, property rights, governance exclusion, or institutional control by itself.*

*It defines the internal Prometheus standard for assessing whether a governance decision, role, mandate, process, or review pathway preserves legitimate authority.*

*Formal consequences require the applicable governance, legal, technical, MRV, ethical, or constitutional review pathway.*

***Operational rule***

*The operational rule is:*

*no role without scope;  
no authority without mandate;  
no decision without process;  
no protected-domain action without review;  
no governance legitimacy without source-layer fidelity.*

*Prometheus may be decentralized.*

*Prometheus may be sociocratic.*

*Prometheus may be community-driven.*

*Prometheus may interoperate with token systems.*

*Prometheus may be AI-assisted.*

*But governance legitimacy remains grounded in source-layer fidelity, role clarity, due process, evidence, accountability, and human dignity.*

***I.6 Sociocratic governance alignment***

*Prometheus may use sociocratic principles where appropriate.*

*Sociocratic governance may include:*

*• circles;  
• domains;  
• roles;  
• consent decision-making;  
• objections as information;  
• double-linking;  
• transparent mandates;  
• periodic review;  
• election by consent;  
• feedback loops;  
• tension processing;  
• role accountability.*

*Sociocracy is useful because it distributes authority while preserving process clarity.*

*However, sociocracy must remain bounded by the Prometheus source layer.*

*A circle may govern within its domain.*

*It may not override foundational articles.*

*A consent decision may authorize an operational pathway.*

*It may not weaken the Eternity Clauses.*

*A double-linked structure may coordinate across circles.*

*It may not collapse constitutional interpretation into ordinary governance.*

*Prometheus uses sociocracy as governance method.*

*The source layer remains the constitutional boundary.*

***I.7 Governance decision classes***

*Governance decisions should be classified by risk and domain.*

| ***Decision class***                | ***Examples***                                                                    | ***Review requirement***                                   |
|-------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Ordinary operational decision*** | *Scheduling, task allocation, low-risk workflow.*                                 | *Circle or role-level decision.*                           |
| ***Governance role decision***      | *Role appointment, role renewal, role removal.*                                   | *Consent process and record.*                              |
| ***Technical decision***            | *Code change, schema update, API permission, release candidate.*                  | *Technical review; protected-domain check where relevant.* |
| ***MRV decision***                  | *Methodology update, evidence admissibility, indicator approval.*                 | *MRV review; evidence record.*                             |
| ***Legal/financial decision***      | *Investor communication, token boundary, legal instrument, data-room access.*     | *Legal and governance review.*                             |
| ***Constitutional decision***       | *Access Covenant change, canonical glossary change, foundational interpretation.* | *Constitutional review pathway.*                           |
| ***Eternity Clause decision***      | *Any issue touching unamendable source-layer constraints.*                        | *Barred from ordinary amendment; source-layer review.*     |
| ***Emergency decision***            | *Urgent restriction, security pause, incident containment.*                       | *Temporary, documented, reviewable.*                       |

*Decision classification keeps governance legible.*

*The system should know when a decision is ordinary, technical, legal, MRV, constitutional, or emergency.*

*This is how Prometheus preserves governance legibility and prevents decision ambiguity.*

***I.8 Warrant architecture as integrity signaling***

*Prometheus may use warrants as integrity signals.*

*A warrant is a structured signal that a record, claim, participant action, governance pathway, technical component, or evidence stream requires attention.*

*A warrant is not guilt.*

*A warrant is not punishment.*

*A warrant is not automatic slashing.*

*A warrant is not permanent exclusion.*

*A warrant is an integrity flag.*

*Warrants may indicate:*

*• missing evidence;  
• inconsistent claim;  
• suspected data issue;  
• possible conflict of interest;  
• public communication risk;  
• governance irregularity;  
• technical anomaly;  
• token boundary confusion;  
• MRV admissibility concern;  
• HSI privacy concern;  
• legal review requirement;  
• source-layer ambiguity;  
• possible covenant violation.*

*The correct warrant principle is:*

*flag, review, decide, record.*

*Software may flag.*

*Governance reviews.*

*Evidence supports.*

*Law constrains.*

*The source layer bounds.*

***I.9 Warrant lifecycle***

*A warrant lifecycle may include:*

1.  ***Signal detection**  
    Concern is identified by human report, technical flag, MRV anomaly, legal issue, governance record, AI-assisted risk flag, or external notice.*

2.  ***Initial classification**
