***J.6 Example flow: Regenerative agriculture project***

*1. Agent registration.*

*Land steward, project entity, verifier, and relevant institutional actors register as agents.*

*2. Commitment.*

*A funder, partner, public entity, or internal treasury creates a defined commitment to support a soil-regeneration project.*

*3. Resource.*

*A land-use-right or project-resource entry is created, linked to site boundaries, OHE context, and legal access documentation.*

*4. MRV event.*

*Satellite data, soil sensors, field observations, laboratory tests, and stewardship records produce MRV-linked evidence events.*

*5. Evidence review.*

*MRV evidence is reviewed for provenance, confidence, methodology alignment, no-double-counting, PHI context, and admissibility.*

*6. PRU-relevant interpretation.*

*Verified indicators may support PRU value analysis only after evidence, methodology, confidence, governance review, admissibility, and legal boundary checks.*

*7. Economic or operational event.*

*Operational outputs, service delivery, procurement, hospitality, education, food production, or other real economic activity may be recorded as REA events.*

*8. Legal or institutional pathway.*

*Any future credit, note, fund interest, revenue right, tokenized representation, or compliant digital instrument requires separate legal structuring, disclosure, investor-protection review, jurisdiction-specific compliance, and anti-double-counting controls.*

*9. Commitment fulfillment.*

*Events fulfilling the original commitment are linked back to the commitment record.*

*10. Governance review.*

*The relevant circle reviews project performance, evidence quality, adverse events, lessons learned, and parameter updates. All steps are recorded through agent-centric source chains and shared validation where appropriate, preserving temporal linking, auditability, and constitutional compliance.*

***J.7 Schema definitions***

*The following simplified schema illustrates the conceptual structure of REA / Valueflows-compatible records.*

*syntax = "proto3";*

*package rea.v1;*

*import "google/protobuf/timestamp.proto";*

*import "syntropic_header.proto";*

*message Resource {*

*bytes id = 1;*

*string name = 2;*

*enum ResourceType {*

*LAND_USE_RIGHT = 0;*

*WATER_ACCESS = 1;*

*BIOMASS = 2;*

*FOOD_OUTPUT = 3;*

*ECOLOGICAL_PERFORMANCE_CLAIM = 4;*

*BIODIVERSITY_RECORD = 5;*

*DATA_RECORD = 6;*

*LABOR = 7;*

*KNOWLEDGE = 8;*

*REPUTATION_CONTEXT = 9;*

*DIGITAL_ASSET = 10;*

*OTHER = 11;*

*}*

*ResourceType resource_type = 3;*

*double quantity = 4;*

*string unit = 5;*

*string location = 6; // GeoJSON*

*bytes ecosystem_id = 7;*

*string metadata = 8; // JSON*

*SyntropicHeader header = 9;*

*}*

*message Event {*

*bytes id = 1;*

*enum EventType {*

*PRODUCTION = 0;*

*CONSUMPTION = 1;*

*TRANSFER = 2;*

*EXCHANGE = 3;*

*MEASUREMENT = 4;*

*VERIFICATION = 5;*

*STEWARDSHIP_ACTION = 6;*

*COMMITMENT_FULFILLMENT = 7;*

*GOVERNANCE_DECISION = 8;*

*CORRECTION = 9;*

*REVIEW = 10;*

*}*

*EventType event_type = 2;*

*string action = 3;*

*bytes provider = 4;*

*bytes receiver = 5;*

*bytes resource_id = 6;*

*double quantity = 7;*

*string unit = 8;*

*string location = 9;*

*TimeWindow time_window = 10;*

*bytes mrv_id = 11;*

*bytes commitment_id = 12;*

*string review_status = 13;*

*string metadata = 14; // JSON*

*SyntropicHeader header = 15;*

*}*

*message Agent {*

*bytes id = 1;*

*enum AgentType {*

*INDIVIDUAL = 0;*

*ORGANIZATION = 1;*

*DAO = 2;*

*PROJECT = 3;*

*DEVICE = 4;*

*AUDITOR = 5;*

*VALIDATOR = 6;*

*LABORATORY = 7;*

*PUBLIC_INSTITUTION = 8;*

*AUTOMATED = 9;*

*}*

*AgentType agent_type = 2;*

*string name = 3;*

*string profile = 4; // JSON*

*bytes reputation_id = 5;*

*string authority_scope = 6; // JSON*

*SyntropicHeader header = 7;*

*}*

*message Commitment {*

*bytes id = 1;*

*enum CommitmentType {*

*AGREEMENT = 0;*

*CONTRACT = 1;*

*PLAN = 2;*

*PROPOSAL = 3;*

*STEWARDSHIP_COMMITMENT = 4;*

*SERVICE_COMMITMENT = 5;*

*FUNDING_COMMITMENT = 6;*

*GOVERNANCE_COMMITMENT = 7;*

*}*

*CommitmentType commitment_type = 2;*

*bytes provider = 3;*

*bytes receiver = 4;*

*bytes resource_id = 5;*

*double quantity = 6;*

*string unit = 7;*

*google.protobuf.Timestamp due_start = 8;*

*google.protobuf.Timestamp due_end = 9;*

*string conditions = 10; // JSON*

*repeated bytes fulfilled_by = 11;*

*enum Status {*

*ACTIVE = 0;*

*FULFILLED = 1;*

*EXPIRED = 2;*

*CANCELLED = 3;*

*UNDER_REVIEW = 4;*

*DISPUTED = 5;*

*}*

*Status status = 12;*

*SyntropicHeader header = 13;*

*}*

*message TimeWindow {*

*string id = 1;*

*google.protobuf.Timestamp start = 2;*

*google.protobuf.Timestamp end = 3;*

*}*

***J.8 Constitutional integration***

*Every entry in the REA / Valueflows DNA should include a syntropic sovereignty header or equivalent canonical integrity metadata.*

*The header ensures that value-flow records remain connected to source-layer constraints, release identity, governance boundaries, and constitutional compliance. Any attempt to record non-compliant events may be rejected, marked non-admissible, quarantined, or routed to governance review.*

*Detailed implementation specifications, including Holochain zome code, validation callbacks, query optimization, privacy controls, and API endpoints, should be maintained in the separate technical documentation package.*

# ***Appendix K: Planetary Boundaries, PHI, and Evidence Review Framework***

***K.0 Overview***

*The h•eart•h Prometheus protocol integrates the Planetary Boundaries framework as a foundational context layer for ecological integrity assessment, regenerative design, MRV prioritization, PHI synthesis, PRU analysis, RAP screening, and capital-readiness review.*

*The purpose of this appendix is not to convert Planetary Boundaries into automatic financial value, investment eligibility, ecological credit issuance, legal rights, or tokenized representation.*

*Its purpose is to define how planetary-boundary context may inform evidence review, risk interpretation, methodological caution, protected-domain routing, and capital-readiness discipline.*

*Within Prometheus, the Planetary Boundaries layer helps answer:*

*• whether an activity supports or weakens ecological integrity;*

*• whether a project operates in a sensitive ecological domain;*

*• whether MRV requirements should be strengthened;*

*• whether PHI signals indicate heightened review needs;*

*• whether PRU-related analysis should remain internal, review-bound, or non-admissible;*

*• whether RAP inclusion requires additional safeguards;*

*• whether public claims require stricter language discipline;*

*• whether capital-readiness interpretation should be limited, conditioned, or paused.*

*Planetary Boundaries and PHI should therefore function as context and review infrastructure.*

*They should not function as automatic scoring authority.*

*They should not automatically create financeability.*

*They should not automatically downgrade, approve, certify, tokenize, or reject a project without the appropriate evidence, methodology, governance, and legal review pathway.*

*The operational rule is:*

*planetary context informs risk;*

*PHI signals review needs;*

*MRV verifies evidence;*

*PRU analyzes only admissible value;*

*RAP aggregates only where portfolio criteria are satisfied;*

*capital-readiness remains downstream from evidence, governance, legal structure, and admissibility.*

*This preserves the distinction between ecological context, evidence review, financial interpretation, legal rights, and institutional use.*

*Planetary Boundaries provide Prometheus with a global scientific horizon.*

*They help the system understand where human activity interacts with the Earth-system processes that regulate planetary stability, biospheric resilience, ecological function, climate integrity, freshwater cycles, land systems, nutrient flows, ocean chemistry, atmospheric conditions, and novel-entity pressure.*

*Prometheus uses this framework to connect global ecological risk with local regenerative action.*

*The purpose is not to reduce Earth-system science into a single score.*

*The purpose is to make planetary context operationally legible inside a regenerative infrastructure system.*

*Within Prometheus, planetary-boundary logic informs:*

*• ecological baseline interpretation;  
• bioregional stress assessment;  
• MRV indicator selection;  
• Planetary Health Index, or PHI, domain structure;  
• boundary-pressure profiling;  
• PRU-relevant ecological analysis;  
• RAP portfolio screening;  
• regenerative design priorities;  
• capital-readiness diligence;  
• long-term continuity and resilience planning.*

*The framework follows the 2023 revision of the Planetary Boundaries architecture and aligns with the scientific direction associated with the Stockholm Resilience Centre, the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research, and the Planetary Boundaries Science initiative.*

*For Prometheus, this scientific architecture becomes part of the wider source-layer discipline:*

*planetary reality provides context;  
bioregional reality provides interpretation;  
field evidence provides grounding;  
MRV provides verification;  
PHI provides synthesis;  
PRU provides value discipline;  
governance provides admissibility;  
capital follows verified regeneration.*

*This appendix specifies the technical implementation of planetary boundary monitoring, threshold calculations, and the derivation of the Planetary Health Index (PHI) used throughout the ecosystem for ecological-context assessment, protective review triggers, systemic-risk awareness, and review-bound ecological response pathways.*

*It establishes a disciplined bridge between Earth-system science and site-level regenerative intelligence.*

***K.1 Purpose and boundary***

*Prometheus exists within planetary reality.*

*Every regenerative claim made inside Prometheus must remain accountable to the living systems that make life possible: soil, water, biodiversity, climate, nutrient cycles, land systems, ecological resilience, biospheric integrity, and the capacity of human communities to live within those systems over time.*

*The purpose of this appendix is to define how Prometheus integrates planetary-boundary thinking, bioregional stress indicators, Planetary Health Index logic, and ecological evidence review into its MRV, PRU, RAP, governance, design, legal, reporting, and capital-readiness architecture.*

*This appendix gives Prometheus a planetary-context layer.*

*It helps answer questions such as:*

*• Which Earth-system pressures are relevant to a given site, project, OHE, bioregion, or portfolio?  
• Which planetary-boundary domains should influence MRV indicator selection?  
• Which ecological risks should be visible before capital formation?  
• Which PHI domains are mature enough for evidence-backed interpretation?  
• Which indicators remain contextual, research-use, MRV-admissible, PRU-eligible, or non-admissible?  
• Which claims require scientific review, governance review, or further field evidence?  
• Which local regenerative actions may reduce pressure, increase resilience, or improve ecological function in relation to planetary-boundary domains?*

*The role of this appendix is therefore to create disciplined ecological legibility.*

*Planetary Boundaries do not operate inside Prometheus as an oracle, automatic approval mechanism, or sovereign enforcement layer.*

*They operate as a global scientific context.*

*PHI does not operate as a magic number.*

*It operates as a structured synthesis of ecological, bioregional, evidentiary, governance, and resilience indicators.*

*MRV remains the evidence-verification pathway.*

*Governance remains the admissibility pathway.*

*Legal review remains the enforceability pathway.*

*PRU and RAP remain downstream from methodology, evidence, confidence, and review.*

*Capital remains downstream from verified regeneration.*

*Prometheus may therefore use planetary-boundary and PHI logic to understand whether a project, OHE, bioregional cluster, PRU-relevant unit, or RAP candidate is operating within conditions of ecological stability, rising stress, threshold pressure, or systemic vulnerability.*

*This assessment is not merely descriptive.*

*It supports design intelligence.*

*It helps Prometheus identify where regeneration is most needed, which indicators matter most, how evidence should be prioritized, where public claims must remain cautious, and how capital can be aligned with real ecological repair.*

*The core operating principle is:*

*planetary context informs design;  
bioregional reality grounds interpretation;  
field evidence anchors claims;  
MRV verifies performance;  
PHI organizes ecological intelligence;  
governance determines admissibility;  
legal structure defines rights;  
capital follows verified regeneration.*

*Prometheus is planetary in awareness, bioregional in interpretation, local in evidence, and constitutional in fidelity.*

***K.2 The Nine Planetary Boundaries: Indicators and Data Sources***

*The Planetary Boundaries framework provides Prometheus with a global Earth-system context for interpreting regenerative performance.*

*The table below should be treated as a versioned planetary-boundary reference snapshot, not as an automatic enforcement mechanism. Its purpose is to identify:*

*• Earth-system process;  
• control variable;  
• pre-industrial or reference condition;  
• planetary-boundary range;  
• current global condition;  
• primary data sources;  
• suggested update frequency.*

*This table supports PHI, boundary-pressure profiling, MRV prioritization, PRU contextual analysis, RAP portfolio screening, and capital-readiness diligence.*

*It does not automatically issue PRU value, approve RAP inclusion, trigger token issuance, determine investor rights, replace MRV, or replace scientific review.*

*Planetary Boundaries provide context.*

*MRV provides evidence.*

*PHI organizes ecological intelligence.*

*Governance determines admissibility.*

*Capital follows verified regeneration.*

| ***Earth system process***              | ***Control variable***                                                                                      | ***Pre-industrial reference*** | ***Planetary boundary / Zone of increasing risk***             | ***Current value / 2023 reference snapshot*** | ***Data sources***                                               | ***Update frequency*** |
|-----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|
| ***Climate change***                    | *Atmospheric CO₂ concentration*                                                                             | *280 ppm*                      | *350–450 ppm*                                                  | *417 ppm*                                     | *NOAA/ESRL, Copernicus, WMO GAW*                                 | *Monthly*              |
| ***Climate change***                    | *Radiative forcing*                                                                                         | *0 W/m²*                       | *+1.0 to +1.5 W/m²*                                            | *+2.91 W/m²*                                  | *IPCC AR6, NASA GISS*                                            | *Annual*               |
| ***Biosphere integrity***               | *Genetic diversity: extinction rate*                                                                        | *0.1–1 E/MSY, background rate* | *10–100 E/MSY*                                                 | *\>100 E/MSY*                                 | *IUCN Red List, Living Planet Index*                             | *Annual*               |
| ***Biosphere integrity***               | *Functional diversity: HANPP, percentage of pre-industrial NPP*                                             | *1.9%*                         | *10–20%*                                                       | *30%*                                         | *FLUXCOM, MODIS, EarthStat*                                      | *Annual*               |
| ***Stratospheric ozone depletion***     | *Ozone concentration*                                                                                       | *290 DU*                       | *276–261 DU*                                                   | *284.6 DU*                                    | *NASA Ozone Watch, Copernicus CAMS*                              | *Daily / Monthly*      |
| ***Ocean acidification***               | *Aragonite saturation state, Ωarag*                                                                         | *3.44 Ωarag*                   | *2.75–2.4 Ωarag, approximately 80–70% of pre-industrial level* | *2.8 Ωarag, approximately 84%*                | *NOAA PMEL, GOA-ON, SOCAT*                                       | *Monthly*              |
| ***Biogeochemical flows — phosphorus*** | *Global phosphorus flow to oceans*                                                                          | *0 Mt/year*                    | *11–100 Mt/year*                                               | *22.6 Mt/year*                                | *FAO, global nutrient-cycle databases*                           | *Annual*               |
| ***Biogeochemical flows — phosphorus*** | *Regional phosphorus fertilizer application*                                                                | *0 Mt/year*                    | *6.2–11.2 Mt/year*                                             | *17.5 Mt/year*                                | *FAO, IFASTAT / International Fertilizer Association statistics* | *Annual*               |
| ***Biogeochemical flows — nitrogen***   | *Reactive nitrogen release*                                                                                 | *0 Mt/year*                    | *62–82 Mt/year*                                                | *190 Mt/year*                                 | *FAO, EDGAR*                                                     | *Annual*               |
| ***Land-system change***                | *Global forest cover, percentage of original forest*                                                        | *100%*                         | *75–54%, global weighted range*                                | *60%*                                         | *ESA CCI, MODIS, Global Forest Watch*                            | *Annual*               |
| ***Land-system change***                | *Tropical forest cover, percentage of original forest*                                                      | *100%*                         | *85–60%*                                                       | *Americas: 83.9%; Africa: 54.3%; Asia: 37.5%* | *FAO FRA, Global Forest Watch*                                   | *Annual*               |
| ***Land-system change***                | *Temperate forest cover, percentage of original forest*                                                     | *100%*                         | *50–30%*                                                       | *Americas: 51.2%; Europe: 34.2%; Asia: 37.9%* | *FAO FRA, Global Forest Watch*                                   | *Annual*               |
| ***Land-system change***                | *Boreal forest cover, percentage of original forest*                                                        | *100%*                         | *85–60%*                                                       | *Americas: 56.6%; Eurasia: 70.3%*             | *FAO FRA, Global Forest Watch*                                   | *Annual*               |
| ***Freshwater change — blue water***    | *Human-induced flow disturbance, 95th percentile deviation*                                                 | *9.4%*                         | *10.2–50%*                                                     | *18.2%*                                       | *WaterGAP, PCR-GLOBWB, GRDC*                                     | *Annual*               |
| ***Freshwater change — green water***   | *Soil-moisture deviation from Holocene variability, percentage of ice-free land*                            | *9.8%*                         | *11.1–50%*                                                     | *15.8%*                                       | *ESA CCI, GLEAM, ERA5*                                           | *Monthly*              |
| ***Atmospheric aerosol loading***       | *Interhemispheric difference in aerosol optical thickness, AOD*                                             | *0.03*                         | *0.1–0.25*                                                     | *0.076*                                       | *MODIS, MISR, Copernicus CAMS*                                   | *Monthly*              |
| ***Novel entities***                    | *Qualitative assessment of synthetic chemicals and novel entities released without adequate safety testing* | *0*                            | *Threshold not quantitatively defined*                         | *Exceeded*                                    | *UNEP, OECD, national chemical registries*                       | *Annual review*        |

*This table should be maintained as a versioned reference table.*

*Each future release should record:*

*• table version;  
• data year;  
• source update;  
• methodology note;  
• uncertainty note;  
• affected PHI domains;  
• affected MRV priorities;  
• affected boundary-pressure profiles;  
• change from prior release.*

*Where updated scientific assessments modify values, thresholds, or interpretations, Prometheus should update the table through the canonical release process.*

*The table should therefore be understood as:*

*global context for ecological intelligence;  
not automatic project-level proof.*

*A local Prometheus pilot may be inspired by planetary-boundary pressure, but its claims must still be grounded in site-level evidence, bioregional interpretation, MRV methodology, confidence scoring, and review.*

*For example:*

*• climate-boundary pressure may guide carbon, biomass, energy, and resilience indicators;  
• biosphere-integrity pressure may guide biodiversity, habitat, pollinator, and corridor indicators;  
• freshwater-boundary pressure may guide infiltration, retention, soil-moisture, irrigation, and watershed indicators;  
• biogeochemical-flow pressure may guide nutrient cycling, composting, fertilizer-dependency, and runoff indicators;  
• land-system-change pressure may guide agroforestry, soil cover, canopy structure, and regenerative land-use indicators;  
• novel-entities pressure may guide material safety, toxic-input reduction, chemical substitution, and contamination-risk review.*

*This is how Prometheus connects planetary reality to local evidence:*

*The planet gives the warning.*

*The bioregion gives the context.*

*The field gives the data.*

*MRV gives the verification.*

*PHI gives the synthesis.*

*PRU gives the value discipline.*

***K.3 Bioregional interpretation***

*Prometheus should interpret planetary-boundary logic through bioregional context.*

*A bioregion is not only a map.*

*It is a living pattern of climate, water, soil, biodiversity, culture, food systems, settlement, and ecological memory. Bioregional interpretation should consider:*

*• climate zone;  
• rainfall pattern;  
• drought risk;  
• flood risk;  
• watershed position;  
• soil type;  
• erosion risk;  
• salinity risk;  
• fire risk;  
• biodiversity corridors;  
• land-use history;  
• agricultural system;  
• cultural and community context;  
• food-system dependency;  
• infrastructure fragility;  
• governance capacity;  
• local knowledge.*

*This allows Prometheus to avoid universal metrics without context.*

*An indicator that matters in Sicily may not matter in the same way in Florida, the Andes, the Sahel, or a future off-world habitat. Prometheus therefore reads planetary boundaries through place. The principle is:*

*planetary science sets the horizon;  
bioregional reality sets the interpretation;  
local evidence sets the claim.*

*A sustained decline in PHI, even while absolute values remain above critical thresholds, may trigger a yellow protective review signal, prompting proactive governance, MRV, ecological, or operational review before negative trends become harder to reverse.*

***K.4 Planetary Health Index logic***

*Prometheus may use a Planetary Health Index, or PHI, as an analytical lens.*

*PHI should not be treated as a single sovereign number.*

*It should be treated as a composite decision-support framework.*

*PHI may help organize ecological and systemic indicators across domains such as:*

*• soil function;  
• water cycling;  
• biodiversity;  
• carbon dynamics;  
• productive resilience;  
• input dependency;  
• pollution reduction;  
• human nourishment;  
• community resilience;  
• governance maturity;  
• evidence quality;  
• continuity resilience.*

*A PHI-style score may support:*

*• baseline understanding;  
• pilot comparison;  
• bioregional diagnosis;  
• risk communication;  
• MRV prioritization;  
• PRU research;  
• RAP portfolio screening;  
• institutional diligence;  
• capital-readiness review.*

*PHI should not automatically:*

*• issue PRU value;  
• approve RAP inclusion;  
• trigger token issuance;  
• determine investor rights;  
• determine governance authority;  
• replace MRV;  
• replace scientific review;  
• replace local stewardship judgment.*

*PHI is a compass. It is not a court.*

***K.5 PHI domain structure***

*A Prometheus PHI framework may be organized into domains.*

| ***PHI domain***            | ***Example indicators***                                                                | ***Use***                              |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| ***Soil vitality***         | *Organic matter, soil carbon, aggregate stability, infiltration, microbial indicators.* | *Regenerative soil function.*          |
| ***Water resilience***      | *Infiltration, retention, irrigation reduction, runoff reduction, water quality.*       | *Hydrological stability.*              |
| ***Biodiversity function*** | *Species richness, habitat structure, pollinators, functional diversity.*               | *Ecological resilience.*               |
| ***Carbon and biomass***    | *Biomass return, perennial cover, carbon storage proxies, energy/input reduction.*      | *Climate-relevant performance.*        |
| ***Productive resilience*** | *Yield diversity, input dependency, seasonal stability, edible biomass.*                | *Food and economic viability.*         |
| ***Pollution reduction***   | *Chemical inputs, waste loops, nutrient leakage, material safety.*                      | *Novel-entity and contamination risk.* |
| ***Community resilience***  | *Local participation, stewardship continuity, food sovereignty, skills.*                | *Social-ecological durability.*        |
| ***Governance maturity***   | *Role clarity, decision records, review pathways, conflict handling.*                   | *Institutional resilience.*            |
| ***Evidence quality***      | *Provenance, confidence, sampling, independent review, admissibility.*                  | *Claim reliability.*                   |
| ***Continuity resilience*** | *Canonical records, offline archives, governance memory, recovery pathways.*            | *Long-term system survival.*           |

*Domains should be weighted according to methodology, site type, bioregion, pilot maturity, and intended use.*

*Weights should be documented.*

*Where weights are experimental, they should be labelled as research-use or pilot-use.*

***K.6 PHI evidence maturity***

*PHI should incorporate evidence maturity.*

*An indicator should not be scored with high confidence merely because it is important.*

*It should be scored according to evidence strength.*

*A PHI record may therefore include:*

| ***Evidence level***      | ***Meaning***                                                |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Narrative / claim***   | *Meaningful but not yet evidenced.*                          |
| ***Observation***         | *Recorded field note, image, measurement, or testimony.*     |
| ***Evidence***            | *Observation with provenance and method.*                    |
| ***Validated indicator*** | *Evidence passed quality checks.*                            |
| ***MRV-admissible***      | *Evidence accepted for reporting under scope.*               |
| ***PRU-eligible***        | *Evidence may contribute to PRU analysis under methodology.* |

*This prevents PHI from becoming decorative scoring.*

*A low-confidence indicator should remain low confidence even if it is emotionally or strategically important.*

*Prometheus should reward evidence maturity, not rhetorical intensity.*

***K.7 Composite scoring caution***

*Composite indices can be useful.*

*They can also hide uncertainty.*

*Prometheus should therefore treat PHI scoring with caution.*

*Composite PHI outputs should disclose:*

*• indicator list;  
• data source;  
• methodology version;  
• weighting logic;  
• confidence level;  
• missing data;  
• uncertainty;  
• reviewer status;  
• intended use;  
• limitations;  
• update date.*

*A PHI score should never be presented without its evidence profile.*

*The correct reporting format is not merely:*

*PHI = 82.*

*The correct reporting format is:*

*PHI = 82  
methodology version: vX  
confidence: moderate  
missing data: biodiversity lab proxy unavailable  
review status: internal review  
use: pilot comparison only.*

*The number becomes credible only when its boundary travels with it.*

***K.8 Boundary pressure profile***

*Prometheus may create a Boundary Pressure Profile for each project or bioregion.*

*This profile maps where a site may reduce or increase pressure in relation to planetary-boundary domains.*

*Possible profile categories:*

| ***Profile category***         | ***Meaning***                                                           |
|--------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Pressure-reducing***        | *Evidence suggests intervention reduces pressure in a boundary domain.* |
| ***Neutral / low relevance***  | *Limited relationship to the boundary domain.*                          |
| ***Pressure-uncertain***       | *Evidence is insufficient or ambiguous.*                                |
| ***Pressure-increasing risk*** | *Potential negative effect requires review or mitigation.*              |
| ***High concern***             | *Serious risk to ecological integrity or public claim reliability.*     |

*Boundary pressure profiles support:*

*• design review;  
• MRV prioritization;  
• risk disclosure;  
• intervention redesign;  
• PRU analysis;  
• RAP portfolio screening;  
• investor diligence;  
• public communication discipline.*

*They should not become automatic enforcement.*

*They are ecological intelligence for review.*

***K.9 MRV Review and Verification Network***

*This network may include:*

*• soil scientists;  
• ecologists;  
• hydrologists;  
• agroecologists;  
• biodiversity specialists;  
• remote-sensing analysts;  
• field auditors;  
• laboratories;  
• local stewards;  
• indigenous or traditional ecological knowledge holders where appropriate and consented;  
• bioregional experts;  
• methodology reviewers;  
• legal or reporting reviewers where necessary.*

*The network does not act as an oracle.*

*It reviews evidence.*

*It validates methods.*

*It identifies uncertainty.*

*It supports admissibility.*

*It improves interpretation.*

*It brings plural intelligence into disciplined review.*

*Prometheus does not need ecological prophecy.*

*It needs accountable ecological verification.*

***K.10 Integration with MRV***

*PHI and planetary-boundary logic should integrate with MRV through evidence classification.*

*Each PHI or boundary-related indicator should reference:*

*• data source;  
• methodology version;  
• evidence maturity;  
• confidence score;  
• reviewer status;  
• admissibility status;  
• reporting boundary;  
• update frequency.*

*MRV should determine whether PHI-relevant evidence is:*

*• claim;  
• observation;  
• evidence;  
• validated indicator;  
• research-use only;  
• MRV-admissible;  
• PRU-eligible;  
• non-admissible.*

*This keeps PHI from becoming a parallel authority.*

*PHI organizes ecological meaning.*

*MRV verifies ecological evidence.*

***K.11 Integration with PRU***

*PHI may inform PRU analysis only through approved methodology.*

*Possible PHI-to-PRU relationships include:*

| ***PHI role***            | ***PRU implication***                                         |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| ***Contextual baseline*** | *Helps describe initial ecological condition.*                |
| ***Indicator family***    | *Helps select relevant indicators.*                           |
| ***Risk screen***         | *Flags ecological domains requiring stronger evidence.*       |
| ***Confidence input***    | *May support confidence adjustment where methodology allows.* |
| ***Performance signal***  | *May contribute to PRU analysis if MRV-admissible.*           |
| ***Portfolio screen***    | *May help compare PRU-relevant units for RAP review.*         |

*PHI should not directly create PRU value.*

*PHI may support PRU only when underlying indicators are evidence-backed, methodology-aligned, and admissible.*

*The PRU rule remains:*

*verified regenerative performance first;  
value-analysis interface second.*

***K.12 Integration with RAP***

*RAP logic may use PHI and planetary-boundary profiles for portfolio-level assessment.*

*Possible uses include:*

*• bioregional diversification;  
• ecological risk mapping;  
• boundary-pressure diversification;  
• climate and water-risk screening;  
• biodiversity exposure;  
• evidence-quality comparison;  
• governance maturity screening;  
• resilience weighting;  
• portfolio stress testing.*

*RAP should not aggregate weak claims into strong portfolio language.*

*A portfolio of weak evidence does not become strong evidence by being grouped.*

*RAP maturity depends on the maturity of underlying units.*

*PHI may improve portfolio intelligence.*

*It may not launder uncertainty.*

***K.13 Integration with Ravel risk-signaling***

*PHI and boundary-pressure profiles may support the Ravel Risk-Signaling Model.*

*They may provide risk signals such as:*

*• ecological stress increasing;  
• water vulnerability rising;  
• biodiversity evidence weak;  
• soil indicators improving slowly;  
• climate risk exposure high;  
• public claims exceeding boundary evidence;  
• portfolio concentrated in high-stress bioregions;  
• governance underprepared for ecological complexity.*

*Ravel may use these signals for review routing.*

*Ravel should not use PHI as automatic enforcement.*

*The correct relationship is:*

*PHI informs ecological risk;  
Ravel routes systemic risk;  
governance reviews;  
MRV verifies;  
legal structures consequences where needed.*

***K.14 Planetary Boundaries and capital readiness***

*Planetary-boundary logic may strengthen capital readiness when used correctly.*

*Capital partners increasingly need to understand ecological risk, transition risk, climate resilience, natural capital dependency, physical risk, and environmental claims reliability.*

*Prometheus may use boundary-aware reporting to show:*

*• how a site reduces ecological pressure;  
• where risks remain;  
• which indicators are verified;  
• which indicators are emerging;  
• where further investment improves resilience;  
• how regenerative intervention affects long-term productive capacity;  
• how PRU-relevant performance may become more robust over time.*

*This makes capital more intelligent.*

*It also makes claims more disciplined.*

*A capital-facing boundary report should distinguish:*

*• verified indicators;  
• preliminary signals;  
• risk assumptions;  
• methodology limitations;  
• scenario analysis;  
• non-admissible claims;  
• future research pathways.*

*Planetary language should increase diligence, not inflate valuation.*

***K.15 Local knowledge and cultural context***

*Planetary-boundary analysis should not erase local knowledge.*

*Local communities, farmers, stewards, indigenous knowledge holders, elders, field workers, and bioregional practitioners may understand patterns invisible to remote systems.*

*Prometheus should include local knowledge where:*

*• consent is obtained;  
• cultural context is respected;  
• attribution is handled properly;  
• data sovereignty is considered;  
• extractive knowledge capture is avoided;  
• evidence classification remains clear.*

*Local knowledge may guide:*

*• indicator selection;  
• seasonal interpretation;  
• water behavior;  
• species presence;  
• land-use history;  
• restoration design;  
• risk interpretation;  
• stewardship practice.*

*Local knowledge is not automatically MRV-admissible.*

*It is also not inferior.*

*It becomes part of the evidence pathway when structured, consented, contextualized, and reviewed.*

***K.16 Network-independent planetary continuity***

*Planetary-boundary and PHI knowledge should be preserved in network-independent forms.*

*A Prometheus continuity archive may include:*

*• local ecological baselines;  
• bioregional maps;  
• seed and species lists;  
• soil and water records;  
• climate-risk notes;  
• agroecological designs;  
• syntropic succession plans;  
• restoration protocols;  
• MRV methods;  
• PHI domain definitions;  
• planetary-boundary educational materials;  
• local-language stewardship manuals;  
• printed field protocols;  
• offline datasets;  
• institutional archive copies.*

*If the network fails, ecological memory must remain.*

*A community should be able to continue stewardship with:*

*paper maps;  
field notebooks;  
seed archives;  
soil knowledge;  
water knowledge;  
local governance memory;  
offline MRV forms;  
and a verified canonical package.*

*Prometheus survives when ecological intelligence can still be practiced without a dashboard.*

***K.17 Suggested PHI record schema***

*A simplified PHI record may include:*

*{*

*"phi_record_id": "phi_record_0001",*

*"site_id": "ohe_or_project_reference",*

*"bioregion": "bioregional_reference",*

*"methodology_version": "phi_method_v1.0",*

*"assessment_date": "2026-01-01",*

*"domains": \[*

*{*

*"domain": "soil_vitality",*

*"indicator": "soil_organic_matter",*

*"value": 3.2,*

*"unit": "%",*

*"evidence_status": "evidence",*

*"confidence": 0.76,*

*"review_status": "internal_review",*

*"data_reference": "mrv_record_hash"*

*}*

*\],*

*"boundary_pressure_profile": {*

*"climate_change": "pressure_reducing",*

*"biosphere_integrity": "pressure_uncertain",*

*"freshwater_change": "pressure_reducing"*

*},*

*"limitations": \[*

*"Biodiversity survey incomplete"*

*\],*

*"permitted_use": "pilot_comparison_only",*

*"pru_relevance": "not_yet_pru_eligible"*

*}*

*This schema is illustrative.*

*The operative schema should be versioned, tested, and aligned with the MRV methodology.*

***K.18 Final planetary-boundary principle***

*Prometheus uses planetary-boundary logic to remember scale.*

*It uses bioregional interpretation to remember place.*

*It uses PHI to organize ecological intelligence.*

*It uses MRV to verify evidence.*

*It uses PRU to translate verified regeneration into value logic.*

*It uses RAP to aggregate only what is mature enough to be aggregated.*

*It uses governance to keep these layers distinct.*

*The final principle is:*

*the planet gives context;  
the bioregion gives meaning;  
the field gives evidence;  
MRV gives verification;  
PRU gives value discipline;  
capital follows what life has made real.*

*Prometheus is planetary in awareness, bioregional in interpretation, local in evidence, and constitutional in fidelity.*

# ***Appendix L: Integration Zome API Documentation***

***L.1 Overview***

*The Integration Zome is the controlled interface layer through which external systems, dashboards, applications, data services, institutional tools, MRV pipelines, and partner systems may interact with the h•eart•h Prometheus ecosystem.*

*Its purpose is not to create unrestricted external access.*

*Its purpose is to provide structured, authenticated, review-bound, and constitutionally constrained integration pathways for data ingestion, evidence retrieval, query execution, operational reporting, governance interaction, and future interoperability.*

*The Integration Zome should preserve the following principles:*

*• source-layer fidelity;*

*• agent sovereignty;*

*• privacy and purpose limitation;*

*• syntropic sovereignty header validation;*

*• MRV provenance;*

*• no-double-counting discipline;*

*• public-claim boundaries;*

*• token-boundary separation;*

*• legal and jurisdictional review where relevant;*

*• Holochain-compatible agent-centric operation;*

*• review before protected-domain action.*

*The Integration Zome is designed to be protocol-flexible.*

*It may support REST, GraphQL, gRPC, webhooks, signed agent calls, internal bridge calls, and future integration methods where appropriate.*

*However, protocol flexibility does not mean authority flexibility.*

*All external requests remain subject to defined authentication, authorization, validation, rate limiting, logging, constitutional constraints, and protected-domain review.*

*The Integration Zome should be understood as a gateway to reviewable interoperability, not as a gateway to automatic rights, token issuance, financial execution, certification, PRU value creation, or legal status.*

*The integration architecture may support the following API paradigms:*

| ***Protocol***           | ***Primary use case***                                                                                                  | ***Advantages***                                                                                    | ***Boundary***                                                                                            |
|--------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| *REST*                   | *Simple data retrieval, public reports, webhook integrations, legacy-system connectivity, operational status endpoints* | *Universal compatibility, stateless operation, cacheable responses, broad developer familiarity*    | *Suitable for low-risk and clearly bounded access; not sufficient by itself for protected-domain actions* |
| *GraphQL*                | *Complex queries, dashboards, filtered reporting, internal tools, visualization interfaces*                             | *Efficient data fetching, typed schemas, flexible aggregation, subscription support*                | *Query scope must respect permissions, privacy, disclosure classification, and public-claim boundaries*   |
| *gRPC*                   | *High-throughput data exchange, inter-service communication, sensor ingestion, internal bridge communication*           | *Streaming support, strong typing, high performance, suitable for infrastructure-level integration* | *Best used for authenticated infrastructure services and not as a public authority layer*                 |
| *Signed Holochain calls* | *Agent-centric operations, source-chain actions, DHT-published records, governance-relevant events*                     | *Cryptographic identity, agent sovereignty, contextual validation, source-chain integrity*          | *Required for actions that affect agent records, governance records, MRV evidence, or protected status*   |
| *Webhooks*               | *Asynchronous notifications for external systems, dashboards, partners, auditors, and operational monitors*             | *Event-driven integration, near-real-time updates, system interoperability*                         | *Webhooks notify; they do not authorize protected-domain action by themselves*                            |

*All API and integration pathways should validate syntropic sovereignty header requirements where applicable.*

*The syntropic sovereignty header ensures that requests, records, responses, and data packets remain connected to the constitutional principles of Prometheus, including non-commodification of life, agent sovereignty, source-layer constraints, evidence discipline, and the spiral of abundance.*

***L.2 Authentication and Authorization***

*All non-public API requests should be authenticated and authorized.*

*Authentication identifies the caller.*

*Authorization determines what the caller is permitted to do.*

*Prometheus should treat authentication and authorization as separate controls.*

*A valid identity does not automatically create permission.*

*A valid API key does not automatically create governance authority.*

*A valid partner credential does not automatically create public-claim authority.*

*A valid investor-data-room credential does not automatically create investor entitlement.*

*A valid system token does not automatically authorize protected-domain actions.*

***L.2.1 Authentication Methods***

*The Integration Zome may support multiple authentication methods, each appropriate for different risk levels and use cases.*

| ***Method***                    | ***Description***                                                                                    | ***Appropriate use case***                                                                                         | ***Boundary***                                                                                                     |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| *API key*                       | *Simple token-based authentication for low-risk access*                                              | *Public reports, dashboard queries, non-sensitive aggregated data, system health endpoints*                        | *Should not authorize protected-domain actions, private data access, governance decisions, or financial workflows* |
| *JWT*                           | *Time-limited signed token containing agent identity, session context, role claims, and permissions* | *User-authenticated requests, partner portals, dashboard access, limited governance interaction*                   | *Role claims must be verified against canonical governance or access records*                                      |
| *Holochain agent signature*     | *Cryptographic signature using an agent key, verified through Holochain-compatible identity logic*   | *Agent-owned records, source-chain actions, MRV submissions, governance-relevant events, high-security operations* | *Preferred for agent-centric actions and protected record creation*                                                |
| *OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect*    | *Delegated authentication through trusted identity providers*                                        | *Enterprise SSO, institutional partners, public-sector systems, research platforms*                                | *External identity does not replace Prometheus authorization rules*                                                |
| *mTLS / service credentials*    | *Mutual authentication between infrastructure services*                                              | *Bridge services, internal infrastructure, sensor gateways, secure backend services*                               | *Should be restricted to known infrastructure contexts and monitored carefully*                                    |
| *Multi-signature authorization* | *Multiple authorized signatures required for high-risk actions*                                      | *Canonical releases, protected-domain changes, emergency procedures, registry actions*                             | *Should be used for constitutional, registry, release, or high-impact governance operations*                       |

***L.2.2 Authorization Levels***

*Authorization levels should be defined according to action scope, risk, data sensitivity, and protected-domain relevance.*

| ***Level***                        | ***Permitted scope***                                                                                              | ***Required authentication***                                             | ***Boundary***                                                                                                        |
|------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| *Public*                           | *Read-only access to approved public, non-sensitive, aggregated, or already published information*                 | *None or API key*                                                         | *Public access must not expose private data, protected records, non-public methodology, or investor-review materials* |
| *Authenticated agent*              | *Read/write access to the agent’s own permitted data and scoped participation records*                             | *JWT or Holochain agent signature*                                        | *Agent access does not authorize changes to other agents, governance outcomes, MRV admissibility, or public claims*   |
| *Project operator*                 | *Operational records, site data, evidence uploads, task updates, internal reporting within assigned project scope* | *JWT plus role verification, or Holochain signature*                      | *Operator access remains project-bounded and review-bound*                                                            |
| *MRV submitter*                    | *Submission of sensor, laboratory, field, satellite, audit, or operational evidence*                               | *Holochain signature, project credential, or approved service credential* | *Submission is not verification; evidence remains under review until validated*                                       |
| *MRV reviewer / validator*         | *Review, validation, anomaly flagging, confidence scoring, and evidence-quality assessment*                        | *Holochain signature plus role authorization*                             | *Review may inform admissibility but does not by itself create financial rights*                                      |
| *Circle delegate*                  | *Governance proposals, circle records, consent records, review participation, delegated communications*            | *JWT or Holochain signature plus governance role verification*            | *Delegate authority is limited by circle scope and source-layer constraints*                                          |
| *Protocol steward*                 | *Methodology review, release alignment, protected terminology, registry status review, compatibility assessment*   | *Holochain signature plus steward authorization*                          | *Stewardship supports coherence; it does not create unilateral sovereign authority*                                   |
| *Canonical registry authority*     | *Registry entries, release records, fork classifications, compatibility status, public verification metadata*      | *Multi-signature authorization*                                           | *Registry changes require strong controls, logs, and release governance*                                              |
| *Institutional / data-room user*   | *Controlled access to investor-review, partner-review, audit, research, or diligence materials*                    | *Contractual access credential plus identity verification*                | *Data-room access does not create investment entitlement, endorsement, or offering status*                            |
| *Emergency / quarantine authority* | *Temporary suspension, quarantine, compromised-key response, fake-release notice, or critical incident response*   | *Multi-signature and documented emergency procedure*                      | *Emergency actions require post-action review and audit trail*                                                        |

***L.2.3 API Key Management***

*API keys should be issued only through authorized governance, operations, or registry procedures.*

*API keys may be scoped by:*

*• endpoint;*

*• project;*

*• role;*

*• organization;*

*• IP range;*

*• rate-limit tier;*

*• expiration date;*

*• data classification;*

*• permitted environment;*

*• read/write boundary.*

*API keys should be hashed before storage. Plaintext keys should be displayed only once at creation.*

*Keys should be rotated periodically.*

*Keys should be revoked immediately if compromised, unused, misused, transferred without authorization, or associated with misleading public claims. API key possession should not be treated as constitutional authority.*

***L.3 REST API Endpoints***

*The REST API may support simple data retrieval, operational submissions, dashboard access, health checks, and integration with legacy systems.*

*The following endpoint structure is conceptual and should be treated as implementation guidance, not as final public API commitment until the canonical technical release confirms base URLs, authentication, schemas, and deployment environments.*

*Base URL: <https://api.heart-intelligence.earth/v1>*

*If this endpoint is not yet live, it should be treated as a planned or reserved endpoint rather than an operational production service.*

*All endpoints should return JSON responses unless otherwise specified.*

*Requests should include the required authentication headers.*

*Protected-domain actions should be routed to review and should not execute automatically merely because an API request is syntactically valid.*

***L.3.1 Agent Endpoints***

| ***Endpoint***                   | ***Method*** | ***Description***                                                                    | ***Authentication***               | ***Rate limit*** | ***Boundary***                                                              |
|----------------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| */agent/register*                | *POST*       | *Register a new agent profile or initiate agent identity creation*                   | *API key or invitation credential* | *10/hour*        | *Registration does not create governance authority or PRU eligibility*      |
| */agent/{id}*                    | *GET*        | *Retrieve permitted agent profile information*                                       | *JWT or Holochain signature*       | *100/hour*       | *Access limited by privacy and role permissions*                            |
| */agent/{id}/status*             | *GET*        | *Retrieve agent operational status within permitted scope*                           | *JWT or Holochain signature*       | *100/hour*       | *Status is contextual and not human ranking*                                |
| */agent/{id}/reputation-context* | *GET*        | *Retrieve permitted reputation context*                                              | *JWT plus role authorization*      | *100/hour*       | *Reputation is an integrity signal, not human worth or automatic authority* |
| */agent/{id}/coherence*          | *GET*        | *Retrieve voluntary, aggregated, privacy-preserving coherence context where enabled* | *JWT plus explicit consent*        | *100/hour*       | *HSI-related data remains voluntary, private, and non-financial*            |

***L.3.2 MRV and Evidence Endpoints***

| ***Endpoint***            | ***Method*** | ***Description***                                                                                         | ***Authentication***                                           | ***Rate limit*** | ***Boundary***                                             |
|---------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| */mrv/submit*             | *POST*       | *Submit MRV-related evidence, including sensor, field, laboratory, satellite, audit, or operational data* | *JWT plus Holochain signature or approved service credential*  | *1000/day*       | *Submission does not create verification or admissibility* |
| */mrv/{id}*               | *GET*        | *Retrieve permitted MRV event details*                                                                    | *API key, JWT, or role credential depending on classification* | *1000/hour*      | *Access depends on disclosure status and data sensitivity* |
| */mrv/project/{id}*       | *GET*        | *Retrieve permitted MRV data for a project*                                                               | *API key, JWT, or data-room credential*                        | *1000/hour*      | *Investor-review data requires controlled access*          |
| */mrv/verify/{id}*        | *POST*       | *Request or perform review of a pending MRV event*                                                        | *MRV reviewer / validator authorization*                       | *100/hour*       | *Verification remains methodology-bound and review-bound*  |
| */mrv/{id}/admissibility* | *POST*       | *Route evidence bundle to admissibility review*                                                           | *MRV reviewer, protocol steward, or governance role*           | *50/hour*        | *Admissibility does not create legal rights by itself*     |
| */mrv/{id}/correct*       | *POST*       | *Submit correction, challenge, or updated evidence*                                                       | *Authorized agent or reviewer*                                 | *100/hour*       | *Corrections require provenance and audit trail*           |

***L.3.3 PRU and Value-Analysis Endpoints***

| ***Endpoint***               | ***Method*** | ***Description***                                            | ***Authentication***                                  | ***Rate limit*** | ***Boundary***                                                               |
|------------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| */pru/project/{id}/analysis* | *GET*        | *Retrieve permitted PRU value-analysis status for a project* | *Authorized agent, reviewer, or data-room credential* | *100/hour*       | *PRU analysis is not market price, security rating, or investor entitlement* |
| */pru/project/{id}/maturity* | *GET*        | *Retrieve PRU maturity-classification status*                | *Authorized agent, reviewer, or data-room credential* | *100/hour*       | *Maturity class supports diligence and does not replace underwriting*        |
| */pru/project/{id}/review*   | *POST*       | *Submit PRU-related evidence bundle for review*              | *MRV reviewer, protocol steward, or governance role*  | *50/hour*        | *Review may result in admissible, non-admissible, or review-required status* |
| */pru/project/{id}/report*   | *GET*        | *Retrieve permitted PRU analysis report*                     | *Data-room credential or authorized role*             | *50/hour*        | *Report classification determines permitted language and use*                |

***L.3.4 Governance Endpoints***

| ***Endpoint***              | ***Method*** | ***Description***                                               | ***Authentication***                                    | ***Rate limit*** | ***Boundary***                                                     |
|-----------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------|
| */governance/circles*       | *GET*        | *List permitted governance circles*                             | *API key or JWT depending on visibility*                | *100/hour*       | *Visibility depends on governance disclosure rules*                |
| */governance/proposals*     | *GET*        | *List active proposals within permitted scope*                  | *API key or JWT*                                        | *100/hour*       | *Proposal visibility may be public, circle-limited, or restricted* |
| */governance/proposal/{id}* | *GET*        | *Retrieve proposal details*                                     | *API key or JWT depending on visibility*                | *100/hour*       | *Protected-domain proposals may require restricted access*         |
| */governance/proposal*      | *POST*       | *Submit new proposal*                                           | *JWT plus role verification or Holochain signature*     | *10/hour*        | *Submission does not guarantee adoption*                           |
| */governance/consent*       | *POST*       | *Record consent, stand-aside, objection, or review position*    | *Holochain signature or verified governance credential* | *100/hour*       | *Consent process remains subject to circle rules*                  |
| */governance/review-route*  | *POST*       | *Route protected-domain question to appropriate review pathway* | *Authorized governance role*                            | *50/hour*        | *Routing is decision support, not final authority*                 |

***L.3.5 Integrity, Warrant, and Quarantine Endpoints***

|-----------------------------------|--------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| */integrity/warrants*             | *GET*        | *List warrants or adverse integrity records within permitted scope*    | *API key, JWT, or governance credential depending on visibility* | *100/hour*       | *Visibility must respect due process and data sensitivity*   |
| */integrity/warrants*             | *POST*       | *Issue a warrant or adverse integrity record*                          | *Authorized reviewer, validator, or governance role*             | *10/hour*        | *Issuance triggers review; it is not guilt by declaration*   |
| */integrity/warrants/{id}/appeal* | *POST*       | *Appeal a warrant or adverse determination*                            | *Affected agent or authorized representative*                    | *5/hour*         | *Appeal pathway must be recorded*                            |
| */integrity/quarantine/{id}*      | *POST*       | *Request quarantine of a record, claim, fork, release, or integration* | *Protected-domain authority or emergency role*                   | *10/hour*        | *Quarantine requires scope, rationale, duration, and review* |
| */integrity/corrections*          | *POST*       | *Submit correction notice or misleading-use notice*                    | *Authorized role*                                                | *20/hour*        | *Public correction requires claim-control review*            |

***L.3.6 Registry and Release Endpoints***

|---------------------------|--------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| */registry/releases*      | *GET*        | *Retrieve canonical release records*                                                | *Public or API key*                             | *1000/hour*      | *Registry visibility does not authorize modification* |
| */registry/releases/{id}* | *GET*        | *Retrieve release manifest, hash references, signatures, and verification metadata* | *Public or API key*                             | *1000/hour*      | *Public verification endpoint*                        |
| */registry/forks/{id}*    | *GET*        | *Retrieve fork status or compatibility classification*                              | *Public or API key*                             | *1000/hour*      | *Fork status must be registry-defined*                |
| */registry/release*       | *POST*       | *Submit or update canonical release metadata*                                       | *Multi-signature registry authority*            | *Restricted*     | *Protected action*                                    |
| */registry/fork-status*   | *POST*       | *Submit fork classification or compatibility update*                                | *Registry authority or protocol steward review* | *Restricted*     | *Requires review and audit trail*                     |

|-----------------------------|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|------------------|----------------------------------------------|

| */system/metrics*           | *GET*        | *System performance metrics*                                 | *API key or admin credential* | *100/hour*       | *Public or internal classification required* |
| */system/status/{agent_id}* | *GET*        | *Retrieve scoped system status for an agent or service*      | *JWT or service credential*   | *100/hour*       | *Status data remains scope-limited*          |

***L.4 GraphQL API***

*The GraphQL API provides a flexible interface for complex queries, dashboards, institutional reports, and real-time subscriptions.*

*It should be used carefully because flexible query systems can expose more information than intended if authorization boundaries are weak.*

*GraphQL access should therefore enforce:*

*• field-level authorization;*

*• query-depth limits;*

*• rate limits;*

*• data classification rules;*

*• privacy filters;*

*• public-claim boundaries;*

*• investor-review classification;*

*• project-level access control;*

*• audit logging.*

***L.4.1 GraphQL Endpoint***

[*https://api.heart-intelligence.earth/graphql*](https://api.heart-intelligence.earth/graphql)

*If this endpoint is not yet live, it should be treated as a planned or reserved endpoint pending canonical deployment.*

***L.4.2 Example Queries***

*The following examples illustrate possible query structures.*

*They are conceptual examples and should not be treated as final public schema commitments until confirmed in the technical documentation package.*

*Example: agent profile with reputation context and recent contributions.*

*graphql  
query GetAgentProfile(\$id: ID!) {  
agent(id: \$id) {  
id  
profile {  
name  
bio  
joinedAt  
}  
reputationContext {  
overall  
components {  
contribution  
reliability  
governanceParticipation  
validationAccuracy  
}  
boundary  
}  
recentContributions(limit: 5) {  
type  
description  
timestamp  
evidenceStatus  
}  
}  
}*

*Example: bioregional dashboard with aggregated metrics.*

*graphql  
query BioregionalDashboard(\$bioregionId: ID!, \$timeRange: TimeRange!) {  
bioregion(id: \$bioregionId) {  
name  
boundaries {  
climate  
water  
biodiversity  
soil  
}  
metrics(timeRange: \$timeRange) {  
phi  
mrvConfidence  
waterInfiltration  
biodiversityIndex  
soilFunction  
regenerativeDelta  
}  
projects {  
id  
name  
status  
stewardCount  
pruMaturityClass  
reviewStatus  
}  
}  
}*

*Example: PRU analysis status.*

*graphql  
query ProjectPruAnalysis(\$projectId: ID!) {  
project(id: \$projectId) {  
id  
name  
pruAnalysis {  
maturityClass  
evidenceStatus  
admissibilityStatus  
confidenceScore  
methodologyVersion  
legalBoundary  
}  
}  
}*

***L.4.3 Subscriptions***

*Subscriptions may support real-time or near-real-time updates for dashboards, operational systems, review workflows, and governance tools.*

*Subscriptions should not be used to imply automatic action.*

*A subscription notifies.*

*It does not authorize.*

*Example: subscribe to new MRV events for a project.*

*graphql  
subscription OnNewMrvEvent(\$projectId: ID!) {  
mrvEventCreated(projectId: \$projectId) {  
id  
type  
value  
confidence  
timestamp  
source {  
type  
id  
}  
reviewStatus  
}  
}*

*Example: subscribe to governance proposal updates.*

*graphql  
subscription OnProposalUpdate(\$circleId: ID!) {  
proposalUpdated(circleId: \$circleId) {  
id  
title  
status  
consentSummary {  
consent  
standAside  
objection  
}  
deadline  
}  
}*

***L.5 gRPC API***

*The gRPC API is optimized for high-throughput, low-latency communication, especially for sensor data ingestion, inter-service communication, internal bridge services, and infrastructure-level data exchange.*

*gRPC should be treated primarily as an infrastructure interface.*

*Public or partner-facing access should be strictly authenticated, authorized, rate-limited, logged, and scoped.*

***L.5.1 Protocol Buffer Definition — Conceptual Excerpt***

*protobuf  
syntax = "proto3";  
package heart_intelligence.v1;*

*service MrvService {  
// Submit a batch of sensor or field readings.  
rpc SubmitEvidence(stream EvidenceReading) returns (SubmissionReceipt);*

*// Get real-time evidence stream.  
rpc StreamEvidence(EvidenceFilter) returns (stream EvidenceReading);*

*// Request MRV review.  
rpc RequestMrvReview(MrvReviewRequest) returns (MrvReviewResponse);  
}*

*service PruAnalysisService {  
// Retrieve PRU analysis status.  
rpc GetPruAnalysis(PruAnalysisRequest) returns (PruAnalysisResponse);*

*// Retrieve PRU maturity classification.  
rpc GetPruMaturity(PruMaturityRequest) returns (PruMaturityResponse);*

*// Request PRU review.  
rpc RequestPruReview(PruReviewRequest) returns (PruReviewResponse);  
}*

*service GovernanceService {  
// Submit governance proposal.  
rpc SubmitProposal(Proposal) returns (ProposalReceipt);*

*// Record consent response.  
rpc RecordConsent(ConsentRecord) returns (ConsentReceipt);*

*// Stream proposal updates.  
rpc StreamProposals(ProposalFilter) returns (stream Proposal);  
}*

*service IntegrityService {  
// Submit adverse integrity record.  
rpc SubmitIntegrityRecord(IntegrityRecord) returns (IntegrityReceipt);*

*// Request quarantine review.  
rpc RequestQuarantine(QuarantineRequest) returns (QuarantineResponse);*

*// Stream protected-domain alerts.  
rpc StreamIntegrityAlerts(AlertFilter) returns (stream IntegrityAlert);  
}*

*service RegistryService {  
// Get canonical release status.  
rpc GetReleaseStatus(ReleaseRequest) returns (ReleaseStatus);*

*// Get fork or compatibility status.  
rpc GetForkStatus(ForkStatusRequest) returns (ForkStatusResponse);  
}*

***L.5.2 gRPC Gateway***

*For systems that cannot use gRPC directly, a gRPC gateway may provide RESTful JSON translation.*

*Conceptual gateway path:*

[*https://api.heart-intelligence.earth/grpc/v1/{service}/{method}*](https://api.heart-intelligence.earth/grpc/v1/%7Bservice%7D/%7Bmethod%7D)

*If this endpoint is not yet live, it should be treated as planned or reserved until confirmed by canonical technical documentation.*

***L.6 Rate Limiting***

*The Integration Zome should implement multi-tier rate limiting to ensure fair usage, system stability, abuse resistance, and infrastructure protection.*

*Rate limits should be adjustable by governance and operations policy.*

*Rate-limit tiers should not create governance status, investor status, PRU eligibility, or certification status.*

| ***Tier***      | ***Indicative rate limit***        | ***Burst***                        | ***Indicative access basis***                                               | ***Boundary***                                   |
|-----------------|------------------------------------|------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| *Public*        | *100 requests/hour*                | *50*                               | *Public access or optional API key*                                         | *Public, non-sensitive, read-only access*        |
| *Community*     | *1,000 requests/hour*              | *200*                              | *Community role, project role, or approved API key*                         | *Does not imply governance authority*            |
| *Bioregion*     | *10,000 requests/hour*             | *1,000*                            | *Bioregional role, institutional partner, or approved integration*          | *Must remain scoped to agreement and role*       |
| *Institutional* | *100,000 requests/hour*            | *5,000*                            | *Partnership agreement, data-room agreement, or infrastructure integration* | *Requires contractual and technical controls*    |
| *Internal*      | *Defined by infrastructure policy* | *Defined by infrastructure policy* | *Core infrastructure, bridge, registry, or operations service*              | *Requires service authentication and monitoring* |

*Rate limits should be enforced per API key, agent ID, service account, IP range, organization, or integration profile.*

*Responses may include headers such as:*

*text  
X-RateLimit-Limit: 1000  
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 843  
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1625097600*

***L.7 External System Connectors***

*The Integration Zome may provide connectors for external systems where integration supports evidence quality, MRV review, operational continuity, institutional reporting, or legal structuring.*

*Connectors should not be treated as automatic truth sources.*

*External data must be evaluated for provenance, license conditions, methodology compatibility, reliability, update frequency, data gaps, and jurisdictional constraints.*

***L.7.1 Environmental Registry and Ecological-Claim Connectors***

*Environmental registry and ecological-claim connectors may support future comparison, reconciliation, or no-double-counting review.*

*They should not imply automatic credit issuance, registry approval, offset eligibility, or market recognition.*

| ***Registry / system type***                       | ***Protocol***                                      | ***Data types***                                                          | ***Verification boundary***                                                  |
|----------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| *Verra / VCS or equivalent registry*               | *REST or registry-specific API where available*     | *Project records, credit status, retirement data, methodology references* | *Cross-reference only; no automatic credit issuance*                         |
| *Gold Standard or equivalent registry*             | *REST or registry-specific API where available*     | *Project records, SDG-related data, credit status*                        | *External registry status must remain separate from Prometheus PRU analysis* |
| *Plan Vivo or equivalent community-based standard* | *REST, manual review, or registry-specific pathway* | *Community-based project data, certificates, methodology references*      | *Requires community consent and registry-specific verification*              |
| *Climate Action Reserve or equivalent registry*    | *REST or registry-specific API where available*     | *Project records, serial numbers, methodology references*                 | *Used for reconciliation and anti-double-counting where relevant*            |
| *Public ecological datasets*                       | *API, file import, or manual ingestion*             | *Land cover, protected areas, climate, biodiversity, hydrology*           | *Context layer; not automatic certification*                                 |

***L.7.2 Satellite and Geospatial Data Connectors***

| ***Provider***                                 | ***Protocol***                 | ***Data types***                                                             | ***Update frequency***        | ***Boundary***                                       |
|------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| *Sentinel Hub*                                 | *OGC WMS/WCS, REST*            | *Multispectral, radar, vegetation indices, derived layers*                   | *Daily or provider-dependent* | *Requires resolution and cloud-cover assessment*     |
| *Google Earth Engine*                          | *REST API or client libraries* | *Catalog access, custom computations, time-series analysis*                  | *On-demand*                   | *Methodology and reproducibility must be documented* |
| *NASA EarthData*                               | *REST API, OPeNDAP*            | *MODIS, Landsat, SRTM, climate and Earth observation products*               | *Daily to monthly*            | *Dataset suitability varies by scale and indicator*  |
| *Copernicus data services*                     | *OData, REST, or provider API* | *Sentinel imagery and derived products*                                      | *Daily or provider-dependent* | *Must document product version and preprocessing*    |
| *Esri Living Atlas or equivalent GIS services* | *REST API*                     | *Land cover, environmental, climate, demographic, and infrastructure layers* | *Varies*                      | *Context layer; not a substitute for site MRV*       |

***L.7.3 IoT Platform Connectors***

| ***Platform***                                     | ***Protocol***                     | ***Data types***                                           | ***Authentication***                        | ***Boundary***                                                   |
